The documents herein define the process for managing an Instance of the Token SkyLink Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-808f-9da5-d0cdb6cc50af
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

####### A.2.3.8.1.1.2.1 - Token SkyLink Management Settlement Cycle

During each settlement cycle, the Prime Agent Reimbursement Module tallies the total token bridging volume on the new chain. The Prime Agent Reimbursement Module calculates the reimbursement due for bridge development costs as a percent of the token bridging volume, in addition to normal Accessibility Rewards if applicable. Core GovOps executes a payment to the Prime Agent for the amount due for reimbursement of bridge development costs, to the extent that such costs have not already been fully reimbursed. Core GovOps updates the Powerhouse system to reflect the amount of bridge development costs that have been reimbursed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-809c-ac4a-e6af9f79422d
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

##### A.2.3.8.1.2 - Token SkyLink Input Requirements

The documents herein define the required inputs for a valid Token SkyLink Primitive. If any input is noncompliant or omitted, the Primitive will be invalidated and the SkyLink deployment will not move forward.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8046-ba7b-e57406b40621
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.8.1.2.1 - Token SkyLink Activation Status

The Token SkyLink Primitive must be Globally Activated.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-808d-b275-fd272a665592
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.8.1.2.2 - List of Active Token SkyLink Deployments

The Token SkyLink Primitive must list each active Token SkyLink deployment. The listing must include the following information: \(1\), the token being bridged, \(2\) the target chain of the bridge, \(3\) the address of the bridge contract on the Ethereum Mainnet, \(4\) the address of the bridge contract on the target chain, \(5\) the audit of the bridge contract, \(6\) the bridge parameters, \(7\) the total bridge deployment and audit costs, and \(8\) the amount of bridge deployment and audit costs that have been reimbursed to date.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8070-9bef-e7a0e78f0454
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

### A.2.3.9 - Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives

Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives are Sky Primitives that target demand generation for USDS. Prime Agents can create and control multiple instances of these Primitives which exist as technical infrastructure incentivizing the adoption or usage of USDS by end users or third parties.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 1b2f2ff0-8d73-8014-91cd-e129bd70eafd
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

#### A.2.3.9.1 - Accessibility Reward Primitive

The documents herein govern the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b4-873c-ec532edc750c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

##### A.2.3.9.1.1 - Introduction

The documents herein provide an introduction to the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-805d-89c0-d1e906f1b0dc
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.9.1.1.1 - Purpose

The purpose of the Accessibility Reward is to incentivize Prime Agents and third parties to drive USDS adoption by providing a financial reward to these actors for all USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8083-90b3-e9b92abb01b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.3.9.1.1.2 - Allowed Number Of Instances

Multiple instances of the Accessibility Reward Primitive are allowed. Each instance corresponds to an Accessibility Reward program with an associated Accessibility Reward Code.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80a0-9481-ed2a184029aa
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.9.1.1.3 - Multi-Instance Coordinator Document

An Agent Artifact that has more than one active instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive is not required to have a `Multi-Instance Coordinator Document`, since each Instance can be managed independently.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80f4-a2c6-e7baca51cf44
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

##### A.2.3.9.1.2 - Global Specification

The requirements herein apply universally across all possible deployments of the Accessibility Reward Primitive by Prime Agents. They include the steps that Agents must take to deploy the Primitive, including Global Activation of the Primitive, Instance Invocation, and ongoing management of the Primitive Instance\(s\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b4-bb9b-f542e56dabae
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.3.9.1.2.1 - Base Elements

The documents herein define base elements of the Accessibility Reward Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8022-9d87-c01157d7f958
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

####### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1 - Integrator Program

Integrators are actors that offer access to the Sky Protocol via their frontends or infrastructure. The documents herein define the Integrator Program, which includes the Accessibility Reward and Integration Boost. \(Base elements specific to the Integration Boost Primitive are defined in [A.2.4 - Base Elements](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73808181a1e6ee8f948733)\)

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d7-82df-e8cb3c209bd9
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.1 - Integrator Requirements

The documents herein define the requirements for Integrators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d4-b10a-f82822f70f6c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.1.1 - Alignment

The Integrator must be aligned with Sky’s overall strategy regarding promoting adoption of USDS. In the near term, this determination of a prospective or current Integrator’s alignment with Sky’s overall strategy is made by Operational GovOps in consultation with Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors. In the future Operational GovOps may make this determination themselves or consult with another actor to do so. Sky Core may choose whether to maintain an Integrator’s Reward Code in its sole and absolute discretion.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8086-b28a-ec64d02bac42
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.1.2 - Compliance With Local Laws And Regulations As A Condition Precedent To Integrators Receiving Accessibility Rewards

This document and its subdocuments define the jurisdictional compliance rules applicable to Integrators that operate user-facing frontends that integrate with, and thus offer access to, the Sky Protocol and receive Accessibility Rewards.  
  
Integrators are solely responsible for complying with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements related to their participation in the Integrator Program. Integrators represent and warrant that their participation and activities under the Integrator Program are and will remain in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.  
  
In connection with integrating with, and thereby providing access to, the Sky Protocol, Integrators must operate their frontends and infrastructure in compliance with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements in the jurisdictions applicable to their services. This requires compliance with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements in relation to frontend operations, marketing, and promotions in the jurisdictions where the Integrator provides access to the Sky Protocol through the integration.  
  
An Integrator’s right to participate in the Integrator Program is contingent upon its ongoing compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-801f-9398-cb61c7033ee5
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.1.2.1 - Consequence For Integrator Non-Compliance With Local Laws And Regulations

Sky Ecosystem Governance, in its absolute and unilateral discretion, retains the right to withhold, revoke, or demand immediate repayment of any and all Accessibility Rewards from any Integrator that is determined, suspected, or alleged to be in violation of the Atlas or any legal, regulatory, or other obligations associated with its integration with, and provision of access to, the Sky Protocol.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-803f-914e-d882e057231c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.1.2.2 - Removal From Integrator Program

If Sky Governance removes an Integrator from the Integrator Program, Operational GovOps must remove the Integrator from the list of Current Integrators in [A.2.4 - List Of Current Integrators](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73808d80a4c04556277a3e) and deactivate the Instances of the Accessibility Reward and Integration Boost Primitive associated with them.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e0-aa4c-db76c0e5e96e
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.2 - Integrator Applications

The documents herein define the process for applying to become an Integrator.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8020-8824-db33079dc737
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.2.1 - Near Term Process

In the near term, applications are made directly to Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors. Viridian Advisors must create and maintain a thread on the Sky Forum for Integrator Applications. Viridian Advisors reviews applications and coordinates with Prime Agents interested in working with specific applicants. Viridian Advisors issues Reward Codes to approved applicants.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ee-9ed8-de7602a696ae
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.2.1.1 - Integrator Program Applications

The list of Integrator Program applicants is defined as Active Data in [A.2.4 - List Of Integrator Applications](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380bfae81ebbe91ee843c).  
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
• The Responsible Party is Viridian Advisors.  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8081-973d-f3e11314ed36
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-08

########### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.2.1.1.0.6.1 - List Of Integrator Applications

The current Integrator Applications are:

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80bf-ae81-ebbe91ee843c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-23

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.2.2 - Long Term Process

In the long term, Integrator applications come exclusively through Prime Agents. Prime Agents may establish whatever processes they deem appropriate to receive inbound requests for potential partnerships.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8095-8e4e-cbac9c3c3f43
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.3 - Integrator Onboarding

The documents herein define the process for onboarding new Integrators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-804a-aa52-d9e23b34194d
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.3.1 - Near Term Process

The near term Integrator Onboarding process is as follows. When a Prime Agent’s Invocation of the Accessibility Reward Primitive involves an actor who is not yet an approved Integrator, that actor must submit an Integrator Application to Viridian Advisors. Viridian Advisors determines, in consultation with Operational GovOps, whether the Integrator Requirements are met, and if so issues a Reward Code to the applicant. After the Reward Code has been issued, the Invocation of the Primitive may proceed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e5-99e6-e730c5662f50
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.1.3.2 - Long Term Process

In the long term, Operational GovOps will review Integrator applications and determine whether the prospective partner satisfies the Integrator Requirements; if so, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code. Operational GovOps may contract with Viridian Advisors or another actor to perform the work of reviewing applications and issuing Reward Codes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80de-b7db-c8f80321d680
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

####### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2 - Reward Codes

The documents herein define base elements related to Reward Codes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8098-b627-d8a8d4e1d9bc
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.1 - Assignment

The documents herein define the process for assigning Reward Codes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ce-b78d-e2e8a8cdeb68
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.1.1 - Near Term Process

In the near term, Reward Codes are assigned by Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80cb-aaf5-f85961de57a2
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.1.2 - Long Term Process

In the long term, Reward Codes are assigned by Operational GovOps. Operational GovOps may contract with Viridian Advisors or another Ecosystem Actor to perform this work for them, at the discretion of Operational GovOps.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b0-b83a-e532e7f8fa7c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2 - Marking

To be eligible for the Accessibility Reward, USDS balances must be “marked” with a Reward Code using the agreed-on Tracking Methodology.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d1-ba01-cb19f5bec0d2
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2.1 - Ethereum Mainnet General Tracking Methodology

The general Tracking Methodology for Ethereum Mainnet is to specify the Reward Code as a parameter to depositing USDS into the Sky Savings Rate contract or Token Rewards contracts. This on-chain deposit data is then combined with withdrawal data. In the interim, this data is further processed by Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors to estimate net deposits associated with the Reward Code on a First In First Out \(FIFO\) basis. In the long term, Operational Executor Agents will wholly manage this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d5-ba6a-c12f7df61339
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2.2 - Ethereum Mainnet CoW Swap Tracking Methodology

The Tracking Methodology for CoW Swap on the Ethereum Mainnet is the same as the general process for the Ethereum Mainnet, with the exception that events on CoW Swap’s decentralized network of solvers are tracked instead.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8042-8601-fe7eea7098d7
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2.3 - Base Tracking Methodology

The Tracking Methodology for Base is to specify the Reward Code as a parameter in calls to the Base PSM contract. Conversions from USDS or USDC to sUSDS are considered “deposits” and net deposits are calculated using an approach similar to that on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e0-a452-c46e188cf0bf
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2.4 - Alternative Tracking Methodologies

The Tracking Methodologies specified above are not exclusive. Prime Agents and Operational GovOps can develop additional Tracking Methodologies, so long as those methodologies reasonably estimate USDS balances that are attributable to the holder of the Reward Code and there is no possibility that the same USDS balances could be “double counted” for multiple Reward Code holders. Tracking methodologies must be based on either \(1\) on-chain data or \(2\) off-chain data that can be independently verified or attested to by a third party.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80de-9158-e34c71a37e4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.2.5 - Lifetime

USDS balances are eligible for an Accessibility Reward for a period of ten \(10\) years from the date of the event marking the USDS balance with the Reward Code. The date of the marking event is determined based on the Primitive Instance’s specified Tracking Methodology.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-801e-a1b5-ebc7e87f9cbd
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.2.3 - Management

In the near term, Ecosystem Actor Viridian Advisors manages the list of Actor Reward Codes. In the future, all current Integrators and onboarding Integrators must be specified in [A.2.4 - Current And Onboarding Integrators](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380c397c0c042c8334c33) so that Prime Agents, through their Operational Executor Agents, can onboard new partners themselves without having to go through a single party.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c7-946a-d1166862f718
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.3 - Fees

The documents herein define the methodology for calculating the Accessibility Reward due to the reward recipient for USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them. The standard Accessibility Reward rate is set at 0.2%. The total 0.2% fee is divided between the frontend reward recipient and the relevant Prime Agent with an expectation of a 50/50 split.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8080-bd02-d93578337aed
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.3.1 - Fee For USDS and sUSDS Balances

The frontend reward recipient can earn up to a maximum of a 0.1% Accessibility Reward rate annualized on all USDS and sUSDS balances attributable to them. The specific fee apportionment must be specified in the relevant Prime Agent Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80df-8db3-ee80a36a5359
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.3.2 - Prime Agent Management Fee

The Prime Agent that Invoked the Accessibility Reward Primitive is entitled to at least a 0.1% Accessibility Reward rate annualized on all USDS and sUSDS balances that use their relevant Reward Code. The specific fee apportionment must be specified in the relevant Prime Agent Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80a7-9f4b-f610a10ef6bf
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4 - Rewards Distribution

The documents herein define base elements of the Accessibility Reward Primitive related to distribution of Accessibility Rewards.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8036-a579-f862d049c95d
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.1 - Reward Cadence

The Accessibility Reward is calculated and distributed on a monthly basis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-808d-96e2-c06936f299fd
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.2 - Reward Payment

The Accessibility Reward payment for each month is equal to   
  
\(1\) the average balance over the month, times   
\(2\) the annual Accessibility Reward Fee specified in [A.2.4 - Fees](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738080bd02d93578337aed), divided by   
\(3\) twelve \(12\).  
  
The Accessibility Reward Fee specified in [A.2.4 - Fees](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738080bd02d93578337aed) includes all of the following elements:  
  
1\) Fees for USDS and sUSDS Balances specified in [A.2.4 - Fee For USDS and sUSDS Balances](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380df8db3ee80a36a5359); and  
2\) the Prime Agent Management Fee specified in [A.2.4 - Prime Agent Management Fee](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380a79f4bf610a10ef6bf).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8004-9f94-e8f9a125dce0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.3 - Treasury Management

The documents herein define the treasury management process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e9-bed4-df71121a7d93
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.3.1 - Near-Term Process

In the near term, Amatsu calculates the Accessibility Reward on behalf of the Support Facilitators. The Accessibility Facilitators then pay the Accessibility Reward from the Accessibility Reward Controller Wallet located on the Ethereum Mainnet at `0x05F471262d15EECA4059DadE070e5BEd509a4e73` within seven \(7\) days of the end of every month. The balance of this wallet may be topped up to 3 million USDS. This funding is subject to an Executive Vote.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8022-92a2-ceb9e4827fb9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.3.2 - Long Term Process

In the long term, Operational GovOps calculates the Accessibility Reward for each month. Operational GovOps then pays the Accessibility Reward recipient from its Buffer. Later Sky Core reimburses the Operational Agent Buffer for the amount paid as part of the Settlement Cycle. This minimizes the role of Sky Core in Accessibility Reward payments and emphasizes the primary role of the Operational Executor Agent, acting through Operational GovOps, in implementing the Sky Primitives. The process is specified in further detail in [A.2.4 - Routine Protocol](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73807089a9c21962a54912).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8071-9f1f-e1ada4775b61
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.1.4.4 - Payment Errors

If it is discovered that previous Accessibility Reward calculations were made erroneously, underpayments are resolved retroactively. In cases where an Integrator was overpaid, the Prime Agent associated with the Integrator must reimburse Sky the overpayment amount and can use future Accessibility Rewards that the Integrator earns to reimburse itself.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-803d-af4a-c64cd5f11332
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

The list of Onboarding Integrators is defined as Active Data in [A.2.4 - List Of Onboarding Integrators](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738021b3f1f480da97e37a).

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.3.1.1.2 - Dependencies

This process is dependent on a Prime Agent Globally Activating the Accessibility Reward Primitive pursuant to [A.2.4 - Global Activation](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380a38583f706a6e9691f).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c6-a223-c0d64d1f33a4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.1.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• The Prime Agent identifies an opportunity to drive USDS adoption through an Accessibility Reward to either 1\) reward an existing Integrator for driving USDS adoption or 2\) incentivize a new actor to onboard as an Integrator to drive USDS adoption.  
  
• Existing Integrators  
  
    ◦ The Prime Agent and the third party, if applicable, develop a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to the actor using either on-chain or off-chain data.  
  
• Prospective Integrators  
  
    ◦ Near Term process:  
        • The Prospective Integrator must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Viridian Advisors per [A.2.4 - Near Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380ee9ed8de7602a696ae).  Post approval, Viridian Advisors issues a Reward Code to the Integrator.  
        • The Prime Agent and the third party develop a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to the actor using either on-chain or off-chain data.  
  
    ◦ Long Term process:  
        • The Prospective Integrator must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Operational GovOps pursuant to [A.2.4 - Long Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380958e4ecbac9c3c3f43). Post approval, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code to the Integrator.  
        • The Prime Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data. Where applicable, the plan should include how the Prime Agent will support the prospective Integrator in including the Reward Code in their on-chain infrastructure.  
  
• The Prime Agent may also be \(or choose to be\) an Integrator itself and deploy a Reward Code on its frontend to earn the Accessibility Reward.  
  
    ◦ Near Term process:  
        •** **If the Prime Agent is not already an approved Integrator, it must apply to the Program and be approved by Viridian Advisors. Post approval, Viridian Advisors issues a Reward Code to the Prime Agent.  
        • The Prime Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data.  
  
    ◦ Long Term process:  
        • The Prime Agent must first apply to the Integrator program and be approved by Operational GovOps pursuant to [A.2.4 - Long Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380958e4ecbac9c3c3f43). Post approval, Operational GovOps issues a Reward Code to the Prime Agent.  
        • The Prime Agent develops a plan to track USDS utilization attributable to it using either on-chain or off-chain data.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80db-a23c-d3e658466dc1
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and organized in sequential stages.  
  
• Drafting of Initial Planning Document  
  
    ◦ Create `Initial Planning Document`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Drafting`  
            • `Integrator`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Integrator Name  
            • `Reward Code`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Reward Code  
            • `Tracking Methodology`  
                ◦ New value: populate with details for tracking utilization  
            • `Custom Instance Parameters`  
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for any custom parameters  
        • Responsible Party: Prime Agent Team  
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Prime Agent's `Initial planning document` Status is set to `Drafting`   
• Initial Planning Document Triggered For GovOps review  
    ◦ Edit `Initial Planning Document`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Tracking Methodology`  
                ◦ New value: updated content, as applicable  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Ready for GovOps Review`  
        • Responsible party: Prime Agent  
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Prime Agent's `Initial planning document` Status is set to `Ready for GovOps review`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-805f-b64a-d23cd23a45e9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d4-bb31-d9e28e6d2744
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps \[automated\]  
• After Prime Agent's `Initial planning document` Status is set to `Ready for GovOps review`.

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.2.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• Operational GovOps reviews the `Initial Planning Document` to ensure:  
  
    ◦ Operational GovOps has the ability to operationalize the proposed tracking mechanism, and  
  
    ◦ The proposed tracking mechanism accurately reflects USDS usage attributable to the actor and there is no possibility that rewards could be “double counted” \(i.e. multiple actors being paid for the same USDS balance\).  
  
• Operational GovOps submits its feedback into the `Operational GovOps Review Document` \(created at Create `Operational GovOps Review Document`.\) along with suggested changes, if any.  
  
• The Prime Agent incorporates feedback from Operational GovOps and edits its `Initial Planning Document` as needed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-802e-90b7-cf8102318b7c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.  
  
• Create `Operational GovOps Review Document`.  
        •Updated fields  
            • `Initial Planning Document`  
                ◦ New Value: automatically links to respective Document  
            • `Feedback Summary`  
                ◦ New Value: GovOps populates with its review commentary and suggested changes that are agreed to by Agent and GovOps, if any.  
            • Responsible party: Operational GovOps.

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.3.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• The Prime Agent creates the `Artifact Edit Draft` Document and works to finalize its draft of the Artifact Edit per discussions with Operational GovOps.  
  
• When draft is finalized, the Prime Agent triggers the creation of the `Artifact Edit Proposal`, which inherits content from the finalized `Artifact Edit Draft` Document.  
  
• The Prime Agent submits the `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document to the Powerhouse system.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c4-91bb-cb962b8bdd12
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.3.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and implemented in sequential stages.  
  
• Agent creates `Artifact Edit Draft` document; drafting in progress:  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Draft`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `In Progress`  
            • Content  
                ◦ New value: populate with drafted content  
  
• Agent finalizes `Artifact Edit Draft`  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Draft`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Draft Finalized`  
  
• Powerhouse System Creates `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Content  
            • New value: Inherits data from Artifact Edit Draft content field.  
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps \[if not automated\]  
  
• Agent submits `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document to Powerhouse system  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Status:  
            • New value: set to  `Pending Facilitator Review`  
    ◦ Responsible party: Agent

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8084-b0c4-c72296c91de0
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8026-a2be-e87255dc026d
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.4.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• The Operational Facilitator reviews the `Artifact Edit Proposal` to ensure alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and the Agent Artifact.  
  
• Where the Proposal is determined to be aligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the `Artifact Edit Proposal`  Document to reflect their approval and commentary, if applicable.  
  
• Where the Proposal is determined to be misaligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the `Artifact Edit Proposal` document to reflect their rejection and commentary. Commentary is required where the Operational Facilitator rejects the proposal for misalignment.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-807d-b5d5-f521ff6bbe13
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.4.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein and organized as two mutually exclusive pathways. Once the Review outcome is determined by the Facilitator, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.  
  
• Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Approved`  
            • `Commentary`  
                ◦ New value \(optional\): populate with reasoning for Approval  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Approved by Facilitator` \[automated\]  
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator  
        • Trigger-Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition For Offchain Vote](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380bf903de5c58a1935fa)   
  
• Operational Facilitator Rejects proposal  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal`  
        ◦ Updated fields  
            • `Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Rejected`  
            • `Commentary`  
                ◦ New value \(required\): populate with reasoning for Rejection  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Rejected By Facilitator` \[automated\]  
        ◦ Responsible party: Operational Facilitator

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80a5-b8bc-ce861b63248f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.4.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process, if any.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c9-babd-ee81a7df8a25
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.3.5.1.2 - Dependencies

This process has no dependencies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d4-8de3-d2259491be93
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.5.2 - Process Flow

The process flow for this process is defined herein:  
  
• Using the finalized `Atlas Edit Proposal` content, the Operational Facilitator sets up an offchain Snapshot vote.  
  
• Prime Agent token holders vote on the proposal.  
  
• After the voting concludes, the Operational Facilitator records the result of the vote in the Powerhouse system.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-807d-a5fb-ee5366ca0f17
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.5.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein.  
  
• After Facilitator prepares Snapshot vote:  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Pending Poll`  
            • Off-chain Snapshot  
                ◦ New value: Populate with link to the official Snapshot page  
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator  
  
• Mutually Exclusive Input Pathways: The two Primitive Inputs below are mutually exclusive pathways. Once the vote concludes, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.  
  
    ◦ Proposal Passes  
        • Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
            • Update fields  
                ◦ Status  
                    • New value: set to `Poll Approved`  
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.  
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal Passes  
  
    ◦ Proposal Fails  
        • Updated fields  
            • Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
                ◦ Status  
                    • New value: set to `Poll Rejected`  
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.  
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal Fails

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e7-9b3b-fd51985dc372
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.3.5.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c2-91cd-e597dcf27586
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80bf-a133-e8830d087378
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.3.5.4.2 - Agent Artifact Updates

The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process. The Output “sets” are mutually exclusive.  
  
• Proposal Passes  
  
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: [Proposal Passes](https://www.notion.so/Proposal-Passes-1adf2ff08d7381f0bf56f4a7d0044f20)  
    ◦ **Edit ****`Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name`**  
        • Updated fields  
            • Invocation Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Approved`  
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps  
        • Trigger - Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition For Artifact Update](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380039fb4d555a447a676)   
• Proposal Fails  
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: [Proposal Fails](https://www.notion.so/Proposal-Fails-1adf2ff08d7381ae889cf6020c003e86)  
    ◦ **Edit ****`Primitive Hub Document/In Progress Invocations Directory/Instance Name`**  
        • Updated fields  
            • Invocation Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Rejected`  
        • Other Document Operations:  
  
            • `Instance Configuration Document` is `Archived` in Primitive Hub Document/Hub Data Repository  
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-801d-a3e1-c19d12e9f9a3
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1 - Process Definition For Reward Calculation By Operational Govops

The documents herein define the process for Accessibility Reward Calculation by Operational GovOps. The Accessibility Reward Calculation includes the calculation of the Fees for Unrewarded USDS Balances, the Fees for Rewarded USDS Balances, and the Prime Agent Management Fee.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8034-b71d-eac2bb84845c

The process initiation logic is specified herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80d6-98a0-f64fc5748b6d
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.1.1 - Triggers

Triggers are specified herein.

**Document Type:** Core

########### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.1.1.1 - Time-Based Triggers

This process is triggered on the 1st of every month for each Instance of the Accessibility Reward Primitive with an Instance Status of `Active`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-800c-a3f2-da66d191d6a4
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.1.1.2 - Document Update Triggers

None.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8046-ac71-e142627ee4bc
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.1.2 - Dependencies

This process has no dependencies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8064-a4a2-e245a0ed9805
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• Operational GovOps calculates the eligible USDS and sUSDS balances using the Tracking Methodology specified in the Primitive Instance.  
  
• Operational GovOps calculates the Accessibility Reward due based on the USDS and sUSDS balances and the Accessibility Reward formula for each.  
  
• Operational GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with both the underlying data and their calculations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-805d-9f9e-ea60a5070c8f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.  
  
• Edit `Accessibility Reward Payments` Document \(Active Data\)  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Status  
            • New value: set to `In Progress`  
        • Underlying data  
            • New value: populate with underlying data used to calculate the eligible USDS and sUSDS balances  
        • Eligible USDS balance  
            • New value: populate with calculated value  
        • Eligible sUSDS balance  
            • New value: populate with calculated value  
        • Accessibility Reward Due  
            • New value: populate with calculated value.  
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps.  
    ◦ Trigger-Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition For Reward Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738024ade9cb0fdb643aea).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ec-bb4f-e8ed4604a9e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.1.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8095-ac91-cbb265bef4ac
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.3.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• Core GovOps reviews Accessibility Rewards calculations, including underlying data and calculation of balances and rewards due.  
  
• Once Core GovOps has completed review, they update Powerhouse system to indicate that they confirm the accuracy of the Accessibility Reward amounts.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80fe-8863-f7f894724b4f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.3.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein and are mutually exclusive pathways.  
  
• Core GovOps Confirms Accuracy of Payment  
    ◦ Edit `Accessibility Reward Payments` Active Data Document  
        • Updated fields  
            • Core GovOps Review/Confirmation  
                ◦ New value: populate with Yes  
            • Core GovOps Review/Commentary  
                ◦ New value \(optional\): populate with reasoning  
        • Responsible party: Core GovOps  
        • Trigger - Process: [A.2.4 - Sky Core Atlas Updates](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73801482fce8530724fb16)   
  
• Core GovOps Finds Inaccurate Payment  
    ◦ Edit `Accessibility Reward Payments` Active Data Document  
        • Updated fields  
            • Core GovOps Review/Confirmation  
                ◦ New value: populate with No  
            • Core GovOps Review/Commentary  
                ◦ New value \(required\): populate with reasoning  
        • Responsible party: Core GovOps  
        • Trigger - Process: Payment Inaccuracy Previously Found By Core GovOps

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ca-abe9-da27e31c37ce
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.3.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8042-97aa-dd081ddabd6b
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

########## A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.3.4.1 - Sky Core Atlas Updates

The Sky Core Atlas documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process.  
  
• Payment Accuracy Previously Confirmed By Core GovOps  
    ◦ The document [A.2.4 - Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d7380d3a912f650063a8829) in the Sky Core Atlas is updated as follows:  
        • Updated Fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: populate with `Pending Payment`  
            • Confirmed Reimbursement Due  
                ◦ New value: populate with total Reimbursement amount  
            • Reward Period  
                ◦ New Value: populate with reward period  
            • Operational Executor Agent  
                ◦ New value: Populate with name of Operational Executor Agent  
            • Prime Agent  
                ◦ New value: Populate with name of Prime Agent.  
        • Responsible Party: Core GovOps  
        • Triggers: [A.2.4 - Process Definition For Executive Vote Reimbursement](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380dc94dff8b959befe4e)   
• Payment Inaccuracy Previously Found By Core GovOps  
    ◦ TBD

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8014-82fc-e8530724fb16
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.4.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• Core GovOps includes the Accessibility Reward reimbursement in the next standard Executive Vote.  
  
• After the Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8053-ab41-dab8055be526
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.1.2.4.1.4.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein and organized as sequential stages.  
  
• Core GovOps adds reimbursement to Executive Vote  
    ◦ Edit `Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Executive Vote Settlement/Executive Vote  
                ◦ New value: links to proposal  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Added to Executive Vote`  
  
• After Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates Powerhouse system  
    ◦ Edit `Sky Core Accessibility Reward Reimbursement Amounts`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details/ Amount Paid  
                ◦ New value: populate with amount paid to reimburse Operational Executor Agent Buffer  
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details / Tx Hash  
                ◦ New value: Populate with transaction hash  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Completed`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c0-a893-e298db27e57b
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

#### A.2.3.9.2 - Integration Boost Primitive

The documents herein govern the Integration Boost Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8080-88d1-d55ef39f46ec
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

##### A.2.3.9.2.1 - Introduction

The documents herein provide an introduction to the Integration Boost Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-802a-a6a4-e52856c2d6b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.9.2.1.1 - Purpose

The Integration Boost Primitive provides a low complexity version of the Sky Savings Rate to integration partners that hold USDS or lending markets that integrate USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ce-95b6-f268c4267e52
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.9.2.1.2 - Allowed Number Of Instances

Multiple instances of the Integration Boost Primitive are allowed. Each instance corresponds to an Integration Boost program.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-805e-ac85-ed42d1a3c4cd
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

###### A.2.3.9.2.1.3 - Multi-Instance Coordinator Document

An Agent Artifact that has more than one active instance of the Integration Boost Primitive is not required to have a `Multi-Instance Coordinator Document`, since each Instance can be managed independently.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8053-9854-ed25a20fefe1
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

##### A.2.3.9.2.2 - Global Specification

The requirements herein apply universally across all possible deployments of the Integration Boost Primitive by Prime Agents. They include the steps that Agents must take to deploy the Primitive, including Global Activation of the Primitive, Instance Invocation, and ongoing management of the Primitive Instance\(s\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8096-bf17-edcc355dfcbb
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.3.9.2.2.1 - Base Elements

The documents herein define the base elements of the Integration Boost Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8081-81a1-e6ee8f948733
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

####### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.1 - Integration Boost Partners

The Integration Boost is provided to DeFi protocol partners that allow users to deposit USDS balances. Sky makes payments to Integration Boost partners equal to the Sky Savings Rate times the Unrewarded USDS balances in their protocol. The expectation is that the Integration Boost Partner will pass these Integration Boost Payments along to their users, thus providing USDS users with the equivalent of the Sky Savings Rate.  
  
Integration Boost partners are part of the Integrator Program as defined in [A.2.4 - Integrator Program](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380d782dfe8cb3c209bd9).   
  
Current and onboarding Integrators are recorded in [A.2.4 - Current And Onboarding Integrators](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380c397c0c042c8334c33).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-802f-83b2-d9886c405931
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.1.1 - No Obligation To Pass Through Integration Boost Payments

Integration Boost partners are under no obligation to pass through any portion of Integration Boost payments to their users. Nevertheless, the expectation is that most partners will pass through Integration Boost payments to remain competitive with other protocols.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ec-976f-eaf2ed2d0961
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

####### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.2 - Data Submission

The documents herein specify data submission requirements related to the Integrator Program.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8018-a742-e390d93817de
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.2.1 - Data Verifiability Requirement

Integration Boost Partners must submit net deposit data in such a form that the data can be verified by Sky using on-chain data. In the short term, Ecosystem Actor BA Labs calculates the net deposit data based on on-chain events and makes it available to Operational GovOps through an API endpoint. The API endpoint is [https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/incentivized-pools/](https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/incentivized-pools/) for Integration Boost Partners on Ethereum and [https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/solana-incentives/](https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/solana-incentives/) on Solana. In the long term, Operational GovOps assumes responsibility for ensuring that the data submission meets the on-chain verifiability requirement; this is done as part of their review of Invocations of the Integration Boost Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80ba-b5c3-cc652fcb674a
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.2.2 - Data Submission Responsible Party

Integration Boost Partners must also identify a responsible party that will submit the net deposit data. In the near term, this is BA Labs. In the long term, this may be the Integration Boost Partner, a third party contractor, or the Prime Agent or Operational GovOps themselves if they agree to do so.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8075-aedf-ca0e97329482
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.2.3 - Data Submission Frequency

Data is calculated on a weekly basis from Monday to Sunday for payment the following Monday. Failure to submit data on time will result in a delay of payment of the Integration Boost until the following week.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c1-a0c7-f8c13c47822a
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

####### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3 - Distribution

The documents herein define base elements of the Integration Boost Primitive related to the distribution of the Integration Boost.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8004-8fe0-ff163fa25192
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3.1 - Cadence

The Cadence is the frequency at which the Integration Boost is calculated and distributed. There are only three options available for the Reward Cadence: \(1\) weekly, \(2\) biweekly, or \(3\) monthly.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-800b-ac84-d37e5d12bbff
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3.2 - Treasury Management

The documents herein define the treasury management process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-803c-bd96-ffd0c882b6fc
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3.2.1 - Near Term Process

In the short term, the Support Facilitators directly pay Integration Boost recipients from Integration Boost wallets. The balance of these wallets on the Ethereum Mainnet may be topped up to 3 million USDS, although the balance of Integration Boost wallets across blockchains may exceed that amount. The funding is subject to an Executive Vote. The addresses of these wallets are:  
  
• Ethereum \(ETH\) - `0xD6891d1DFFDA6B0B1aF3524018a1eE2E608785F7`  
• Solana \(SOL\) - `7Gf8AqAtmYkkVhQbbJr18RxVUuoGjA8ZEw3Af4NauyaY`  
• Solana Hot Wallet - `BKT2JR5wRWsXQBxrht1LbbbKtgUkz3sJdaBY8UzgRJPL`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8006-8d52-f441b4e85f5b
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3.2.2 - Long Term Process

In the long term, Operational GovOps calculates the Integration Boost for each occurrence of the specified Cadence. Operational GovOps then pays the Integration Boost recipient from its Buffer. Later, Sky Core reimburses the Operational Agent Buffer for the amount paid as part of the Settlement Cycle. This minimizes the role of Sky Core in Integration Boost payments and emphasizes the primary role of the Operational Executor Agent, acting through Operational GovOps, in implementing the Sky Primitives. The process is specified in further detail in [A.2.4 - Routine Protocol](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73804ca39ce43206812e8a).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e5-9492-cee6e21858d7
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.3.3 - Payment Errors

If it is discovered that previous Integration Boost calculations were made erroneously, underpayments are resolved retroactively. In cases where an Integrator was overpaid, the Prime Agent associated with the Integrator must reimburse Sky the overpayment amount and can use future Integration Boost payments that the Integrator earns to reimburse itself.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e2-9262-c04109146b4e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.3.9.2.2.1.4 - Accessibility Rewards

Net USDS balances in a DeFi protocol that is receiving an Integration Boost are also eligible for the Accessibility Reward.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b9-9a79-d4760cafc9dc
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.4.1 - Reporting Of Net USDS Balances Is Valid Tracking Methodology

The methodology used to report net USDS balances in the protocol for the Integration Boost is itself an acceptable Tracking Methodology for purposes of the Accessibility Reward.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b9-bfda-ea8ef1815cde
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.4.2 - No Double Payments

Accessibility Reward may only be paid on net USDS balances of an Integration Boost partner to the extent that an Accessibility Reward is not already being paid on those balances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80bc-9d0d-db77b55d06b8
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.1.4.3 - Prime Agent May Choose Whether To Share Accessibility Reward With Integration Boost Partner

Prime Agents may choose whether to share Accessibility Reward payments with Integration Boost Partners.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-808a-8716-c6275738b5fc
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.1.1.2 - Dependencies

This process is dependent on a Prime Agent Globally Activating the Integration Boost Primitive pursuant to [A.2.4 - Global Activation](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380f6b6e2dcc21d43f842).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c4-b823-e61e090b75d3
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.1.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• The Prime Agent identifies a DeFi protocol or market where an Integration Boost would drive adoption. The Prime Agent estimates the potential earnings from the Accessibility Reward associated with the incremental USDS usage versus the operational cost of funding the Sky Savings Rate payouts.  
  
• The Prime Agent and the prospective Integration Boost partner discuss \(1\) the proposed Integration Boost cadence and \(2\) whether and how the protocol operator also receives a portion of the Accessibility Reward.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c7-bd26-efd1b671e59d
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and organized in sequential stages.  
  
• Drafting of Initial Planning Document  
  
    ◦ Create `Initial Planning Document`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Drafting`  
            • `Integration Partner Name`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner name  
            • `Integration Partner Reward Address`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner reward address  
            • `Integration Partner Chain`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Partner chain  
            • `Integration Boost Cadence`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Integration Boost cadence  
            • `Integration Boost Data Submission Format`  
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for format of data submission  
            • `Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor`  
                ◦ New Value: set to Actor responsible for data submission  
            • `Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy`  
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for handling adjustments to Sky Savings Rate  
            • `Custom Instance Parameters`  
                ◦ New Value: populate with details for any custom parameters  
        • Responsible Party: Prime Agent Team  
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Prime Agent's `Initial planning document` Status is set to `Drafting`   
  
• Initial Planning Document triggered for GovOps review  
  
    ◦ Edit `Initial Planning Document`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy`  
                ◦ New value: updated content, as applicable  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Ready for GovOps Review`  
        • Responsible party: Prime Agent  
        • Trigger - Required Output: After Prime Agent's `Initial planning document` Status is set to `Ready for GovOps review`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8043-817d-e7af89451feb
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8098-83fe-d84b89ae9ca7

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.2.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• Operational GovOps reviews the `Initial Planning Document` to ensure:  
    ◦ The submitted data can be verified using on-chain data;  
    ◦ the Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy ensures that the payment on USDS balances equals the Sky Savings Rate, and  
    ◦ the submitted data accurately reflects USDS deposits in the Integration Partner protocol and there is no possibility that rewards could be “double counted” \(i.e. multiple actors being paid for the same USDS balance\).  
  
• Operational GovOps submits its feedback into the `Operational GovOps Review Document` \(created at Create `Operational GovOps Review Document`\) along with suggested changes, if any.  
  
• The Prime Agent incorporates feedback from Operational GovOps and edits its `Initial Planning Document` as needed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8069-9b30-cc2190124334
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.2.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.  
  
• Create `Operational GovOps Review Document`  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • `Initial Planning Document`  
            • New Value: automatically links to respective Document  
        • `Feedback Summary`  
            • New Value: GovOps populates with its review commentary and suggested changes that are agreed to by Agent and GovOps, if any.  
        • Responsible party: Operational GovOps.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80de-a428-e77507ce5d2f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8062-8bbe-debef86e2456
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.3.1.2 - Dependencies

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.3.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• The Prime Agent creates the `Artifact Edit Draft` Document and works to finalize its draft of the Artifact Edit per discussions with Operational GovOps.  
  
• When draft is finalized, the Prime Agent triggers the creation of the `Artifact Edit Proposal`, which inherits content from the finalized `Artifact Edit Draft` Document.  
  
• The Prime Agent submits the `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document to the Powerhouse system.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80f1-b6e2-eec9f20cc3df
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.3.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs for this process are defined herein and implemented in sequential stages.  
  
• Agent creates `Artifact Edit Draft` document; drafting in progress  
  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Draft`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `In Progress`  
            • Content  
                ◦ New value: populate with drafted content  
  
• Agent finalizes `Artifact Edit Draft`  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Draft`  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Draft Finalized`  
  
• Powerhouse System Creates `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Content  
            • New value: Inherits data from `Artifact Edit Draft` Document’s Content field.  
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps \[if not automated\]  
  
• Agent submits `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document to Powerhouse system  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Status:  
            • New value: set to  `Pending Facilitator Review`  
    ◦ Responsible Party: Agent

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-804b-9d7f-c7323d6c7252
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8014-bb5f-c16bc98cf4ec
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

    ◦ Active Instances/Instance Name/Instance Status  
        • New value: `Proposal Pending Facilitator Review`  
• Responsible Party: Operational GovOps  
• Triggers: [A.2.4 - Process Definition for Operational Facilitator Review](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738020aae5d182a4ef7134).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e7-99d5-f75b34ecde1c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8020-aae5-d182a4ef7134

Triggers are specified herein.

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.4.1.2 - Dependencies

This process has no dependencies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8022-a0df-f46817a7f363
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.4.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• The Operational Facilitator reviews the `Artifact Edit Proposal` to ensure alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and the Agent Artifact.  
  
• Where the Proposal is determined to be aligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the `Artifact Edit Proposal`  Document to reflect their approval and commentary, if any.  
  
• Where the Proposal is determined to be misaligned, the Operational Facilitator updates the `Artifact Edit Proposal` document to reflect their rejection and commentary. Commentary is required where the Operational Facilitator rejects the proposal for misalignment.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8032-a283-cb74ad9def8e
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.4.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein and organized as two mutually exclusive pathways. Once the Review outcome is determined by the Facilitator, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.  
  
• Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Approved`  
            • `Commentary`  
                ◦ New value \(optional\): populate with reasoning for Approval  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Approved by Facilitator` \[automated\]  
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator  
        • Trigger-Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition for Offchain Vote](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738097a6a5d7da66a36632).  
   
• Operational Facilitator Rejects Proposal  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal`  
        • Updated fields  
            • `Operational Facilitator Review/Review Decision`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Rejected`  
            • `Commentary`  
                ◦ New value \(required\): populate with reasoning for Rejection  
            • `Status`  
                ◦ New value: set to `Proposal Rejected By Facilitator` \[automated\]  
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80b2-ab00-d96e7ee3f7e6
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.4.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process, if any.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80f3-b2ae-db692669ded2
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.

This process is triggered by the Artifact Update specified at Operational Facilitator Approves Proposal.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8079-bcdd-d11a67934abf
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.1.2 - Dependencies

This process has no dependencies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-809b-b452-df36923f0a0c
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.2 - Process Flow

The process flow for this process is defined herein:  
  
• Using the finalized `Atlas Edit Proposal` content, the Operational Facilitator sets up an offchain Snapshot vote.  
  
• Prime Agent token holders vote on the proposal.  
  
• After the voting concludes, the Operational Facilitator records the result of the vote in the Powerhouse system.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8017-b655-c99fc377f79b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are defined herein.  
  
• After Facilitator prepares Snapshot vote:  
    ◦ Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
        • Updated fields  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Pending Poll`  
            • Off-chain Snapshot  
                ◦ New value: Populate with link to the official Snapshot page  
        • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator  
  
• Mutually Exclusive Input Pathways: The two Primitive Inputs below are mutually exclusive pathways. Once the vote concludes, the corresponding Pathway is followed to the exclusion of the other.  
  
    ◦ Proposal Passes  
        • Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
            • Update fields  
                ◦ Status  
                    • New value: set to `Poll Approved`  
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.  
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal passes  
  
    ◦ Proposal Fails  
        • Updated fields  
            • Edit `Artifact Edit Proposal` Document  
                ◦ Status  
                    • New value: set to `Poll Rejected`  
            • Responsible party: Operational Facilitator.  
            • Trigger - Required Output: Proposal fails

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80bd-afdf-d7d9eb3121b3
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.4 - Required Outputs

The documents herein specify the required outputs from this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-802a-9360-e262e9511275
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.4.1 - Sky Core Atlas Updates

No Sky Core Atlas documents are updated as the output of this process.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8000-8e24-f959442d588d
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.3.5.4.2 - Agent Artifact Updates

The Agent Artifact documents specified herein are updated as the output of this process. The Output “sets” are mutually exclusive.   
  
• Proposal passes  
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Passes   
    ◦ **Edit** `Primitive Hub Document`  
        • Fields Updated  
            • Active instances/instance name/instance status - set to `Approved`  
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps  
        • Trigger - Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition for Artifact Update](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d738096ab60d9ddadeeb69b).   
  
• Proposal fails  
    ◦ Required Primitive Input Trigger: Proposal Fails   
    ◦ **Edit** `Primitive Hub Document`  
        • Fields Updated  
            • Active instances/instance name/instance status - set to `Rejected`  
        • Other Document Operations:  
            • `Instance Configuration Document` is `Archived` in Primitive Hub Document/Data Repository  
        • Responsible Party: Operational GovOps

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8058-a715-efb37089f852
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######## A.2.3.9.2.2.3.6.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The following inputs must be submitted into the Primitive using the Powerhouse interface.  
  
• Edit `Instance Configuration Document` to reflect ratified Primitive Instance.  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Parameters/Status - automatically inherits from `Primitive Hub Document`  
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Name  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Partner Name` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Reward Address  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Partner Reward Address` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Partner Chain  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Partner Chain` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Cadence  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Boost Cadence` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Data Submission Format  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Boost Data Submission Format` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Boost Data Submission Responsible Actor` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy  
            • New Value: set to `Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy` value from approved Proposal  
        • Parameters/Custom Instance Parameters  
            • New Value: set to `Custom Instance Parameters` value from approved Proposal  
        •  Responsible party: Operational GovOps \[if not automated\]

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8022-b571-cb1abae86ea8
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

########### A.2.3.9.2.2.4.1.1.1.1.1 - Time-Based Triggers

This process is triggered at the end of each occurrence of the Cadence for each Instance of the Integration Boost Primitive with an Instance Status of `Active`.

**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.4.1.1.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein:  
  
• At the end of each Integration Boost period, the data provider submits the net USDS deposit data to Powerhouse.  
  
• Operational GovOps verifies this data by checking on-chain balances and confirms that there are no discrepancies.  
  
• Operational GovOps then calculates the payout based on the net USDS deposit data and the Sky Savings Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8077-ab4e-ebf42f623783
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.4.1.1.3 - Required Primitive Inputs

The required Primitive Inputs to this process are specified herein.  
  
• Edit `Integration Boost Payments` Document \(Active Data\)  
    ◦ Updated fields  
        • Status  
            • New value: set to `In Progress`  
        • Underlying data  
            • New value: populate with underlying data used to calculate the net deposits  
        • Net deposits  
            • New value: populate with calculated value  
        • Integration Boost payment due  
            • New value: populate with calculated value.  
    ◦ Responsible party: Operational GovOps.  
    ◦ Trigger-Process: [A.2.4 - Process Definition For Integration Boost Payment Issuance From Operational Executor Agent Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380368bd1c7936f273bc9).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8041-ada7-f41758cac29f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80a7-b6ff-db616b5b094f
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

######### A.2.3.9.2.2.4.1.2.2 - Process Flow

The process flow is defined herein.  
  
• Operational GovOps makes the payment to the reward address specified in the Primitive Instance from the Operational Executor Agent Buffer.  
  
• Operational GovOps updates the Powerhouse system with the transaction details.

            • Executive Vote Settlement/Executive Vote  
                ◦ New value: links to proposal  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Added to Executive Vote`  
  
• After Executive Vote passes, Core GovOps updates Powerhouse system  
    ◦ Edit `Sky Core Integration Boost Reimbursement Amounts`  
        •  Updated fields  
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details/ Amount Paid  
                ◦ New value: populate with amount paid to reimburse Operational Executor Agent Buffer.  
            • Executive Vote Settlement / Transaction Details / Tx Hash  
                ◦ New value: Populate with transaction hash  
            • Status  
                ◦ New value: set to `Completed`

#### A.2.3.9.3 - Pioneer Chain Primitive

The documents herein govern the Pioneer Chain Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-802e-b115-dd87c80ba766
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

##### A.2.3.9.3.1 - Introduction

The documents herein provide an introduction to the Pioneer Chain Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80c4-9393-c92b9cbd8ed9
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.3.9.3.1.1 - Pioneer Prime Requirements

Pioneer Primes must be designated by the official team or foundation of the Pioneer Chain, they must be created for the specific purpose of being a Pioneer Prime from their genesis moment, and it is only possible for a Pioneer Chain to have Pioneer Prime once, and for a Pioneer Prime to have the Pioneer Prime status with a single blockchain, once.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-807e-a916-ed1388511ba0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.3.9.3.1.2 - Pioneer Prime Benefits

Pioneer Primes gain benefits and responsibilities related to the general adoption of USDS on the Pioneer Chain, and this benefit comes in two forms.  
  
First, during the Pioneer Phase, the Pioneer Prime counts as having tagged, for the purposes of calculating the Accessibility Reward, all USDS and sUSDS accounts and balances on the Pioneer Chain that have not been tagged by another Prime. At the end of the Pioneer Phase, all untagged USDS accounts and balances are one-time tagged by the Pioneer Prime, and this tag will remain normally for the following ten \(10\) years unless tagged by a different Prime, or retagged.  
  
Second, during the Pioneer Phase, all Unrewarded USDS bridged to the Pioneer Chain counts towards Pioneer Incentive Pool payments, see [A.2.4 - Pioneer Incentive Pool](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73801691ebe8ae6bdc92a6).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-804b-9188-fea5262ef72c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.3.9.3.1.2.1 - Pioneer Phase

For purposes of [A.2.4 - Pioneer Prime Benefits](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73804b9188fea5262ef72c), the Pioneer Phase is a period beginning on the date that a Prime Agent satisfies the Pioneer Prime Requirements specified in [A.2.4 - Pioneer Prime Requirements](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73807ea916ed1388511ba0) and ending three \(3\) years thereafter.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 217f2ff0-8d73-8067-921f-e8dce7bc1b6b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.3.9.3.1.3 - Pioneer Incentive Pool

Every monthly settlement cycle, an amount of funds equivalent to the Sky Savings Rate multiplied by the balance of Unrewarded USDS is paid into the Pioneer Prime’s SubProxy Account \(the “Pioneer Incentive Pool”\). 80% of the funds going to the Pioneer Incentive Pool are not the property of the Pioneer Primes and must instead be used as incentives to promote general adoption of USDS on the Pioneer Chain. This must provide general benefit for the sake of growing USDS activity on the Pioneer Chain and cannot be biased towards benefiting the Pioneer Prime. The remaining 20% of the funds going to the Pioneer Incentive Pool may be retained by the Pioneer Prime as income.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-8016-91eb-e8ae6bdc92a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.3.9.3.1.3.1 - Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool

A Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool is a temporary, chain-specific incentive mechanism designed to bootstrap USDS adoption on a new blockchain before a formal Pioneer Prime is established for that chain. It allows a designated Agent to direct incentive payments to ecosystem partners on that specific chain. The Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool may operate under distinct rules that differ from the standard Pioneer Incentive Pool, which rules will be specified in an Ecosystem Accord between the respective Agent and Sky Core. A Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool serves as a transitional phase until the conditions for a formal Pioneer Incentive Pool are met. See [A.2.4 - Pioneer Incentive Pool](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73801691ebe8ae6bdc92a6).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-80d4-91e5-ea4171470c7d
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

### A.2.3.10 - Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives

Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives are Sky Primitives focused on capital allocation and risk management.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 1b2f2ff0-8d73-801b-8e84-c86a016fa6cb
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

#### A.2.3.10.1 - Allocation System Primitive

The Allocation System Primitive is the base mechanism that enables Prime Agents to put up Risk Capital to deploy USDS collateral into different opportunities that provide risk-adjusted return, by borrowing at the Base Rate from Sky, and following Asset Liability Management restrictions on the liquidity of deployed assets.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b2f2ff0-8d73-808d-9531-f3f0d06a63b1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

##### A.2.3.10.1.1 - Allocation System Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for initial setup and ongoing management of an Allocation Instance \(alternatively referred to as “conduits”\) as part of the Allocation System Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b2f2ff0-8d73-8052-8d63-e6ffa9de903c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.2.3.10.1.1.1 - Base Elements

The documents herein define base elements of the Allocation System Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-802a-ba53-ff35bc6aa2d8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.2.3.10.1.1.1.1 - Liquidity Layer Parameter Definitions

The documents herein define common parameters of implementations of the Allocation System based on the Spark Liquidity Layer, including the Grove Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80cb-aa5b-e548d92645de
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.2.3.10.1.1.1.1.1 - Rate Limiter

Rate Limiter refers to the overall mechanism or system that limits the volume of token movements over time, implemented via the `RateLimits` contracts. The Rate Limiter manages multiple rate limits and enforces constraints on controller operations to prevent rapid asset drainage and mitigate risks from compromised relayers or other attacks. This ensures that the maximum amount of tokens processed within a specific time period stays within safe bounds. The `RateLimits` contracts and their addresses for each chain can be found in the Allocation System Primitive for a Prime, under ALM Contracts.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80df-933d-cea5d2fbac14
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.2.3.10.1.1.1.1.2 - Rate Limits
