Standard Activity Staking Rewards \(SASR\) provide rewards to users who contribute to security by staking and delegating their SKY votes at least once.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8015-b8da-f48745435d59
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

####### A.2.4.1.2.5.2.2 - Payout From Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer

The Standard Activity Staking Reward Distribution occurs linearly over the course of each month. The monthly payout from the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer \(SASRB\) is equal to the lesser of \(1\) 20% of Step 4 Capital, or \(2\) 15% of the SASRB, with a minimum floor of 5% of the SASRB.  
  
In periods of high profitability, funds build up in the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer. Then in periods of low profitability these funds are paid out from the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer, ensuring that there is a continued incentive for involvement in governance even in periods of low profitability to maintain security.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-802d-951e-fac41527bdec
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

####### A.2.4.1.2.5.2.3 - Standard Activity Staker Qualification

SKY that is both staked and delegated to an Aligned Delegate will qualify for Standard Activity Staking Rewards.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8099-9cc1-ee95b8f39002
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

#### A.2.4.1.3 - Sourcing Of Internal Senior Risk Capital From Surplus Buffers

Internal Senior Risk Capital \(ISRC\) consists of a portion of the excess capital of the Sky Protocol that is reinvested in providing Senior Risk Capital to Prime Agents. ISRC is sourced from the various Buffers defined in [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d7380c8b637e83e07261ce9), according to the specifications of the subdocuments below.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8036-8418-c3bf66351cc0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

##### A.2.4.1.3.1 - USDS in High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer

All USDS in the High Activity Staking Rewards Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-808e-bf68-d328524e2a32
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

##### A.2.4.1.3.2 - USDS In Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer

All USDS in the Standard Activity Staking Rewards Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-80f3-a7f3-deba0e031f5a
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

##### A.2.4.1.3.3 - USDS In Smart Burn Buffer

All USDS in the Smart Burn Buffer is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-80bf-9a25-d885fefa7d54
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

##### A.2.4.1.3.4 - USDS In Stability Capital Buffer Above Target

All USDS in the Stability Capital Buffer above the Stability Capital Buffer Target is used for Internal Senior Risk Capital. Since this capital is already above the optimal target to absorb risk it can be redirected to a more efficient use.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-80f0-b615-d60839f48c94
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

##### A.2.4.1.3.5 - USDS In Stability Capital Buffer Below Target

One third of the USDS in the Stability Capital Buffer below the Stability Capital Buffer Target is also used for Internal Senior Risk Capital. This ensures that there is always some Internal Senior Risk Capital to drive the growth of the ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8060-8093-f51e8e964aaa
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

#### A.2.4.1.4 - Short Term Transitionary Measures

As a short-term transitionary measure, the net revenue of the Sky Protocol is currently allocated manually through Executive Votes rather than through the allocation steps defined above. This manual process will be removed in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8023-98b2-ca0f05f6a435
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

## A.2.5 - Sky Core Monthly Settlement Cycle

This Article governs the Sky Core Monthly Settlement Cycle \(MSC\), a recurring process that synchronizes core financial operations, governance functions, and risk management activities across the ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-807c-80f0-e58dde70cc27
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-17

### A.2.5.1 - Monthly Settlement Cycle Overview

The documents herein define the Monthly Settlement Cycle \(MSC\), a standardized, system-wide process executed at the end of each calendar month.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-80e2-8cd0-d72fcaed9923
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

#### A.2.5.1.1 - Operational Processes

The Monthly Settlement Cycle \(MSC\) synchronizes several key operational processes across the ecosystem, including:  
      
1. Sky Protocol’s net revenue from the previous month is calculated and allocated through the steps of the Treasury Management Function. See [A.2.5](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d73806683c7dbce9a54a65d).   
     
2. The monthly Senior Risk Capital \(SRC\) origination process is settled: the clearing price is established, costs are deducted from winning Prime Agents’ accounts, and their accounts are credited with Originated SRC \(OSRC\) for the upcoming month. See [A.3.3 - Settlement Of Origination](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d7380f992f1c4c002cadb83).   
      
3. Queued conversions between USDS and srUSDS within the SRC system are processed. See [A.3.3 - Deposit And Redemption Queues](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d7380d58e22e3186c025f28).   
      
4. Pioneer Incentive Pools are funded with an amount equivalent to the Sky Savings Rate multiplied by the balance of Unrewarded USDS. See [A.2.4 - Pioneer Chain Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73802eb115dd87c80ba766).   
      
5. The Smart Burn Buffer's burning operation occurs synchronously with each Monthly Settlement Cycle. At each cycle, the system calculates the appropriate Burn Rate based on current market conditions and executes the burn operation, if applicable, according to the formula specified in the Atlas. See [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Allocation Steps - Step 4: Smart Burn And Standard Activity Staking Rewards - Smart Burn Buffer - Usage Of Smart Burn Buffer](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d738057b8e3ce6e5f343cf8).   
      
6. Critical Core GovOps functions related to the operationalization of Sky Primitives are executed, including payment/reimbursement processing, compliance monitoring, and the calculation and application of retroactive penalties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d8f2ff0-8d73-8017-baaf-f2d9ce1ad87f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

#### A.2.5.1.2 - Implementation

The documents herein define the initial implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle. This initial implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle does not include the Senior Risk Capital System, which is still being developed, and funds continue to be allocated through Executive Votes rather than the allocation steps of the Treasury Management Function. See [A.2.5 - Treasury Management - Treasury Management - Short Term Transitionary Measures](https://www.notion.so/1d8f2ff08d73802398b2ca0f05f6a435).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8057-ba83-fd73f9daea16
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

##### A.2.5.1.2.1 - Process Definition

The documents herein define the initial process for performing the Monthly Settlement Cycle. The calculations to be performed as part of the Monthly Settlement Cycle are defined in [A.2.6 - Implementation Stages](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d73805c89f4c9f5b6c9fa2d). This process definition specifies who performs the calculations and how funds are ultimately paid out.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8077-8db0-dd3f5558993f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.1 - Initial Calculation By Operational Executor Agent

Within five \(5\) calendar days of the end of each month, each Operational Executor Agent posts on the Sky Forum a calculation of the net amounts due to or from the Prime Agents that it has an Executor Accord with \(the “Initial Calculation”\). The Initial Calculation must be posted under the “Sky Core” category using the `monthly-settlement-cycle` tag and must contain reasonable supporting detail. The required contents of the Initial Calculation will be further specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-80b8-bb59-d978c9a5bc6e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.1.1 - Demand Side Stablecoin Primitive Recipients

For each Prime Agent, the Initial Calculation must specify whether payments with respect to Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives \(see [A.2.4 - A8 - Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73801491cde129bd70eafd)\) have already been made by the Operational Executor Agent, and thus should be reimbursed to the Operational Executor Agent instead of being paid to the Prime Agent.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8084-977e-e902b801572d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.2 - Independent Calculation By BA Labs On Behalf Of Core Council

Within five \(5\) calendar days of the posting of each Initial Calculation, BA Labs, on behalf of the Core Council, replies to the Initial Calculation with their own independent calculation of the net amounts due to or from the Prime Agents covered by the Initial Calculation \(the “Independent Calculation”\). The Independent Calculation must specify whether each amount is an Agreed Amount or a Disputed Amount \(see [A.2.6 - Allowed Deviation](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380eebd75d2ae7598b49d)\) and contain reasonable supporting detail. The required contents of the Independent Calculation will be further specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8067-a24e-da64873601a9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.2.1 - Agreed Amount

The net amount due to or from a Prime Agent in an Initial Calculation is an Agreed Amount if the absolute value of the percentage difference between the amounts in the Initial Calculation and the Independent Calculation is less than or equal to the Allowed Deviation \(see [A.2.6 - Allowed Deviation](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380eebd75d2ae7598b49d)\). The net amount due to or from a Prime Agent in an Independent Calculation is also an Agreed Amount if there is no Initial Calculation \(see [A.2.6 - Missing Initial Calculations](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380d9a043c50fda67307e)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8021-8198-f99e7b13b74d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.2.2 - Disputed Amount

The net amount due to or from a Prime Agent in an Initial Calculation is a Disputed Amount if it is not an Agreed Amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-802a-b4b6-de51b5dc217f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.2.3 - Allowed Deviation

The Allowed Deviation is the maximum allowed deviation, on a percentage basis, between the net amount due to or from a Prime Agent in the Initial Calculation and Independent Calculation before resolution of these differences is required. It recognizes that if the Initial Calculation and Independent Calculation are truly developed independently then differences in methodology are likely to result in small deviations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-80ee-bd75-d2ae7598b49d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.1.2.3.1 - Allowed Deviation Current Value

The current value of the Allowed Deviation is 1%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-800d-aee1-c9f7a017193c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.2.4 - Missing Initial Calculations

If an Operational Executor Agent does not deliver the Initial Calculation as specified in [A.2.6 - Initial Calculation By Operational Executor Agent](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380b8bb59d978c9a5bc6e), BA Labs must prepare an Independent Calculation with respect to the Prime Agents covered by such Initial Calculation within ten \(10\) calendar days of the end of each month. The Independent Calculation must be posted under the “Sky Core” category using the `monthly-settlement-cycle` tag, contain reasonable supporting detail, and specify that no Initial Calculation was posted by the Operational Executor Agent.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-80d9-a043-c50fda67307e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.3 - Resolution Of Differences By Core GovOps Atlas Axis

If there are any Disputed Amounts \(see [A.2.6 - Disputed Amount](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d73802ab4b6de51b5dc217f)\), then Core GovOps Atlas Axis must resolve them. Atlas Axis must work with the relevant Operational Executor Agent, relevant Prime Agents, and BA Labs to reach a mutually acceptable resolution and may consult other parties to the extent it deems advisable.  
  
Core GovOps Atlas Axis makes the final decision in resolving differences between the Initial Calculation and the Independent Calculation \(the “Final Calculation”\). Atlas Axis must post the Final Calculation as a reply to the Independent Calculation within five \(5\) calendar days of the posting of an Independent Calculation that includes a Disputed Amount. The Final Calculation must specify the Final Amount and contain reasonable supporting detail. The required contents of the Final Calculation will be further specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8043-850b-f201cc193912
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.3.1 - Final Amount

The net amount due to or from a Prime Agent in a Final Calculation is a Final Amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8051-b7eb-f464a86f84d5
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.3.2 - Disputes By Prime Agents

A Prime Agent may dispute the calculation of the net amount due to or from it \(a “Dispute Notice”\). The Dispute Notice must be posted as a reply to the Initial Calculation within five \(5\) calendar days of the posting of the Initial Calculation. The Dispute Notice must specify the errors in the Initial Calculation and the Prime’s own calculation of the net amount due to or from it. The required contents of the Dispute Notice will be further specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.  
  
Core GovOps Atlas Axis may at its discretion resolve the differences between the calculations using the process specified in [A.2.6 - Resolution Of Differences By Core GovOps Atlas Axis](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d738043850bf201cc193912). Alternatively, Atlas Axis may elect to determine the correct amount and true up any differences in the next Monthly Settlement Cycle as specified in [A.2.6 - True Up In Subsequent Monthly Settlement Cycle](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d73803ca750ddccb077b481). In either case Atlas Axis must post its decision as a reply to the Dispute Notice.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8058-8398-e899ec61f14e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.4 - Settlement Through Sky Core Executive Vote

When all amounts due to or from Prime Agents are Agreed Amounts or Final Amounts then the Core Facilitator must include payments of these amounts in the next Sky Core Executive Vote as specified herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8098-a552-c4a74283840a
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.4.1 - Payment Of Amounts Due To Prime Agents

Amounts due to Prime Agents, excluding reimbursements made to Operational Executor Agents \(see [A.2.6 - Reimbursement Of Payments Made By Operational Executor Agents](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380b68592cbf597fe5c71)\), are transferred from the Sky Surplus Buffer to the Prime SubProxy Account through an Executive Vote.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-807e-a6c6-d64e97d8900e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.4.2 - Collection Of Amounts Due From Prime Agents

Amounts due from Prime Agents, excluding reimbursements made to Operational Executor Agents \(see [A.2.6 - Reimbursement Of Payments Made By Operational Executor Agents](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d7380b68592cbf597fe5c71)\) are settled in a way that is equivalent to a transfer from the Prime to the Sky Surplus Buffer through actions included in an Executive Vote.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-806e-a452-f803cb0fd288
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.4.3 - Reimbursement Of Payments Made By Operational Executor Agents

Reimbursements of payments already made by the Operational Executor Agent with respect to Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives \(see [A.2.4 - A8 - Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73801491cde129bd70eafd)\) are transferred to the Operational Executor Agent’s Buffer through an Executive Vote.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-80b6-8592-cbf597fe5c71
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.1.4.3.1 - Reimbursement To Accessibility Reward Wallet And Integration Boost Wallets

On an interim basis, Accessibility Reward reimbursements may also be made to the Accessibility Reward Controller Wallet \(see [A.2.4 - Near-Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73802292a2ceb9e4827fb9)\) at the request of an Operational Executor Agent. Similarly, Integration Boost reimbursements may also be made to the Integration Boost Wallet \(see [A.2.4 - Near Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380068d52f441b4e85f5b)\) at the request of an Operational Executor Agent.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-805e-bda0-d1550a3f445a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.5 - True Up In Subsequent Monthly Settlement Cycle

If the amounts of any payments included in the Sky Core Executive Vote \(see A.2.6 - Settlement Through Sky Core Executive Vote\) are subsequently determined to be incorrect, adjustments are included in the next Monthly Settlement Cycle to ensure that each party pays the amount it owes or receives the amount to which it is entitled.  
  
Core GovOps Atlas Axis must prepare a statement specifying the errors, the correct amounts, and the adjustments needed \(the “Correction Calculation”\). The Correction Calculation must be posted to the Sky Forum under the “Sky Core” category with the `monthly-settlement-cycle` tag. The required contents of the Correction Calculation will be further specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-803c-a750-ddccb077b481
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.1.6 - Interim Measures

The documents herein define interim exceptions to the process definition for the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8056-88b7-c7d3e4a31626
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.6.1 - Scope Of September 2025 Monthly Settlement Cycle

The initial Monthly Settlement Cycle conducted in September 2025 will be for the two month period from July 1, 2025 to August 31, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-802c-be6c-c4eda544821d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-16

####### A.2.5.1.2.1.6.2 - Process For September 2025 Monthly Settlement Cycle

For the initial Monthly Settlement Cycle conducted in September 2025, the Initial Calculation prepared by Operational Executor Agent Amatsu will only include calculations related to Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives \(see [A.2.4 - A8 - Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73801491cde129bd70eafd)\). The Independent Calculation prepared by BA Labs on behalf of the Core Council will be prepared normally. The calculations related to Demand Side Stablecoin Primitives will be subject to the normal resolution process defined in [A.2.6 - Resolution Of Differences By Core GovOps Atlas Axis](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d738043850bf201cc193912). For all other calculations, the amounts in the Independent Calculation will be treated as Agreed Amounts. The Initial Calculation and the Independent Calculation will be posted to the Sky Forum by September 10, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8039-b5b2-ca0d6c03a2df
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

##### A.2.5.1.2.2 - Implementation Stages

The initial implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle will occur in three stages as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-805c-89f4-c9f5b6c9fa2d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

###### A.2.5.1.2.2.1 - Stage 1

The documents herein define Stage 1 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8014-b124-e76f5f5c91fc
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1 - Stage 1 Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation

In Stage 1 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle, the net amount due from each Prime to Sky will be calculated using a Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8034-b77a-eba485c363ec
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.1 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Demand Side Primitives And Agent Rate

The amount due from Sky to each Prime with respect to Demand Side Primitives and the Agent Rate is calculated as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8014-a783-c899c252313b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.1.1 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Accessibility Reward

The amount due from Sky to a Prime in a month with respect to the Accessibility Reward is the amount earned by the Prime with respect to all USDS and sUSDS balances marked with the Prime’s reward codes. See [A.2.4 - Accessibility Reward Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380b4873cec532edc750c). This includes any Bonus Accessibility Reward. See [A.2.10 - 2025 Bonus](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738095acd7f80dc1d457e4).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80c8-bff7-c5d5f1a820f2
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.1.2 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Agent Rate

The amount due from Sky to a Prime in a month with respect to the Agent Rate is calculated as specified in [A.3.2 - Core Stability Parameters - Parameters - Agent Rate](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73802bb77bdbba137b55f3).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80e2-ba7a-cc61d7ff7f95
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.1.3 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Accessibility Reward And Agent Rate

The amount due from Sky to a Prime in a month with respect to the Accessibility Reward and the Agent Rate is the sum of the amounts specified in [A.2.6 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Accessibility Reward](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380c8bff7c5d5f1a820f2) and [A.2.6 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Agent Rate](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380e2ba7acc61d7ff7f95).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8031-a18e-e83cb5efb139
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2 - Amount Due From Prime To Sky With Respect To Supply Side Primitives

The amount due from each Prime to Sky with respect to Supply Side Primitives is calculated as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8071-85d4-e131da1af12c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.1 - Step 1: Calculate Total Allocation System Revenue

First, Total Allocation System Revenue is calculated for each Prime for the month. Total Allocation System Revenue for a Prime in a month is the sum of Instance Revenue for each Active Instance of the Allocation System Primitive that the Prime has deployed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80ec-aa44-ddb62e38fbfb
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.1.1 - Instance Revenue

Instance Revenue for an Allocation System Instance for a month is all earnings from that Instance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-803f-8251-d60582cbe211
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.2 - Step 2: Calculate Total Allocation System Profit

Second, Total Allocation System Profit is calculated for each Prime for the month. Total Allocation System Profit for a Prime in a month is the sum of Instance Profit for each Active Instance of the Allocation System Primitive that the Prime has deployed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-800b-ad0f-edc7fb4a226b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.2.1 - Instance Profit

Instance Profit for an Allocation System Instance is equal to the greater of \(1\) Instance Revenue \(see [A.2.6 - Instance Revenue](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d73803f8251d60582cbe211)\) minus Instance Expenses \(see [A.2.6 - Instance Expense](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380bb9b9fe9e23b243ce1)\) and \(2\) zero.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8038-a411-d3ea9116cf81
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

########### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.2.1.1 - Instance Expense

Instance Expense for an Allocation System Instance for a month is the interest expense on the funds from Sky’s Collateral Portfolio invested in the Instance. It is calculated on a per block basis based on the amount invested from Sky’s Collateral Portfolio and the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80bb-9b9f-e9e23b243ce1
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.3 - Step 3: Calculate Adjusted Allocation System Profit

Third, Adjusted Allocation System Profit is calculated for each Prime for the month. Adjusted Allocation System Profit for a Prime in a month is Total Allocation System Profit minus the Distortion Penalty \(see [A.2.6 - Distortion Penalty](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d738032b7def5de2a02b778)\) and the Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Penalty \(see [A.2.6 - Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Penalty](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380a898e6fcc1cc0dc8a7)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80fc-8091-d811acaa6281
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.3.1 - Distortion Penalty

The Distortion Penalty is a discretionary penalty designed to mitigate the potential misalignment caused by the asymmetric nature of the Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation, where Primes earn profits on Allocation System Instances that earn more than the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate but do not suffer losses when Instances earn less than the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate.  
  
The Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors, may assess a penalty of up to 100% of Total Allocation System Profit \(see [A.2.6 - Step 2: Calculate Total Allocation System Profit](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d73800bad0fedc7fb4a226b)\) if it determines that a Prime’s decisions were distorted by this incentive structure.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8032-b7de-f5de2a02b778
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

########## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.3.2 - Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Penalty

The Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Penalty is a formulaic penalty designed to prevent Primes from holding excessive amounts of low yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral.  
  
The penalty, as a percentage of the Total Allocation System Profit, is the lesser of \(1\) ten times the Excess Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Percentage and \(2\) 100%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80a8-98e6-fcc1cc0dc8a7
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.3.2.1 - Excess Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Percentage

The Excess Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Percentage for a Prime is equal to the excess of \(1\) its Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral \(see [A.2.6 - Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral Penalty](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380a898e6fcc1cc0dc8a7)\) as a percentage of its Collateral Portfolio above \(2\) its Minimum Actively Stabilizing Collateral \(see [A.3.4 - Minimum Actively Stabilizing Collateral](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73805494b1f3072a3086e3)\) as a percentage of its Collateral Portfolio.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80d1-99c4-de844a13622a
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

############ A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.3.2.1.1 - Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral

Low Yield Actively Stabilizing Collateral is Actively Stabilizing Collateral \(see [A.3.4 - Resting Actively Stabilizing Collateral](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d7380c9aeebf437ebcbf04d)\) that earns less than the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8040-8a03-ccbfed9c9e53
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.2.4 - Step 4: Calculate Amount Due To Sky With Respect To Supply Side Primitives

Fourth, the amount due to Sky with respect to Supply Side Primitives is calculated for each Prime for the month. The amount due to Sky with respect to Supply Side Primitives is Total Allocation System Revenue \(see [A.2.6 - Step 1: Calculate Total Allocation System Revenue](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380ecaa44ddb62e38fbfb)\) minus Adjusted Allocation System Profit \(see [A.2.6 - Step 3: Calculate Adjusted Allocation System Profit](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d7380fc8091d811acaa6281)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80d5-a5ca-d33e21ea4bfe
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.3 - Settlement

The amounts due from Sky to each Prime with respect to Demand Side Primitives and the Agent Rate \(see [A.2.6 - Amount Due From Sky To Primes With Respect To Demand Side Primitives And Agent Rate](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d738014a783c899c252313b)\) and the amounts due from each Prime to Sky with respect to Supply Side Primitives \(see [A.2.6 - Amount Due From Prime To Sky With Respect To Supply Side Primitives](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d73807185d4e131da1af12c)\) are settled as specified in [A.2.6 - Settlement Through Sky Core Executive Vote](https://www.notion.so/26bf2ff08d738098a552c4a74283840a).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8008-929c-ec648127aeba
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.4 - Exceptions

The documents herein define exceptions to the Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation for specific assets held by certain Prime Agents.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-8077-a656-d0e236482705
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.4.1 - USDT Held By Spark

Spark pays the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate with respect to funds borrowed by Spark to invest in USDT and held in SparkLend. Spark thus realizes the full profit and loss associated with these investments.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-800c-b218-e4c0783a8ce4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.1.4.2 - pyUSD Held By Spark

Spark pays the Agent Credit Line Borrow Rate with respect to funds borrowed by Spark to invest in pyUSD and held in SparkLend or Spark’s ALM Proxy. Spark thus realizes the full profit and loss associated with these investments.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 26bf2ff0-8d73-80e1-bdd6-c987ddff8957
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.2 - Stage 1 Timing

Stage 1 is currently expected to be implemented in the August 21, 2025 Executive Vote for the period from July 1, 2025 to July 31, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8078-926b-cfee8052f0f4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.1.3 - Stage 1 Actions

The actions specified herein must be completed to achieve Stage 1 implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-809c-a230-c399c154ae28
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.3.1 - Reduction Of Prime Allocator Vault Stability Fees

The Stability Fees for each Prime Allocator Vault must be reduced to zero so that value is transferred from Primes to Sky Core exclusively through Executive Votes. The Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors, is directed to use the Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module to reduce the Stability Fee for each Prime Allocator Vault to zero. See [A.3.9 - Measures For Endgame Transition - Stability Parameter Bounded External Access Module - Allocator Vault Parameters](https://www.notion.so/1d9f2ff08d7380f9b10ed60fda02bb37).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80f4-8a2c-ff8c783898d2
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.1.3.2 - Automation Of Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation

The Stability Scope Advisors must develop tooling to allow the net amount due from each Prime to Sky each month under the Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation to be computed automatically.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-803e-a5fd-c40ae93ac9ec
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.5.1.2.2.2 - Stage 2

The documents herein define Stage 2 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80fa-b868-f4d4cae6ba35
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.2.1 - Stage 2 Virtual Base Rate

In Stage 2 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle, the net amount due from each Prime to Sky is calculated as specified in the Atlas without the use of the Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation. However, interest under the Base Rate does not accrue in real time onchain and is instead calculated offchain and paid through Executive Votes as part of the Monthly Settlement Cycle. This is known as the Virtual Base Rate as these calculations occur offchain.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8064-83de-d59ec69e292a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.2.2 - Stage 2 Timing

The timing for Stage 2 implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8085-a0ca-c18b6479b191
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.2.3 - Stage 2 Actions

The actions specified herein must be completed to achieve Stage 2 implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8026-97b4-c6f983bc655a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.2.3.1 - Automation Of Monthly Settlement Cycle Calculation Including Virtual Base Rate

The Stability Scope Advisors must develop automated tooling for calculating the net amount due from each Prime to Sky each month, incorporating the Virtual Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-8018-90d5-e1ed6981472e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.5.1.2.2.3 - Stage 3

The documents herein define Stage 3 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80a4-baae-c3c6187b0990
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.3.1 - Stage 3 Onchain Base Rate

In Stage 3 of the implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle, the net amount due from each Prime to Sky is calculated as specified in the Atlas without the use of the Simplified Profit And Loss Calculation. In Stage 3, interest from the Base Rate accrues onchain to the Allocator Vault of each Prime. Thus, in Stage 3 Executive Votes are only required to pay down accrued interest on Allocator Vaults, as necessary, and settle other amounts due to and from Primes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-800d-92c1-d7e13db563ff
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.3.2 - Stage 3 Timing

The timing for Stage 3 implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle will be specified in a future iteration of the Atlas.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-809a-8390-fa7aeb801a24
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

####### A.2.5.1.2.2.3.3 - Stage 3 Actions

The actions specified herein must be completed to achieve Stage 3 implementation of the Monthly Settlement Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-801a-9c66-ed553e38fa2d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.3.3.1 - Integration Of Base Rate Into Allocator Vaults

Allocator Vaults must be updated to accrue interest in real time based on the Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80c8-b965-d561bc16294c
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-31

######## A.2.5.1.2.2.3.3.2 - Automation Of Monthly Settlement Cycle Based On Base Rate

The Stability Scope Advisors must update the tooling specified in [A.2.6 - Automation Of Monthly Settlement Cycle Calculation Including Virtual Base Rate](https://www.notion.so/241f2ff08d73801890d5e1ed6981472e) to reflect the onchain accrual of the Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80c1-bd05-c635a73aad97
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-31

## A.2.6 - Agent Incubation

This Article governs the incubation of Agents. It defines the necessary infrastructure and processes to ensure Agents are maximally supported to generate as much value as possible for the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 4a2804d9-e510-49e6-a73c-f4b372e2df48
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

### A.2.6.1 - Support For Agents

This Section defines elements and infrastructure to support Agents.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 3405ea90-3344-4eba-beed-8d4323cc0e58
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

## A.2.7 - Ecosystem Actor Incubation

This Article governs the incubation of new Ecosystem Actors to support the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 77843b48-1d30-4499-ac49-46cd921da76c
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

### A.2.7.1 - Ecosystem Actor Incubation

The Support Scope is responsible for incubating Ecosystem Actors. This Section defines the elements to support this objective.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** c3da7ceb-ecb5-4b09-a7b6-0748edf704af
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.7.1.1 - Incubating Ecosystem Actors

Incubating Ecosystem Actors are Ecosystem Actors that are being developed to support the Sky Protocol or its current and future Agents. Sky Governance can assign these actors projects that benefit the Sky Protocol or incubating Agents, such as solutions for branding, marketing, user acquisition; referral marketing and revenue share systems; smart contract development; and protocol development.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 7e1c77ba-4c4e-43a6-90c2-6e2285c5af76
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.7.1.2 - Budgets

The Support Facilitators have budgets available to support the tasks described in this Section, including the Incubation Overhead budget and the Incubation budget.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 68d7da04-9954-4800-8ef4-ae07dbc10fd7
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.7.1.4 - Currently Incubating Ecosystem Actors Template

The list of Incubating Ecosystem Actors must follow this template for each recorded Incubating Ecosystem Actor:  
  
• **.x:** \[Incubating Ecosystem Actor name and short description\]  
  
• **.1:** \[Budget information\]  
  
• **.2:** \[Deliverables and focus areas\]  
  
• **.3:** \[Team information, including headcount grouped by skill sets\]

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** a57fabfd-f4c1-4749-afd7-52033eb408df
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.7.1.3 - Currently Incubating Ecosystem Actors

Incubating Ecosystem Actors and their current key terms and data are defined as Active Data in [A.2.8 - Currently Incubating Ecosystem Actors](https://www.notion.so/4e9bdb49705643af86d9bdca1d440ad9).   
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
  
• The Responsible Party is the Support Facilitators.  
  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 7019dc3d-040a-49a5-95ed-e5ad92693b2d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-08

## A.2.8 - Ecosystem Communication Channels

This Article regulates the unified communication infrastructure for governance ecosystem communication. This infrastructure must include channels for inter-Agent and Ecosystem Actor interaction.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 9814c083-b7df-452d-821a-8c5f3d2275ba
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

### A.2.8.1 - Ecosystem Communication Channels

The Support Scope maintains the overall unified communication infrastructure used for governance ecosystem communication. This Section defines key elements and infrastructure supporting this objective.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 42050fbc-e6a1-4a3f-94b8-ac500ff5f69c
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

#### A.2.8.1.1 - Communications Infrastructure

The Core Facilitator is tasked with maintaining an ecosystem-wide communications infrastructure to enable Agent participants and Ecosystem Actors to interact with each other and discuss the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 6861c09f-cfc3-4a62-a189-d749e483820e
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

##### A.2.8.1.1.1 - Forum

The communications infrastructure must include an ecosystem Forum devoted to discussions on Agent-related business proposals, partnerships and interactions, as well as casual conversation for the broader Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** ff459a54-8442-46ad-9b83-4c6e15e8cda2
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.8.1.1.2 - Chatroom

The communications infrastructure must include a chatroom for broad discussion related to Agents, Ecosystem Actors and Sky. As an initial bootstrapping measure, the Core Facilitator can use Discord.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d6fc568f-c320-4bcd-8a48-a12ebf7a316b
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

#### A.2.8.1.2 - Moderation

The documents herein define the moderation policies and responsible moderators for Sky's communication channels.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f8b22570-4264-4fda-a793-23a1e3661da1
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

##### A.2.8.1.2.1 - Moderation Policies

The documents herein define the moderation policies applicable to Ecosystem Communication Channels.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-809f-852e-ccd6b9d55569
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

###### A.2.8.1.2.1.1 - General Requirements

This document defines the requirements generally applicable to all Ecosystem Communication Channels.  
  
Responsible moderators are granted the prerogative to ban users from the communication channels for which they are responsible when they deem users to be acting in a misaligned fashion. Misalignment can include maliciously exploiting the communication channel or disrupting its efficient functioning through improper use or antisocial behavior.  
  
However, unless egregiously misaligned or disrupting behavior takes place, responsible moderators should err on the side of charity and issue a public warning for users who engage in bad behavior before resorting to banning them. Egregiously misaligned or disrupting behavior includes spamming, flooding, threatening, or using inappropriate or abusive language.  
  
Only the Core Facilitator may ban a user from the Sky Forum when doing so would operationally block an ongoing governance process \(e.g. the user has submitted an otherwise eligible governance proposal\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8084-befd-e759e89c25c4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.2.8.1.2.1.1.1 - Public Communication Of Bans

The documents herein define the requirements regarding public communication of bans. The Sky Forum is subject to special requirements that are defined in [A.2.9 - Ecosystem Communication Channels - Moderation - Sky Forum-Specific Requirements](https://www.notion.so/21ef2ff08d7380d2a1f8e690978e04d1). Requirements applicable to all other Ecosystem Communication Channels are defined in [A.2.9 - Ecosystem Communication Channels - Moderation - Requirements Applicable To All Other Channels](https://www.notion.so/21ef2ff08d738021938ccd72b8573040).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8009-8994-f7af3b84a2b6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

######## A.2.8.1.2.1.1.1.1 - Sky Forum-Specific Requirements

For the Sky Forum, the Core Facilitator must publicly communicate the ban and the reasons for banning a user if the ban interrupts the users' exercise of a governance process.  
  
In all other cases involving the Sky Forum, responsible moderators may exercise their best judgment in determining whether to publicly communicate a ban and the reasons for a ban. In doing so, the responsible moderators should consider the importance of transparency and community awareness as well as whether the banned user has engaged in egregiously misaligned and disrupting behavior. In every case, the moderators must still use the built-in forum functionality to register the ban reason for every banned user so that all forum users can see it when visiting the forum profile of the banned user.  
  
If it is assessed that a public communication is necessary, the Core Facilitator is responsible for writing and publishing it.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-80d2-a1f8-e690978e04d1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######## A.2.8.1.2.1.1.1.2 - Requirements Applicable To All Other Channels

For all Ecosystem Communication Channels other than the Sky Forum, the responsible moderators may exercise their discretion in determining whether to publicly communicate a ban and the reasons for the ban.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8021-938c-cd72b8573040
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

####### A.2.8.1.2.1.1.2 - Use Of Automated Tools

Responsible moderators may use automated tools, including AI and bots, to help fulfill their moderation responsibilities. The responsible moderators retain responsibility for ensuring that these tools conform to moderation policies and must establish a process through which automated decisions can be reviewed by the responsible moderators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8024-9e21-c94847477e42
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

####### A.2.8.1.2.1.1.3 - Unbanning

Bannings are by default permanent. However, Ranked Delegates can propose the unbanning of users by publishing a forum post that states the handle\(s\) of the user\(s\) whose unbanning they request and the rationale for their unbanning. The forum post must tag the Core Facilitator, who will in turn prepare a binary Weekly Cycle Governance Poll to be published on the Voting Portal as soon as reasonably possible. The outcome of the Governance Poll is binding.  
  
Where identity can be proven, the ban on unbanned users will be lifted across all communication channels.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8033-b4f1-e9c78c40ef27
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.2.8.1.2.1.1.4 - Intrinsic Limitations

Given the digital nature of these communication channels and the consequent practical unenforceability of permanent bannings, responsible moderators are expected to have to deal with recurring banned users under new aliases. Responsible moderators must exercise best judgement when suspecting a user to be a banned user under a different guise and when assessing whether they pose a risk of recidivism.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-80c3-8178-cabbece4e996
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

##### A.2.8.1.2.2 - List of Responsible Moderators

The responsible moderators for each communication channel are:  
  
• Sky Forum - The Core Facilitator  
  
• Sky and Sky Builder Discord - The Core Facilitator and Ecosystem Actor TechOps Services  
  
• Sky X / Twitter Account - Ecosystem Actor Maker Growth  
  
• MakerDAO Subreddit - Ecosystem Actor Maker Growth

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-80e8-82b3-c9a038125379
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-03

#### A.2.8.1.3 - Communications Infrastructure Budget

The ecosystem communication infrastructure budget is 0 USDS per quarter.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8025-abb8-cb3450462359
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

## A.2.9 - Ecosystem Accords

This Article governs Ecosystem Accords, agreements between actors in the Sky Ecosystem that are enforceable by Sky Governance.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 522d3ea5-3842-4434-979a-f349d08f8009
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

### A.2.9.1 - Dispute Resolution

This Section defines the process for resolving disputes regarding Ecosystem Accords.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80af-9a73-c538cac436f1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

#### A.2.9.1.1 - Dispute Resolution By Core Council

Disputes regarding Ecosystem Accords are resolved by the Core Council as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80b9-9e24-e5a9da3d32c5
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

##### A.2.9.1.1.1 - Roles

The documents herein define the respective roles of Core GovOps and the Core Facilitator in resolving disputes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80e5-9115-d59ea5f330f9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

###### A.2.9.1.1.1.1 - Role Of Core GovOps

Core GovOps manages the overall dispute resolution process, including establishing communication channels for dispute resolution, communicating with the parties, and gathering and analyzing information relating to the dispute.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-806a-a8c9-d3045431f98c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

###### A.2.9.1.1.1.2 - Role Of Core Facilitator

The Core Facilitator decides how the dispute is ultimately resolved. The Core Facilitator is isolated from the parties to maintain impartiality. The Core Facilitator exclusively reviews the information prepared by Core GovOps and delivers a decision to Core GovOps, which communicates it to the parties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8002-a7c5-eca1255f1db2
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

##### A.2.9.1.1.2 - Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for resolving disputes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80b5-bab6-ec1035acad7f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

###### A.2.9.1.1.2.1 - Dispute Intake

The documents herein define the process for dispute intake for dispute resolution.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8008-abc5-f1282801c986
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.1.1 - Formal Request For Dispute Resolution

The dispute resolution process begins with an actor in the Sky Ecosystem requesting formal dispute resolution from Core GovOps.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-806d-8492-cd1f76c7adce
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.1.2 - Preliminary Determination Of Reasonableness By Core GovOps

After receiving the request for dispute resolution, Core GovOps makes a brief preliminary determination regarding whether the request is reasonable \(i.e. not vexatious or frivolous\), within three \(3\) working days.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8015-a21d-d4bf88345ac6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.1.3 - Handling Of Unreasonable Requests

If Core GovOps determines that the request for dispute resolution is not reasonable, it will inform the actor who submitted the request accordingly, providing their reasons. In this case, there is no further action.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-809c-aa25-cf0e31bd89c7
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.1.4 - Handling Of Reasonable Requests

If Core GovOps makes a determination that the request is reasonable, then Core GovOps formally begins the dispute resolution process by notifying all relevant parties that the process is about to begin. The notifications to each party will outline the process to be followed, including relevant steps and timeframes that the party should be aware of.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80f8-86bb-cc9365305e22
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

###### A.2.9.1.1.2.2 - Presentation Of Arguments

The documents herein define the process for the presentation of arguments for dispute resolution.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80a3-8df0-d404d28341a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.2.1 - Statement Of Problem

The party that initiated the dispute resolution process has five \(5\) working days from the formal initiation of the process to submit a Statement of Problem, laying out the facts they consider relevant to the dispute and the issues related to the Atlas as they see them.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-803b-b71b-e08d7f4b5ed8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.2.2 - Statement Of Response

Core GovOps provides the Statement of Problem to the other party/parties. They have five \(5\) working days to submit a Statement of Response, which sets out their perspective on the relevant facts and issues in the dispute.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80b3-a09b-f6664138fdeb
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.2.3 - Statement Of Rebuttal

Core GovOps provides the Statement of Response to the party that initiated the dispute resolution process. They have three \(3\) working days to submit a Statement of Rebuttal.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-80e7-ac10-feec4a5ea71a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

###### A.2.9.1.1.2.3 - Decision

The documents herein define the decision process for dispute resolution.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8023-9880-cd53df1441ff
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.3.1 - Analysis By Core GovOps

Core GovOps prepares a confidential analysis of the dispute for the Core Facilitator. This analysis must include a summary of all relevant facts as far as it is possible to ascertain, clear identification of any areas of factual ambiguity or disagreement between the parties, a structured presentation of the arguments in favor of each party, and a recommendation if Core GovOps believes one conclusion is clearly supported by the facts.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-805b-95c5-dff7cccd31d2
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.3.2 - Adjudication By Core Facilitator

The Core Facilitator reviews the analysis prepared by Core GovOps along with the arguments presented by the parties and reaches a decision within three \(3\) working days.  
  
The Core Facilitator must draft a decision setting out relevant facts and the core reasoning of their decision. In doing so, the Core Facilitator is free to adopt arguments presented by the parties, by Core GovOps, or to advance their own analysis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-802d-80e7-f13532e9dc8c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.3.3 - Release Of Decision

The Core Facilitator communicates their decision to Core GovOps. Core GovOps communicates the decision to all related parties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8056-8ea3-f916c2c5a7b4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

####### A.2.9.1.1.2.3.4 - Publication Of Decision

The Core Facilitator publishes the decision on the Sky Forum and updates the [A.2.10 - Dispute Resolutions](https://www.notion.so/279f2ff08d738011a8fec6e2e0fd9d32) Active Data document in the Atlas, redacting any confidential information as needed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8053-aaf0-fabf14a5bfdd
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

#### A.2.9.1.2 - Dispute Resolution Recording

Resolutions of disputes involving Ecosystem Accords are defined as Active Data in [A.2.10 - Dispute Resolutions](https://www.notion.so/279f2ff08d738011a8fec6e2e0fd9d32).  
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:      
◦ The Responsible Party is the Core Facilitator.      
◦ The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-806c-bf3c-c2e319ffa272
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

##### A.2.9.1.2.0.6.1 - Dispute Resolutions

The resolutions of disputes regarding Ecosystem Accords are:  
  
• **Dispute Between Spark And Grove Regarding Effective Date Of Their Ecosystem Accord** \(September 2, 2025\) - [Facilitator Decision on Grove/Spark Dispute](https://forum.sky.money/t/facilitator-decision-on-grove-spark-dispute/27141)

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** 279f2ff0-8d73-8011-a8fe-c6e2e0fd9d32
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-25

### A.2.9.2 - Active Ecosystem Accords

The subdocuments herein record currently active Ecosystem Accords.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 8d42fcd0-0d4e-4fef-ae1a-f1bfc6132582
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-26

#### A.2.9.2.1 - Ecosystem Accord 1: Grove And Spark Agents

The subdocuments herein record the terms of agreement between Grove and Spark as agreed in Ecosystem Accord 1.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80f6-ac3e-c2b07fa8d098
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

##### A.2.9.2.1.1 - Accord Key Details

The subdocuments herein set out the key details of Ecosystem Accord 1, such as parties to the agreement and the duration of the Accord.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8039-a829-c94c1af13999
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

###### A.2.9.2.1.1.1 - Parties To The Accord

The Grove and Spark Agents are the parties to Ecosystem Accord 1. These parties are also referred to as Grove and Spark, respectively, in the terms of this Accord.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8032-95f7-c783a587a9e0
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.1.2 - Duration Of The Accord

The duration of Ecosystem Accord 1 is six \(6\) months, commencing from May 29, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-802d-a03f-cf6b26be9d21
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

##### A.2.9.2.1.2 - Accord Substantive Terms

The subdocuments herein set out the substantive terms of Ecosystem Accord 1.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8070-bfd9-f68472c3de0c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.1 - Revenue Share

Grove and Spark agree to a bilateral revenue share, up to 40% maximum with JRC rental, see [A.2.4 - Junior Risk Capital Rental Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d7380eeba94e1f7b69e1f36).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8093-915c-ebbea3306a9e
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.1.1 - Revenue Share Definition

Each Agent retains the right to force the other Prime Agent to do revenue sharing up to 40% by providing that Prime with rented JRC. Sharing is done on the net revenue after deducting the cost to Sky. This is only considered on the spread earned on USDS debt, including any subsidy amounts.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80fe-a50d-fd4e1323bf15
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.2 - Grove Agent Exclusivity

Grove exclusivity includes Real World Assets \(RWAs\), which exclusivity does not extend to Treasuries used for ASC purposes or stablecoin liquidity provisioning.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8084-b8f3-fe7304b0b3e0
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.3 - Spark Agent Exclusivity

Spark Agent exclusivity includes Maple, cryptocurrency OTC lending, and Established DeFi Lending Protocols, as defined in [A.2.10 - Established DeFi Lending Protocol Definition](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380108863e3ffab8bc1c0).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8087-bd6d-e8ca2e61eeb3
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.3.1 - Established DeFi Lending Protocol Definition

Established DeFi Lending Protocols are defined as DeFi lending protocols deployed six months ago or earlier.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8010-8863-e3ffab8bc1c0
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.4 - Right Of First Refusal

Grove provides the Spark Agent with a right of first refusal for DeFi opportunities on unclaimed chains and new protocols, including bootstrapping incentive programs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8095-96e2-e4408504fec1
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.4.1 - DeFi Opportunities Right of First Refusal Duration

Grove provides the Spark Agent a right of first refusal for pursuing cross-chain bootstrapping opportunities, which lasts four weeks after notification by Grove. If Spark does not wish to deploy SparkLend or provide capital to the protocol in question, Grove retains the right to pursue the opportunity pending security and risk assessments of the target protocol. Upon written notification from Grove of a new DeFi opportunity via a communications channel mutually agreed by the parties, Spark has four weeks to decide whether it wants to deploy SparkLend or lend to the target chain and protocol. If Spark opts not to do so by the end of the four-week period, Grove is permitted to pursue the opportunity.  
  
_Example:__ A lending protocol \(i.e. Aave, Euler, Morpho, etc\) plans on deploying on blockchain X. Grove provides written notification to Spark about the potential new deployment. Spark evaluates the opportunity and decides there are better opportunities to pursue. The opportunity reverts to Grove after Spark’s refusal; or, if Spark does not issue a formal refusal, the opportunity reverts to Grove after four weeks._

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80ec-ac2d-c3e72700f1e1
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.4.2 - DeFi Opportunities Complementary Deployments

Grove and Spark may combine efforts if there is a large enough cross-chain opportunity. For example, Grove may deploy stablecoin liquidity on a target chain and Spark may deploy a SparkLend instance. In this case, complementary efforts are made to mutually benefit the cross-chain deployment, with significant economic benefits received by both parties. This chain would also serve as a new distribution channel for Spark, Grove, and Sky products.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-806f-89c9-e36f0871e646
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.5 - ASC Provision

Grove retains the right to deploy into repo lending protocols to meet ASC requirements. Deposits must be capped at the lower of either 10% of protocol USDC deposits or the Minimum Actively Stabilizing Collateral, as defined in [A.3.4 - Minimum Actively Stabilizing Collateral](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73805494b1f3072a3086e3). The Spark Agent retains the right to use the PSM for ASC, provided it covers the cost to Grove.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80e0-aba8-d23ee699afc7
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.6 - Treasuries Provision

The Spark Agent retains the right to deploy into tokenized US Treasury Bills to meet ASC requirements.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80e8-be72-cc80cbc2ab6d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.7 - Basis Trade Products

Basis trade products are available to both Grove and Spark.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80d2-94a4-cd6600f832ce
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.8 - Grand Prix Airdrops

Grove receives any airdrops from the Grand Prix.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8099-9d45-f309ba36111f
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.1.2.9 - USDe and sUSDe Revenue Share

Spark and Grove agree to share revenue from USDe and sUSDe as specified in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8097-ae3e-debe0520f76e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.9.1 - Revenue Share

Spark and Grove will share 50% of all revenue and expenses associated with Spark’s Allocation System investments in USDe and sUSDe. Revenue includes \(1\) protocol yield on the amount deployed into these assets, \(2\) any OTC or negotiated arrangements, payments, or incentives, and \(3\) manual payments or rebates related to withdrawal activity. Expenses include all costs associated with Spark’s investment in USDe and sUSDe, including mint / burn fees, swap fees, and costs of borrowing funds from Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8039-a678-ce5cefe826d0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.9.2 - Risk Capital Share

Grove agrees to rent to Spark 50% of the Required Risk Capital associated with Spark’s investments in USDe and sUSDe as a Junior Risk Capital Rental. See [A.2.4 - Junior Risk Capital Rental Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d7380eeba94e1f7b69e1f36).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80b5-bf8b-fcbc88a508a8
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

######## A.2.9.2.1.2.9.2.1 - Implementation

Spark and Grove expressly intend that Grove shares in 50% of the risks of Spark’s investment in USDe and sUSDe and not in the risk of any other investments by Spark.  
  
To the extent that USDe or sUSDe investments experience losses and Spark is allocated a greater than 50% share of those losses relative to Grove under the TIP JRC mechanism \(see [A.3.3 - Initial Loss Absorption By “Tip JRC”](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d738099bb42c9ab9a5f62b7)\), Grove will transfer funds to Spark so that Grove and Spark each bear 50% of the losses that occur.  
  
To the extent that Spark experiences losses on investments other than USDe or sUSDe and a portion of those losses is allocated to the Junior Risk Capital rented from Grove, Spark shall reimburse Grove for those losses.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-80f8-94fa-c244891053dd
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

####### A.2.9.2.1.2.9.3 - Term

This revenue share shall be effective retroactively, dated back to the launch of Spark’s allocation into USDe and sUSDe beginning the week of July 14, 2025.  
It shall remain in effect until one of the following conditions is met, whichever occurs first:  
      
1. Grove begins to allocate capital to USDe or sUSDe directly; or      
2. Spark or Grove terminates the revenue share, with reasonable advance notice to the other party.  
  
Any changes to the structure or scope of this arrangement will be discussed transparently and approved by both Spark and Grove.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 248f2ff0-8d73-8015-b934-d79176504150
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

#### A.2.9.2.2 - Prime Program

The subdocuments herein record the terms of agreement between Sky, Grove, and Spark as agreed in Ecosystem Accord 2.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8016-86e1-f559e3e0d598
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

**Document Type:** Core

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

The parties to Ecosystem Accord 2 are Sky, Spark, Grove, and Moonbow, as defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8028-815f-d5e5f1a61977
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.1.1.1 - Sky Details

The party ‘Sky’ comprises Sky Core.

**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-80b4-8720-d4118918b31c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.1.1.2 - Spark Details

The party ‘Spark’ comprises the Spark Prime Agent, Spark Foundation, and Phoenix Labs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-80f6-842c-dfb01750842f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.1.1.3 - Grove Details

The party ‘Grove’ comprises the Grove Prime Agent, and Grove Foundation.

**Document Type:** Core

**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.1.1.4 - Moonbow Details

The party ‘Moonbow’ is the entity owning relevant intellectual property.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8088-a79b-c6420d892c9e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

###### A.2.9.2.2.1.2 - Duration Of The Accord

The duration of Ecosystem Accord 2 is indefinite, commencing from May 29, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80ec-aded-fe8aa5159753
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

##### A.2.9.2.2.2 - Accord Substantive Terms

The subdocuments herein set out the substantive terms of Ecosystem Accord 2.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8022-856f-cddaec4391b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-19

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.1 - Tokenomics

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to tokenomics.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80d9-9534-c75a7c4602d4
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.1 - Total Token Supply

The total token supply of each of the SPK and GROVE tokens shall be 10,000,000,000 \(ten billion\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8067-bc65-c42d12a02560
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.2 - Token Allocations

Specific token allocations are defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80b7-a670-c390b18d3666
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.1.2.1 - Grove Prime Treasury

3,000,000,000 GROVE tokens are allocated to the Grove Prime Treasury \(Grove’s SubProxy\), with an option to further increase this allocation by 5% \(500,000,000 tokens\). The additional 5% will be distributed to relevant stakeholders / users from Sky’s allocation of GROVE tokens, but that distribution will happen from Grove’s SubProxy. This distribution plan must be approved by Sky before the transfer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8098-88b2-c3bd8e410ede
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.1.2.2 - Sky Retained Tokens And Reward Pools

Sky retains 7,000,000,000 GROVE tokens and 6,500,000,000 SPK tokens, distributing these as token rewards over a 10-year period according to the schedules outlined below.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8034-a229-ead169c098bb
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.2.2.1 - Grove Token Reward Distribution Schedule

Sky will distribute GROVE tokens annually, per the following schedule:  
  
| Year | Tokens for USDS Users | Tokens for Staking Users | Total           |  
|------|------------------------|---------------------------|-----------------|  
| 1    | 1,225,000,000          | 525,000,000               | 1,750,000,000   |  
| 2    | 1,225,000,000          | 525,000,000               | 1,750,000,000   |  
| 3    | 612,500,000            | 262,500,000               | 875,000,000     |  
| 4    | 612,500,000            | 262,500,000               | 875,000,000     |  
| 5    | 306,250,000            | 131,250,000               | 437,500,000     |  
| 6    | 306,250,000            | 131,250,000               | 437,500,000     |  
| 7    | 153,125,000            | 65,625,000                | 218,750,000     |  
| 8    | 153,125,000            | 65,625,000                | 218,750,000     |  
| 9    | 153,125,000            | 65,625,000                | 218,750,000     |  
| 10   | 153,125,000            | 65,625,000                | 218,750,000     |  
| Total| 4,900,000,000          | 2,100,000,000             | 7,000,000,000   |

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8091-a52d-e783438ca9bc
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.2.2.2 - Spark Token Reward Distribution Schedule

Sky will distribute SPK tokens annually, per the following schedule:  
  
| Year | Tokens for USDS Users | Tokens for Staking Users | Total           |  
|------|------------------------|---------------------------|-----------------|  
| 1    | 1,137,500,000          | 487,500,000               | 1,625,000,000   |  
| 2    | 1,137,500,000          | 487,500,000               | 1,625,000,000   |  
| 3    | 568,750,000            | 243,750,000               | 812,500,000     |  
| 4    | 568,750,000            | 243,750,000               | 812,500,000     |  
| 5    | 284,375,000            | 121,875,000               | 406,250,000     |  
| 6    | 284,375,000            | 121,875,000               | 406,250,000     |  
| 7    | 142,187,500            | 60,937,500                | 203,125,000     |  
| 8    | 142,187,500            | 60,937,500                | 203,125,000     |  
| 9    | 142,187,500            | 60,937,500                | 203,125,000     |  
| 10   | 142,187,500            | 60,937,500                | 203,125,000     |  
| Total| 4,550,000,000          | 1,950,000,000             | 6,500,000,000   |

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-809d-ac41-eb9753c05d41
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.3 - Transfer Limit

A maximum of 1,000,000,000 tokens can be transferred by Spark and Grove to their respective Foundations in the first year for the purpose of rewarding contributors, and 500,000,000 tokens per year thereafter for the purpose of rewarding contributors.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-807e-9833-eb1c27647573
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.4 - Prime Token Generation Event

The respective Prime Foundations for Spark and Grove, or one of their subsidiaries or affiliate companies, will conduct the token generation event. In the token generation event, the Agent tokens will be split and sent to the Prime SubProxy and Sky Proxy. In this process, Sky will receive a universal override over the Prime Agent Token in question.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80e0-84cd-eec0311dbcf9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.1.5 - Minimum Float

For each of Spark and Grove, a minimum of 10% of the total token supply must be in circulation \(float\) within a year, and maintain that float afterward. If the float goes below the 10% threshold, the Prime Agent might have 1 natural year to get the float back to 10% or above. If the float falls below 10%, Sky will mint additional Prime Agent tokens until the 10% threshold is reached. Newly minted tokens would be farmed and the revenue from farming would be returned to the Prime Agent, net of a penalty for needing to trigger the minimum float.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-801c-9b66-ddeffc698ae9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.2 - Borrowing Capacity And Mechanism

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to borrowing capacity and the borrow rate mechanism.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80b8-bad5-d6082ac9a7ff
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.1 - Subsidized Borrowing

Both Spark and Grove are entitled to borrow up to 1,000,000,000 USDS from Sky at a subsidized rate for an initial period of 2 years. The subsidized loan must be triggered before the end of 2025. This subsidized rate is set out in [A.2.10 - Borrow Rate Mechanism](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380d498b9f27eb014203f).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-809e-884d-e62c57d44ff8
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.2 - Borrow Rate Mechanism

The borrow rate subsidy for Spark and Grove will be calculated according to the formula: `t-bill_rate + ((base_rate - t-bill_rate) * T/24)`, where T represents elapsed months and is a counter that increases monthly over the 2-year period.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80d4-98b9-f27eb014203f
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.3 - Base Rate

The Base Rate will be dynamic and aligned with the SSR \(Sky Savings Rate\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-802e-bd3e-fee39c317e5c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.4 - Minimum Borrowing Threshold for Grant Eligibility

Primes, including Spark and Grove, must each maintain a minimum borrowing of 1,000,000,000 USDS from Sky at all times to remain eligible for the full monthly reimbursement grant. If the amount borrowed falls below this threshold, the reimbursement grant will be proportionately slashed. This grant will cover the difference between the borrowing Base Rate and the T-bill rate. Spark and Grove can borrow amounts exceeding the 1,000,000,000 USDS limit at the prevailing Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80d6-a379-e8f530afb97f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.5 - Borrowing Above Subsidized Limit

Spark and Grove can borrow amounts exceeding the 1,000,000,000 USDS limit at the prevailing Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8043-8e16-fb2924cb04ad
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.2.6 - Recourse

Sky’s recourse for bad debt consists of \(1\) minting Prime Agent tokens; \(2\) terminating a Prime’s right to further borrowing; and \(3\) activation of the Resolution Mechanism which includes, without limitation, the authority to suspend all protocol operations, seize or reallocate all of the Prime assets, override or disable smart contract functions, initiate managed restructuring, or execute full and permanent liquidation of the affected sub-protocol and all of its assets, in order to contain risk and preserve the stability and solvency of the broader ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8046-bbf5-ce09f28548f8
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.3 - Accessibility Reward

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to the Accessibility Reward.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80fa-9447-c389912f9aa9
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.3.1 - Accessibility Reward Rate

The standard Accessibility Reward rate is set at 0.2%. The Accessibility Reward is divided according to the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8077-8a46-deafc5616371
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.3.1.1 - Frontend Portion

Up to a maximum of 50% of the Accessibility Reward rate is apportioned to frontends.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80aa-b95b-fc5bc40e2ca1
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.3.1.2 - Prime Portion

A minimum of 50% of the Accessibility Reward rate is apportioned to Prime Agents. This percentage can be up to 100%, dependent on reduction from the Frontend Portion.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80cc-aa4e-f95e71ce60b9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.3.2 - Split Specification

Spark and Grove will specify the split of the Prime Portion in their respective Agent Artifacts, with an expectation of a 50/50 split \(0.1% each\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8054-91c3-eae67f96f86e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.3.3 - 2025 Bonus

An additional 0.4% Accessibility Reward bonus will apply during the calendar year 2025 \(ending December 31, 2025\). This bonus is strictly limited to the Prime and does not extend to the Prime Foundation. The bonus is subject to the limitation specified in [A.2.10 - Bonus Limitation](https://www.notion.so/21ff2ff08d73805ba6e1c0a093936dfe).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8095-acd7-f80dc1d457e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.3.3.1 - Bonus Limitation

USDS and sUSDS balances held by the Prime itself are not eligible for the Accessibility Reward bonus specified in [A.2.10 - 2025 Bonus](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738095acd7f80dc1d457e4).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ff2ff0-8d73-805b-a6e1-c0a093936dfe
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.3.4 - Sky Spread

A fixed margin of 0.1% \(Sky Spread\) forms part of the overall differential between the Savings Rate and the Base Rate, representing the premium earned by Sky for facilitating the ecosystem’s financing. The total spread between the Savings Rate and the Base Rate is therefore 0.3%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-809a-bcd8-f33374359034
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-11

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.4 - Double Distribution Upkeep To Sky

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to distributions to Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8031-927c-f773225e5a29
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.4.1 - Distribution Requirement

To ensure continuous ecosystem support over the period of years 10 to 30, Prime Agents must distribute either 0.5% of the market capitalization of their token per year, if distributed in tokens; or 0.6% of their market capitalization of their token per year if paid in stablecoins as a fee.
