These amounts include both the Standard Annual Upkeep Payment as set out in [A.2.10 - Standard Annual Upkeep Payment](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380b7b905c399c8d9fb5a) and the Additional Incremental Upkeep Payment as set out in[ ](https://atlas-axis.notion.site/Additional-Incremental-Upkeep-Payment-Core-If-a-Star-Agent-elects-not-to-distribute-certain-fund-1fdf2ff08d73802c804adcf401e7d66f#1fdf2ff08d73814ba4ffc4c497133102)[A.2.10 - Additional Incremental Upkeep Payment](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380158f85e0eea56822f1).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-801c-809f-c8476dcaeae9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.4.2 - Distribution Structure

To ensure continued ecosystem support, Primes, including Spark and Grove, are required to make annual upkeep payments to Sky, calculated based on retained funds as set out in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80aa-a2f5-d6c11b41d3b4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.4.2.1 - Standard Annual Upkeep Payment

All Prime Agents will pay a standard annual upkeep payment of 25 basis points \(bps\) \(0.25%\) per year. This standard upkeep payment applies permanently and is separate from any additional payments explained below.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80b7-b905-c399c8d9fb5a
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.4.2.2 - Additional Incremental Upkeep Payment

If a Prime Agent elects not to distribute certain funds to Sky, but instead retains those funds in its treasury, the Prime incurs an additional incremental upkeep payment. The incremental payment is calculated based on the total retained funds at the rate of 2.5 basis points \(bps\) \(0.025%\) per year per $1 million retained, if the payment is made in tokens. If the payment is made in cash \(USD\) rather than tokens, the incremental payment increases by 20%. This results in a rate of 3 basis points \(bps\) \(0.03%\) per year per $1 million retained.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8015-8f85-e0eea56822f1
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.5 - Genesis Capital Allocation

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to genesis capital allocations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-808c-a364-fc7cdb03d93e
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.1 - Spark Initial Allocation

The Initial Allocation for Spark is 25,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80c1-b0ab-e993bcaf8767
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.2 - Grove Initial Allocation

The Initial Allocation for Grove is 25,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-803a-8baa-c9c5be981fc8
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.3 - Initial Allocation Distribution

The Initial Allocation is distributed in USDS to each Prime SubProxy \(i.e. the Spark SubProxy and Grove SubProxy\). Both Grove and Spark elected to distribute 5% of the total token supply over a period of 20 years, lasting from year 10 to year 30, and will receive USDS 25,000,000 each.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80cb-b320-d8afb95b33b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.4 - Initial Allocation Mechanism

Shortly after the Agent Token launch, an Atlas Edit Proposal submitted to Sky Governance will allow the respective founding teams of Spark and Grove to propose an initial cash grant to their respective Prime Foundation. Sky Governance must consent to this initial cash grant via approving the Sky Atlas modification.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80c9-b89e-e105d5e9e881
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.5.4.1 - Initial Cash Grant To Spark Foundation

The founding team of Spark has proposed a cash grant of 800,000 USDS per month to the Spark Foundation from Spark’s Genesis Capital Allocation for a three \(3\) month period, beginning at the time of the Genesis Capital Allocation. The purpose of the grant is to enable Spark Foundation to fulfill its purpose of promoting the growth and development of Spark. This funding will support essential activities such as engineering efforts, community engagement, research, infrastructure, and administrative operations.  
  
Sky Governance hereby consents to this cash grant. The first month’s transfer must be made to the Spark Foundation immediately after the transfer of the Genesis Capital Allocation. See [A.2.10 - Transfer Of Genesis Capital Allocation To Spark SubProxy](https://www.notion.so/21ff2ff08d73805a9fc8da7d1b1e893a). Transfers for subsequent months will be made proportionally in Spark Spells included in Sky Executive Votes unless otherwise agreed by Sky and Spark.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ff2ff0-8d73-8018-be75-c28cee3dddb7
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.5 - Subsequent Allocation Mechanism

After the initial cash grant \(see [A.2.10 - Initial Allocation Mechanism](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380c9b89ee105d5e9e881)\) but before the SPK or GROVE token is decentralized enough to allow meaningful governance by token holders, the respective founding teams may propose subsequent cash grants to their Prime Foundations. Sky must consent to each subsequent cash grant via approving the Sky Atlas modification.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-801f-a2cd-c91c1dcce539
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.5.5.1 - Subsequent Cash Grant To Spark Foundation

The founding team of Spark has proposed a cash grant of 1,100,000 USDS per month to the Spark Foundation from Spark’s Prime Treasury for a three \(3\) month period, beginning on October 1, 2025. The purpose of this grant is to enable Spark Foundation to fulfill its purpose of promoting the growth and development of Spark. This funding will support essential activities such as engineering and product development, community engagement and growth initiatives, research and governance contributions, infrastructure and operational maintenance, and administrative operations.  
  
Sky Governance hereby consents to this cash grant. The transfer for October must be made to the Spark Foundation in a Spark Spell included in the October 2, 2025 Executive Vote. Transfers for subsequent months will be made proportionally in Spark Spells included in Sky Executive Votes unless otherwise agreed by Sky and Spark.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-8019-baf3-cefdd05d4a14
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.5.6 - Genesis Capital Backstop

Each genesis capital allocation is subject to the Genesis Capital Backstop \(see [A.3.9 - Genesis Capital Backstop](https://www.notion.so/280f2ff08d7380889de0c3e5be362184)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-807b-b13f-da2eb4f54417
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.6 - Intellectual Property

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to intellectual property.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80b2-bcab-ef08ef354f49
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.6.1 - License Agreements

Each Prime Foundation \(i.e. the Spark Foundation and Grove Foundation\) must sign a license agreement with Moonbow for all relevant intellectual property. This license agreement must contain the following terms:  
  
• Worldwide, commercial, exclusive license, revocable for material breach \(immediate revocation in severe cases, 30-day notice for non-severe cases\).  
• Royalty-free.  
• Perpetual term, subject to termination provisions.  
• Sublicensing allowed in specified cases without approval; any other sublicenses allowed only with Moonbow's explicit approval \(not to be unreasonably withheld\).  
• Licensee responsible for IP protection and enforcement; license agreement to define specific requirements.  
• Quarterly/annual reporting requirements on fraud cases and brand protection actions.  
• Moonbow's right to audit \(at Moonbow's cost\).  
• All improvements/developments belong to Moonbow.  
• Monetary penalties for IP violations shall be explicitly defined in the full licensing agreement, with specific thresholds for triggering penalties to be mutually agreed and stipulated therein.  
• Customary mutual indemnification and limitation of liability provisions.  
• Mandatory arbitration except in case of injunctive relief.  
• Other customary terms, conditions, representations and warranties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80ae-91eb-ca8cf6bbd984
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.6.2 - Open Source Protocol Code

All components of code that form part of the Prime Protocols \(i.e. Spark and Grove\) will be open source.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8032-83c7-c59d724b668b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.6.3 - Financial Strategies

The financial strategies implemented by the Prime Foundations \(i.e. the Spark Foundation and Grove Foundation\) will be closed source. Operational specifics are further defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80be-ae56-e7c91bb59687
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.6.3.1 - Role Of Prime Foundation

The Prime Foundation \(i.e. the Spark Foundation and Grove Foundation\) will retain ownership of the code and strategies that determine how capital is allocated for its Prime. They will contract with the Development Company for the development and execution of the strategies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8037-83ec-febab162f3a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.6.3.2 - Role Of Development Company

The Development Company for each Prime Agent will execute the strategy and relay the results to the Operational Executor Agent.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8024-9ff0-f5d493f8e90b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.6.3.3 - Role Of Operational Executor Agent

The relevant Operational Executor Agent, which is already engaged to the Prime in question, will conduct a principled compatibility analysis to ensure the strategy aligns with the Sky Atlas and the relevant Prime Artifact. Once validated, the Operational Executor Agent executes the capital allocation in question.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8064-8300-da2b342a9f4f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.6.3.4 - Prime Tokenholder Governance Rights

To ensure accountability, Prime token holders will retain governance rights, including the ability to revoke the Operational Executor Agent’s Executor Accord through a vote if necessary.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8058-90b9-f9571ae95882
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.7 - Peg Stability Module Management And Ownership

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to the Peg Stability Module \(PSM\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-807c-8a55-fbd6e8035c83
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.7.1 - Current and Future Control

The subdocuments herein set out details about the current control and oversight of the PSM, including the transition to ownership by Grove.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80aa-93a4-c8a720f62e6e
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.1.1 - Current Control (Until 2026)

The Peg Stability Module \(PSM\) remains directly managed and controlled by Sky until the full deployment and operationalization of the Actively Stabilizing Collateral \(ASC\) system. Sky retains responsibility for setting debt ceilings, determining liquidity levels, and overseeing asset management activities within the PSM during this period.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8043-86d4-f4efc1d3073c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.1.2 - Transition to Grove (2025)

Beginning in 2025, Grove shall assume operational and accounting ownership of the PSM, incorporating the PSM’s assets into its total value locked \(TVL\). Sky maintains active oversight and retains final control over strategic and operational decision-making within the PSM until full ASC implementation occurs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80fb-8ee0-c290389d9c17
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.7.2 - Operational Responsibilities

The subdocuments herein set out details about operational responsibilities relating to the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80e4-9823-cf0f73d36865
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.2.1 - Base Rate Obligation

Grove will be required to pay the Base Rate on all assets within the PSM. The Base Rate is dynamic and will align with the SSR \(Sky Savings Rate\), expected to fluctuate between the Aave and Ethena rates. See [A.2.10 - Base Rate](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d73802ebd3efee39c317e5c).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80a1-b3e4-c00f9e96ea8c
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.2.2 - Yield on USDC

Grove shall earn yield through Coinbase Custody on USDC holdings within the PSM, enabling potential positive carry depending upon the spread between custody yield and the Base Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-801a-a52a-eaa597a1c7fb
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.2.3 - Economic Rationale

Grove will pay the Base Rate on the PSM for the purpose of meeting ASC requirements, if there are no better alternatives available. Unlike other alternatives, USDC in the PSM will have no capital requirement. If other, better options for ASC are available, Grove should empty the PSM and allocate the assets to those options. It is anticipated that other protocols will also be willing to rent ASC "points", potentially allowing for the management of ASC needs across several Prime Agents for a profit.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80ac-8394-f5373a017474
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.7.3 - Future Transition and Long-Term Vision

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to the future transition and long-term vision for the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80da-88b9-f335649bf60d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.3.1 - Short Term (2025-2026)

The PSM will remain a central liquidity hub for stablecoin deployment. Grove will manage the PSM, paying the Base Rate while earning USDC yield, under Sky oversight until full ASC implementation. PSM assets will count toward Grove's TVL, enhancing its economic standing and collateral efficiency.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8075-b6da-d8c742c8b871
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.3.2 - Long Term (Post-2026)

The PSM's role will diminish as the ASC system becomes the primary liquidity management tool. Grove can wind down the PSM entirely if ASC requirements are fulfilled through other mechanisms \(e.g., Uniswap positions, Curve pools\). If ASC obligations rise significantly, PSM usage will increase, but as the system matures, its relevance will gradually decline.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80dd-bf38-d6671d5be9b3
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.7.4 - Implications for Grove

The subdocuments herein set out implications of the PSM terms with respect to Grove.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8054-bd87-e9fdcc3153f3
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.4.1 - Positive Carry And Liquidity Balancing

Grove can generate positive carry if the USDC yield exceeds the Base Rate. If the Base Rate rises significantly, the PSM may become costly, requiring liquidity balancing.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-800a-88c9-fa98e2b513b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.4.2 - Capital Efficiency

Grove can deploy USDC in high-yield strategies while retaining enough ASC to meet requirements. Aave USDC \(aUSDC\) and Curve LP positions are likely alternatives to direct PSM usage, albeit potentially with higher JRC requirements.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80f8-9bce-d9f6cf384d4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.7.4.3 - Risk Management

The PSM serves as a capital buffer, enabling Grove to scale liquidity massively without additional credit enhancement. As ASC obligations fluctuate, the PSM will act as a dynamic backstop, ensuring resilience during periods of high demand.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8004-af39-e752eea60dbc
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.8 - Token Launch Penalty, Capital Transfer, And Income Generation

The subdocuments herein set out agreed terms with respect to a token launch penalty, capital transfer, and income generation.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8081-a267-e887de3071e6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.1 - Token Launch Penalty

If either Spark or Grove \(each, a “Prime”\) does not complete its Prime Token Generation Event \(“TGE”\) by July 1, 2025, a penalty of thirty percent \(30%\) will apply. This penalty will be calculated on the income \(as defined in [A.2.10 - Income Definition](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380749edbe10ba2afae6e)\) the Prime is receiving or is meant to receive \(including revenue that has accrued but has not been transferred\) until the TGE occurs.   
  
The penalty shall be deducted from the Genesis Capital Allocation at the time of the Capital Transfer. If the Prime has already received its Genesis Capital Allocation, then the 30% penalty is paid as part of the Monthly Settlement Cycle. See [A.2.6 - Settlement](https://www.notion.so/248f2ff08d738008929cec648127aeba).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8086-abbc-f15f42a52a9f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.2 - Transfer Of Capital Funds

The transfer of the Genesis Capital Allocation to each Prime SubProxy \(i.e. the Spark SubProxy and Grove SubProxy\) shall occur once funding from the Surplus Buffer is approved. This condition ensures that capital deployment is aligned with the successful launch of the Prime’s token and the commencement of its independent operations. Any penalties applied under [A.2.10 - Token Launch Penalty](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738086abbcf15f42a52a9f) shall be deducted from the Capital Allocation at the time of transfer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8042-b803-e48beff70632
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.8.2.1 - Transfer Of Genesis Capital Allocation To Spark SubProxy

The transfer of 20.6 million USDS from the Surplus Buffer to the Spark SubProxy for the Genesis Capital Allocation must be included in the June 26, 2025 Executive Vote. This action is authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.  
  
This amount reflects that 4.4 million USDS of pre-TGE expenses were paid by Sky as specified in [A.2.10 - Treatment of Expenses Paid By Sky Pre-TGE](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738050b59fdea4df51d0e6). This includes 2 million USDS transferred from the Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget to Spark to provide liquidity to market makers and 2.4 million USDS transferred from the Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget to Spark to provide liquidity to exchanges. See [A.2.10 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Spark For Market Makers](https://www.notion.so/217f2ff08d738061b0eefab638f1ee5f) and [A.2.10 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Spark For Exchanges](https://www.notion.so/217f2ff08d7380e58c8ddb1f77a47083).  
  
No penalties were applied under [A.2.10 - Token Launch Penalty](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738086abbcf15f42a52a9f).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ff2ff0-8d73-805a-9fc8-da7d1b1e893a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.3 - Cessation Of MKR and SKY Token Flow

Upon the commencement of Income Generation \(as defined in [A.2.10 - Income Definition](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380749edbe10ba2afae6e)\) by a Prime, any existing or previously agreed-upon allocation or distribution of MKR or SKY tokens from Sky to that Prime shall immediately cease. The intent is for the Primes to transition to financial self-sufficiency upon the initiation of their respective income-generating activities. Furthermore, any token vesting to the Prime Foundation will be reduced by 50% until the TGE occurs.  Prior to the TGE, Primes agree to share with Sky the Prime Agent Token vesting schedules and lock-ups for their teams to ensure they align with the principles of long-term commitment to the Prime.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-80f6-ac91-fd22a59dae1c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.4 - Treatment of Expenses Paid By Sky Pre-TGE

Any operational or other expenses incurred by a Prime and paid directly by Sky after July 1, 2025 shall be treated as an advance against the Prime’s Genesis Capital Allocation. The total amount of such expenses shall be documented and shall be deducted from the Prime’s allocated capital funds at the time of the Capital Transfer \(as outlined in [A.2.10 - Transfer Of Capital Funds](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738042b803e48beff70632)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8050-b59f-dea4df51d0e6
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.8.4.1 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Spark For Market Makers

Sky has transferred 2 million USDS from the Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget to Spark to provide liquidity to market makers \(see [A.5.9 - Launch Project - Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping](https://www.notion.so/109f2ff08d738029babada835b70d03e)\). This amount shall be treated as an advance against Spark’s Genesis Capital Allocation and deducted from Spark’s allocated capital funds at the time of the Capital Transfer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 217f2ff0-8d73-8061-b0ee-fab638f1ee5f
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

######## A.2.9.2.2.2.8.4.2 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Spark For Exchanges

Sky has transferred 2.4 million USDS from the Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget to Spark to provide liquidity to exchanges \(see [A.5.9 - Launch Project - Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping](https://www.notion.so/109f2ff08d738029babada835b70d03e)\). This amount shall be treated as an advance against Spark’s Genesis Capital Allocation and deducted from Spark’s allocated capital funds at the time of the Capital Transfer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 217f2ff0-8d73-80e5-8c8d-db1f77a47083
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.5 - Income Generation And Pre-TGE Credit

"Income Generation" shall be deemed to commence on July 1, 2025. From that date until the Prime’s TGE, seventy percent \(70%\) of any income generated by the Prime’s activities shall be credited to its capital account \(net of any expenses paid by Sky as per [A.2.10 - Treatment of Expenses Paid By Sky Pre-TGE](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738050b59fdea4df51d0e6)\), subject to any penalties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-801c-b512-d60d3ee9d43d
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

####### A.2.9.2.2.2.8.6 - Income Definition

"Income" means all revenues or fees received or accrued by the applicable Prime after July 1, 2025, including: \(i\) Accessibility Rewards \(see [A.2.10 - Accessibility Reward Rate](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d7380778a46deafc5616371)\), \(ii\) Accessibility Reward Bonus for 2025 \(see [A.2.10 - 2025 Bonus](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738095acd7f80dc1d457e4)\), \(iii\) any Platform Fees charged to users, and \(iv\) Real World Asset fees charged to users, including any origination, servicing, or related charges, and \(v\) the blended cost of allocation spread between Junior and Senior Risk Capital.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fef2ff0-8d73-8074-9edb-e10ba2afae6e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.9 - Spark Senior Risk Capital

In the short term before the implementation of the Senior Risk Capital system, Sky will provide Spark with 15 million USDS of Senior Risk Capital. See [A.3.3 - Short Term Transitionary Measures](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73806bb7f7de5696200eb3). This Senior Risk Capital will not be transferred by Sky to Spark’s SubProxy Account; instead, it will be credited towards Spark’s Total Risk Capital. See [A.3.3 - Total Risk Capital](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d7380c5be85fc449781852f).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 217f2ff0-8d73-800c-b642-c16c63371afc
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-25

###### A.2.9.2.2.2.10 - Grove Senior Risk Capital

In the short term before the implementation of the Senior Risk Capital system, Sky will provide Grove with 15 million USDS of Senior Risk Capital. See [A.3.3 - Short Term Transitionary Measures](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73806bb7f7de5696200eb3). This Senior Risk Capital will not be transferred by Sky to Grove’s SubProxy Account; instead, it will be credited towards Grove’s Total Risk Capital. See [A.3.3 - Total Risk Capital](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d7380c5be85fc449781852f).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-8094-b531-f81f02237ac8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

#### A.2.9.2.3 - Ecosystem Accord 3: Sky And Launch Agent 2

The subdocuments herein record the terms of agreement between Sky and Launch Agent 2 as agreed in Ecosystem Accord 3.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-800e-9715-d2c7340e4f9d
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

##### A.2.9.2.3.1 - Accord Key Details

The subdocuments herein set out the key details of Ecosystem Accord 3, such as parties to the agreement and the duration of the Accord.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-806c-b628-f6d907a2a805
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

###### A.2.9.2.3.1.1 - Parties To The Accord

The parties to Ecosystem Accord 3 are Sky and Launch Agent 2, as defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-8048-a221-d1a54fda19d1
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.3.1.1.1 - Sky Details

The party ‘Sky’ comprises Sky Core.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-802a-a68a-c5e5356fb90e
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

####### A.2.9.2.3.1.1.2 - Launch Agent 2 Details

The party ‘Launch Agent 2’ comprises the Launch Agent 2 Prime Agent, Launch Agent 2 Foundation, and Launch Agent 2 Development Company.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-80cb-a9bd-f3543e9c018e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

###### A.2.9.2.3.1.2 - Duration Of The Accord

The duration of Ecosystem Accord 3 is indefinite, commencing from June 23, 2025.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-803a-96ff-c03101607ba7
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

##### A.2.9.2.3.2 - Accord Substantive Terms

The subdocuments herein set out the substantive terms of Ecosystem Accord 3.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-800b-9db1-f48b3f3ad897
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-31

###### A.2.9.2.3.2.1 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Launch Agent 2

Sky has transferred 500,000 USDS from the Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget to Launch Agent 2 to provide liquidity to decentralized finance protocols on Solana \(see [A.5.9 - Launch Project - Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping](https://www.notion.so/109f2ff08d738029babada835b70d03e)\). This amount shall be treated as an advance against Launch Agent 2’s Genesis Capital Allocation and deducted from Launch Agent 2’s allocated capital funds at the time of the Capital Transfer.  
  
The transfer must be made to a multisig controlled by Launch Agent 2’s Operational Executor Agent and the multisig must have the ability to withdraw the liquidity provided to decentralized finance protocols at any time. The address of the multisig on Solana is `6cTVPDJ8WR1XGxdgnjzhpYKRqcv78T4Nqt95DY8dvMmn`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-8095-b548-d67974ecda3a
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-31

####### A.2.9.2.3.2.1.1 - Use Of Funds For Launch Agent 2 Development Expenses

Launch Agent 2 may also use the funds specified in [A.2.10 - Transfer From Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget To Launch Agent 2](https://www.notion.so/226f2ff08d738095b548d67974ecda3a) to fund development expenses at its discretion.  
  
If Launch Agent 2 elects to use funds in this way, it shall notify its Operational Executor Agent of the amount to be used for development expenses. The Operational Executor Agent will then withdraw the liquidity from the decentralized finance protocols, if necessary, and transfer it to an account designated by Launch Agent 2.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 241f2ff0-8d73-80be-a4a4-ca265ef757cd
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-31

###### A.2.9.2.3.2.2 - Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool

Launch Agent 2 is eligible for a Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool. See [A.2.4 - Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool](https://www.notion.so/226f2ff08d7380d491e5ea4171470c7d).   
  
The Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool is calculated on a monthly basis as \(1\) the Sky Savings Rate multiplied by all USDS balances on Solana, less \(2\) all Integration Boost payments made to partners on Solana.  
Payments are made on a monthly basis from the Integration Boost wallets specified in [A.2.4 - Near Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380068d52f441b4e85f5b) to a Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool wallet controlled by Launch Agent 2’s Operational Executor Agent. The address of the Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool wallet on Solana is `8JmDPG5BFQ6gpUPJV9xBixYJLqTKCSNotkXksTmNsQfj`.  
  
Funds from the Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool wallet may be used to incentivize partners on Solana to promote USDS adoption as directed by Launch Agent 2. Funds from the Pre-Pioneer Incentive Pool may not be transferred to Launch Agent 2’s SubProxy Account, Launch Agent 2 Foundation, or Launch Agent 2 Development Company.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 226f2ff0-8d73-8017-bfe5-e594a5770d9a
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

### A.2.9.0.3.1 - Business Activities - Element Annotation

The element "business activities" refers to the commercial activities, transactions, and interactions that Ecosystem Actors perform within the ecosystem. These may include, but are not limited to, service delivery, product development, collaboration, and information exchange.

**Document Type:** Annotation
**UUID:** 348018b6-32b8-4a54-bc1a-a437707aa539
**Last Modified:** 2024-10-04

### A.2.9.0.3.2 - Ecosystem - Element Annotation

The element "Ecosystem" in the phrase "Ecosystem Accords" should be understood as the collaborative network in which multiple stakeholders \(referred to as "Ecosystem Actors"\) interact to conduct business operations benefiting Sky.

**Document Type:** Annotation
**UUID:** 182bfe0c-876b-4dc1-b869-11aad6e524e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

## A.2.10 - Legal Resilience

This Article governs the Resilience Fund and defines infrastructure and processes to support legal risk management and legal governance.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** ee1614e6-2f5e-403f-be3e-81a320a3d8b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

### A.2.10.1 - Legal Resilience

This Section manages the Resilience Fund \(also “RF”\) and other infrastructure for legal risk management and legal governance.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** f7f80580-a059-4a9a-8aa7-acb3823b8ccb
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.1 - Legal Defense Resources

This document defines the resources available for legal defense. Over time, it can include both Sky Governance-controlled assets and external third-party resources and may be used to cover additional risks.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** b3123a4c-6cd8-480a-ae23-ab74c7d83cc5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.1.1 - Resilience Fund

The Resilience Fund \(RF\) is a self-insurance instrument fully controlled by Sky Governance, which will cover legal defense expenses in case of legal or regulatory action against Sky or active participants in the Sky Ecosystem. The RF will be the primary source for direct legal defense funding. The conditions of use are defined in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Claim Management Process](https://www.notion.so/92d5a9c0981745a3b6719dc1a78e31bc).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 53381d8a-905d-4bab-8849-e89dddbda278
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.1.1 - Resilience Fund Budget

The budget of the Resilience Fund is defined in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Current Budget](https://www.notion.so/e46f169874554c738e1c75962facfe04). The Support Facilitator can propose to pay out the budget manually through a Weekly Governance Cycle, according to the rules related to claims described in this Section. The Support Facilitator can propose modifications to the document cited above through the Weekly Governance Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 454155d8-4449-4802-9f5e-11e8fb2121c4
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.1.1 - Resilience Fund Current Budget

The current active budget for the Resilience Fund is: 5,000,000 USDS per year, with the full amount available at the start of each calendar year.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e46f1698-7455-4c73-8e1c-75962facfe04
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.1.2 - Resilience Fund Transition Funding

Legal defense expenses directly related to Sky may be temporarily financed using the resources of the Resilience Fund. However, a separate Claim Protocol and Standard Operational Protocol must be developed to govern cases where Sky is the target of a legal or regulatory action.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** bb88f9c0-adc7-429b-b596-084e36def4f4
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.1.2.0.3.1 - Separate Claim Protocol And Standard Operational Protocol - Element Annotation

The element refers to a separate set of procedures to be applied where Sky is the target of a legal or regulatory action. These separate procedures have not yet been defined. Once defined and ratified, they will be detailed in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Legal Defense Standard Operational Protocols](https://www.notion.so/5649bed1adcb408cb92c08d96aa26f8d). The Resilience Technical Committee must assist in elaborating this ruleset.

**Document Type:** Annotation
**UUID:** ce063dbe-6074-4e3c-a626-fa6fabdd8b70
**Last Modified:** 2024-08-31

###### A.2.10.1.1.1.2 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee

The Resilience Technical Committee is a group of Ecosystem Actors authorized by Sky to provide operational support such as onboarding new beneficiaries to the RF, approving quotes, and assessing claims to be supported by the RF. Additional operational support includes providing general advice on the further development of the fund, amendments to the claim procedure, resilience measures and other risk-management topics.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** b30b9793-69e5-44fb-ae75-d20ea27ba77f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.2.1 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Selection And Compensation

The Support Facilitator selects the members of the Resilience Technical Committee and manages payments for their services on a project basis. The associated budget is defined in  [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Current Budget](https://www.notion.so/e46f169874554c738e1c75962facfe04).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** fe3e5738-3ec1-445a-b2ca-9508df06ee82
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member Requirements

The individual members of the Resilience Technical Committee who are directly involved in providing operational services on behalf of Sky must fulfill the requirements defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f90a1906-2d30-44ea-b523-3de3b292f2e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2.1 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member Skill Set Requirement

Members of the Resilience Technical Committee must have relevant experience or current employment in the legal industry, or with a world-leading insurance broker, insurance company, or risk management firm.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e800e697-f014-43eb-a3e7-80fee42206a0
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2.2 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member Experience Requirement

Members of the Resilience Technical Committee must have at least three \(3\) years of experience managing self-insurance instruments or experience in legal or regulatory risk analysis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 733428ff-2bf7-4f62-ba5a-a888371a9b4b
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2.3 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member Professional Degree Requirement

Members of the Resilience Technical Committee must have a law, management, risk, or insurance professional degree.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 07689ba3-d2d4-49b5-888c-0a06e5c22a97
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2.4 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member No Conflicts Requirement

Members of the Resilience Technical Committee must not be involved in any business activity outside Sky or in any role within Sky that could result in a conflict of interest, either directly or indirectly.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 18d5b439-eb5a-427c-80b2-08ecac0dd017
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.2.5 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member Technology Experience Requirement

Members of the Resilience Technical Committee must have at least three \(3\) years of experience in the cryptocurrency, DeFi, Web3, or emerging technology sectors.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 2f4a858d-ef70-48c7-8f30-e99ce228815d
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.2.3 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee Current Membership

Approved Resilience Technical Committee members are defined as Active Data in [A.2.11 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee List Of Current Members](https://www.notion.so/5c198fe61e734ebcbae36e22afa50b3a).  
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
  
• The Responsible Party is the Support Facilitators.  
  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 1a78a24d-672f-4fa2-ba04-b43041e19de9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-08

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.2.3.0.6.1 - Resilience Fund Technical Committee List Of Current Members

List of active Resilience Technical Committee Members:  
  
• Gallagher

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** 5c198fe6-1e73-4ebc-bae3-6e22afa50b3a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

###### A.2.10.1.1.1.3 - Resilience Fund Policy

This provision and its subdocuments will govern the conditions and terms of use of the Resilience Fund.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1469f3d1-3289-48b6-8c86-13599719ece5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.1 - Resilience Fund Policy Loss Events

The Resilience Process covers legal defense or legal representation expenses incurred by a participant of the Sky Ecosystem when they are the defendant or respondent in a legal or regulatory action \(“The Loss Event”\). There must be a direct relationship between the legal or regulatory action and the Beneficiary’s activity at Sky.  
  
A \(non-exhaustive\) list of legal or regulatory actions that may qualify is:  
  
• Official requirements, or communications, from a regulatory body, governmental authority, or a court  
  
• Subpoenas  
  
• Lawsuit  
  
• Writs

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5c346dd6-2d7f-4a85-b0ca-73ebb968a708
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.2 - Resilience Fund Policy Exclusions

The cases specified in the following subelements will generally be excluded from coverage by the legal defense process:  
  
• Prior or pending claims  
  
• Claims between persons involved in the Sky Ecosystem  
  
• Loss covered by other insurance  
  
• Willful criminal offenses, fraud, or dishonesty  
  
• Conflict of interest

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** c99ed07c-0930-472d-9639-bc976c3a6f56
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.3 - Resilience Fund Policy Beneficiaries

Eligible beneficiaries of the Resilience Fund are persons who fulfill the following requirements:  
  
a\) act on their own behalf OR b\) act on behalf of a legal entity OR c\) act on behalf of a collective AND have one of the following roles:  
  
Previous Structure:  
• Recognized Delegates  
• Core Unit Facilitators  
• Core Unit Contributors  
• Dai Foundation Board Members  
  
Current Structure:  
• Active SKY or Agent token holders that participate regularly in governance \(e.g., voting, writing proposals\)  
• Alignment Conservers   
◦ Aligned Delegates \(ADs\)  
• Scope Facilitators  
• The Guardian \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - The Guardian](https://www.notion.so/15823ae53ae04c7285e9e6dc5d4063bd)\) or actors that fulfill an equivalent role.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8c0db79a-5407-47c9-9665-2c2833115474
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.3.3.1 - No Acquired Right Or Claim For Resilience Fund Policy Beneficiaries

Persons qualified as beneficiaries do not have any acquired right or claim. The claim decision process is described in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Processes And Principles](https://www.notion.so/eb08fafe7bfd49689a92341fd882f784), and the payout of a claim is subject to the approval of the Resilience Technical Committee \(at their sole and absolute discretion\) and further contingent on a SKY vote endorsing payment of the claim.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1168b70f-c43e-4070-84e3-3ac5fe85e911
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.4 - Resilience Fund Policy Geographic Coverage

Geographical coverage is worldwide.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 7efa7f37-7c4b-4fb8-b849-89be31cd2edc
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.5 - Resilience Fund Policy Effective Date

A Beneficiary’s eligibility for coverage under this Artifact will start after ratification of MIP106: 2023-03-27. \(“Effective Date”\) and expire twenty-four \(24\) months after cessation of their role as a Beneficiary.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 2b978a8d-ae50-4d44-b43c-7889586c0240
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.3.6 - Resilience Fund Policy Base Of Coverage

Coverage is “Claims made”. This means the Loss Event \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Policy Loss Events](https://www.notion.so/5c346dd62d7f4a85b0ca73ebb968a708)\) must occur after the Effective Date \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Policy Effective Date](https://www.notion.so/2b978a8dae504d44b43c7889586c0240)\).  
  
The facts and circumstances that originated the Loss Event may have occurred in the past for a maximum period of up to twenty-four \(24\) months before the date representing the later of \(a\) the Effective Date and \(b\) the date the Beneficiary is first eligible for coverage under this Artifact \(the “Retroactivity Period”\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** abb1e761-88d2-4b6f-a2cb-63fcd1ee7ff1
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.1.4 - Resilience Fund Processes And Principles

Overview of the related processes and principles:  
  
• Application process  
  
• Claim management process  
  
• Caps and Exclusions  
  
• Refund of amounts to the Resilience Fund

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** eb08fafe-7bfd-4968-9a92-341fd882f784
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1 - Resilience Fund Application Process

The Resilience Fund application process is defined in the subdocuments of this document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f78ed429-ba9d-4ce6-8e10-b215750e1194
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.1 - Resilience Fund Proof Of Eligibility

To become recognized as a Beneficiary, an Applicant must first select an Ethereum address that is linked to their activity at Sky \(“Proof of Eligibility” or “POE”\). Each Beneficiary type will have specific Proofs of Eligibility that are suitable for their role.  
  
Valid PoE:  
  
• Address set as owner and used to sign transactions from Sky multisigs, such as Core Unit operational wallets, auditor wallets or SPFs \(old structure\), or a Facilitator multisig \(Endgame Structure\)  
  
    ◦ PoE valid for former Core Unit Contributors, Core Unit Facilitators, and Scope Facilitators.  
  
• Address that voted directly or through delegation in at least ten Governance Polls or Executive Votes.  
  
    ◦ PoE valid for active MKR or SKY holders, former Recognized Delegates \(old structure\), and Aligned Delegates \(new structure\)  
  
• Address that received compensation from Sky, with the following conditions:  
  
    ◦ at least six \(6\) payouts spread over at least six \(6\) months in Dai or USDS OR  
  
    ◦ Dai or USDS DssVest stream spanning at least six \(6\) months OR  
  
    ◦ 3 MKR or SKY payouts spread over at least three \(3\) months OR  
  
    ◦ MKR or SKY DssVest stream spanning at least three \(3\) months  
    ◦ PoE valid for former Core Unit Contributors and Facilitators  
  
• Address that holds >1 MKR or >24,000 SKY  
  
• Attestation  
  
    ◦ If no PoE is available, a verified Beneficiary must attest eligibility on behalf of the applicant.  
  
    ◦ This PoE will be used exceptionally if no other Proof is available and will be assessed individually by the Resilience Fund Technical Committee.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 4fb32eb6-2aac-4ad7-8c56-1045f53bb388
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.2 - Resilience Fund Proof Of Eligibility Template

Applications for the Resilience Fund must follow this template:  
  
• .x: \[Application RF\]  
  
• .x.1: \[PoE\]  
  
• .x.2: \[Active Period\]  
  
• .x.3: \[Relevant Executive Proposal: Governance decision ratified by a governance vote\]  
  
• .x4: \[Signature hash\]

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5302dcf1-0470-420e-ab9b-d72d4a616c68
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.3 - Resilience Fund Proof Of Eligibility Digital Signature

The Applicant for the Resilience Fund must additionally sign an application message from the Ethereum Address \(Digital Signature\) used as PoE [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Proof Of Eligibility](https://www.notion.so/4fb32eb62aac4ad78c561045f53bb388).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 07681d4a-3e63-482f-99c4-c10d9e36e6a9
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.4 - Application Resilience Fund Application Terms And Conditions

By signing this message, the Applicant accepts and agrees:  
  
• To comply with the terms and rules of [A.2.11](https://www.notion.so/ee1614e62f5e403fbe3e81a320a3d8b5) \(the “Legal Resilience Fund Terms”\).  
  
• That participation in the program is opt-in and voluntary and can be waived anytime by the Beneficiary.  
  
• The Legal Resilience Fund Terms can be amended at any time through the established governance processes.  
  
• That being registered as Beneficiary does not give rise to any right, benefit, entitlement, or claim, nor creates an obligation on any party to pay the Beneficiary.  
  
Additionally, the Beneficiary declares that:  
  
• No situation currently involves or appears to involve a conflict of interest, and any emerging potential conflict of interest shall be disclosed as soon as it happens.  
  
• Any role change in Sky or termination of active engagement will be immediately communicated to the Resilience Technical Committee.  
  
The Resilience Technical Committee will elaborate a user-friendly onboarding manual for the RF.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 12e68099-dbdb-4118-83a3-073e1cac25ac
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.5 - Resilience Fund Application Confirmation

The Applicant for the Resilience Fund will additionally send an encrypted message to the Resilience Technical Committee Member in charge of the onboarding process to confirm the application.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0df424af-8850-4f81-90e2-22a80988d0c9
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.1.6 - Resilience Fund Approval Process And Verifiability

PoEs rely on a governance decision that was ratified by an Executive Vote.  
  
The Resilience Technical Committee Member must identify this governance decision by verifying the spell that enacted the Executive Vote ratifying the respective governance decision. This spell contains the hash of the respective Executive Vote. Exceptional circumstances where no direct governance decision is available or difficult to assert will be handled case by case.  
  
The Resilience Technical Committee Member will confirm the onboarding decision via an encrypted message to the Applicant.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** c2d96756-3b52-44de-bf2b-56e33f31ace1
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2 - Resilience Fund Claim Management Process

Overview of the claim management processes:  
  
• Legal Counsel Pre-approval  
  
• Claim approval / Advance Payment  
  
• Reimbursement

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 92d5a9c0-9817-45a3-b671-9dc1a78e31bc
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.1 - Resilience Fund Legal Counsel And Quote Pre-Approval

The first step in the claim management process is to approve the Legal Counsel to undertake legal defense or representation and the quote presented to commence legal work. The Beneficiary must present the quote from the law firm they selected for their legal defense/representation. The request must indicate at least the following:  
  
• Name of Legal Counsel  
  
• Name of Law Firm  
  
• If not in the Lawyer Registry, Proof of Eligibility  
  
• Quote  
  
The quote must include:  
  
1. The initial payment required by Counsel to commence work immediately. This is the initial amount to be claimed against the Resilience Fund.  
  
2. A global estimated fee based on an hourly rate OR fixed fee OR monthly retainer fee.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5d6eef98-66f6-46bc-be58-f38f0e307afc
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.1.1 - Resilience Fund Qualified Counsel

If the Legal Counsel is RF-qualified in the Lawyer Registry \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Lawyer Registry](https://www.notion.so/e9959a4aaf2b4ea6b1140f31c815876d)\), the claim is automatically submitted to the Claim Approval process \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Claim Approval](https://www.notion.so/82e07e7bfe3a450ea0164775997920bc)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e41025ba-b501-4d53-a073-9785b4bedd36
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.1.2 - Not Resilience Fund Qualified Counsel

If Legal Counsel is NOT RF-qualified in the Lawyer Registry \([A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Lawyer Registry](https://www.notion.so/e9959a4aaf2b4ea6b1140f31c815876d)\), then the Support Facilitators, or alternatively the Resilience Fund Technical Committee Member, will verify if the Legal Counsel complies with requisites in Lawyer Registry Acceptance Criteria and LR Resilience Fund Acceptance Criteria. See:  [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Lawyer Registry Acceptance Criteria](https://www.notion.so/0730777ae51a4919874d4b395c1726c5) and [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Representation Requirements](https://www.notion.so/3a57039ee705459596d771ed11264f7a).  
  
If the Legal Counsel of the quote is determined by the Resilience Technical Committee to comply with the requirements of this Artifact, the quote is pre-approved and the legal counsel is added to the LR.  
  
If the Legal Counsel doesn’t comply with the LR requirements, the claim is rejected and the Beneficiary must propose a different Legal Counsel.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 4d8a6666-c181-4538-b5a4-f3179e387d9f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval

If a Beneficiary incurs a Loss Event, they can submit a Reimbursement Payout Claim against the LD RF according to the process specified in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 82e07e7b-fe3a-450e-a016-4775997920bc
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.1 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Payout Claim

The Support Facilitator must review the Reimbursement Claim and decide whether to trigger a Governance Poll to perform a claims payout, by developing an internal model with input from experts and professionals.  
  
The Payout Reimbursement Claim must contain the following elements:  
  
    ◦ Description of Loss Event with relevant commentaries and context  
  
    ◦ Advance Payment or Reimbursement  
  
    ◦ Type of process  
  
    ◦ Date of writ/subpoena/lawsuit  
  
    ◦ Supportive Documentation  
  
        ▪ Law firm’s proposal and invoice OR quote  
  
        ▪ Copy of the Lawsuit/writ/ communication OR official requirement issued by a Court or Governmental Agency Supportive documentation is highly sensible. It is required to use encryption tools.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 859230fa-698f-4db2-b429-b934a161a501
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.2 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Coverage Framework

In consultation with the Resilience Technical Committee, the Support Facilitators must develop a framework for establishing limits and coverage amounts per case, and apply these limits to individual claims.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e5bd96eb-6282-4d6a-8da3-30dce04a013c
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.3 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Support Facilitators Review

The Support Facilitators will review the Payout Claim and if it contains all required elements and supportive documentation, will immediately transmit it to the Resilience Fund Technical Committee.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0fc7857d-cdb0-4d02-a933-bb35538c3771
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.4 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Technical Commitee Review

The Resilience Fund Technical Committee will verify the merits of the claim according to the following substantial criteria \(non-exhaustive list\):  
  
• Absence of Exclusions  
  
• Identity and Role of Beneficiary  
  
• Time scope  
  
• Authenticity of writ/lawsuit  
  
• Reasonability of lawyer fees, which must take into account market rates in the respective jurisdiction

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1b684078-c590-4576-a727-0688309a7bd4
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.5 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Technical Committee Review Process

From the moment the Payout Claim is filed, the Resilience Technical Committee will have five \(5\) working days to provide a payout recommendation. The recommendations of the Technical Committee are made in their sole and absolute discretion, are definitive, and are not subject to appeal.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** cc1cff4a-5639-47c7-a51b-3e5f08253c8b
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.6 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Quorum And Decision Majorities

Decisions related to claim payouts require a quorum of three \(3\) experts from the Resilience Technical Committee with a simple majority \(>50%\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** c3d71dc4-7301-434b-ba69-f2392506cb83
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.7 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Decision Protections

The recommendations of the Resilience Technical Committee are non-binding and will not give rise to any right or claim to the beneficiaries nor give rise to any obligation or responsibility.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0208605c-4d37-476f-9c59-f960a7b783cf
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.2.8 - Resilience Fund Claim Approval Support Facilitators Decision

Based on the recommendation of the Resilience Technical Committee, the Support Facilitators will decide whether to trigger a Governance Poll through the Weekly Governance Cycle to perform a claim payout.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d1b45d9e-750c-4446-8982-f4798b8b5ea6
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.2.3 - Resilience Fund Payout / Reimbursement

If the Governance Poll for paying out a claim is successful, an Executive Vote must be created and approved through Sky Governance processes to draw the funds from the Surplus Buffer and send them to the Beneficiary’s registered wallet. The spend must be accounted for in the budget in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Current Budget](https://www.notion.so/e46f169874554c738e1c75962facfe04).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** ef8fb085-f4b2-4908-84c3-6d6b6d19efd4
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.3 - Resilience Fund Caps And Exclusions

Unless otherwise approved by a governance vote of Sky, claim funds approved under this Section are subject to the caps and exclusions specified in the subdocuments of this document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 379fba53-45bc-4012-9872-5c161332d43f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.3.1 - Resilience Fund Aggregate Cap

Claims will be subject to an aggregate cap for all claims in relation to a Claim Event determined by the risk models elaborated by the Resilience Technical Committee.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 4fced61a-66c7-4e91-8d78-4f4324564604
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.3.2 - Resilience Fund Direct Costs And Legal Expenses Cap

Claims can only be used to reimburse Beneficiaries for their losses and disbursements directly arising from a Claim Event, and reasonable legal expenses. “Reasonable legal expense” is a variable amount that will be determined taking into account the average market rates of the respective jurisdiction.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 00f761aa-c6e4-4042-a580-6c5778ecec08
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.3.3 - Resilience Fund Caps Government Or Regulatory Fines Or Damages Exclusion

Must not be used to reimburse the payment of government or regulatory fines or damages awarded by the court.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 605e3613-ab5b-4004-8729-bfd8afbdc195
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.4 - Refund Of Amounts To Resilience Fund

Where Beneficiaries receive financial benefit or reimbursement of any type as a result of orders of a court, governmental or investigative body or regulatory agency that results in a windfall to the Beneficiary, these windfall amounts will be returned by the Beneficiary to the Resilience Fund within 14 days of receipt.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 6c4cdcc3-56a5-45a4-bbc9-81b1b63b43ee
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.4.1 - Refund Of Amounts To Resilience Fund Examples

By way of example only and without limiting the generality of the principle described in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Refund Of Amounts To Resilience Fund](https://www.notion.so/6c4cdcc356a545a4bbc981b1b63b43ee), such amounts include, without limitation:  
  
• Amounts awarded by a court towards the Beneficiary’s legal fees or disbursements;  
  
• An award of damages to a Beneficiary in relation to a Claim Event;  
  
• Monies or digital assets located by police or other investigative bodies that were identified as the property of Sky; and  
  
• Interest payable to a Beneficiary in relation to a Claim Event.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** de8c3844-c688-4bdc-b3c9-c8ca1d53e6c0
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.1.4.5 - Resilience Fund Litigation / Defense Management

Beneficiaries and their legal teams must, in the conduct of the litigation and as a condition of receiving reimbursement from the Resilience Fund, satisfy all of the conditions listed in the subdocuments of this document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8491c5be-0525-4a6c-9f98-fa38a9092cf5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.5.1 - Good Faith In Resilience Fund Litigation / Defense Management

Beneficiaries and their legal teams must act honestly, consistently, and fairly in handling claims and litigation.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e2e38ee7-4dc3-4be6-8c93-8e77db000e49
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.5.2 - Promptness In Resilience Fund Litigation / Defense Management

Beneficiaries and their legal teams must make an early assessment of the prospects of success and deal with claims promptly.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5811c1c0-0424-4a54-a88c-9f3686a8524f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.5.3 - Cost Control In Resilience Fund Litigation / Defense Management

Beneficiaries and their legal teams  must keep costs to a minimum and avoid reliance on technical defenses which have low probability of success.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 12a40bc7-8832-46c0-a0d2-892e260ef916
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

######## A.2.10.1.1.1.4.5.4 - Alternative Dispute Resolution In Resilience Fund Litigation / Defense Management

Beneficiaries and their legal teams must consider alternative dispute resolution \(ADR\) options at all times.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d790e2d8-43cc-4aa8-97a4-a762930f4575
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.1.2 - Lawyer Registry

The Lawyer Registry \(also “LR”\) is a registry of specialized Ecosystem Actors who are qualified to perform legal work for Sky Ecosystem or participants in the Sky Ecosystem, including, but not limited to, legal representation or legal defense.  
  
Lawyers will be onboarded in the Lawyer Registry covering at least the areas specified in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Lawyer Registry Covered Areas](https://www.notion.so/4e5d88429ead4c35b9f58ee5de8f42c0).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** e9959a4a-af2b-4ea6-b114-0f31c815876d
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.1 - Lawyer Registry Covered Areas

`| Category                         | Areas of Law                                         | Examples                                                                                                          |  
|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|  
| Disputes/Investigations          | Regulatory Enforcement – Securities/Finance Law      | SEC/FinCEN/CFTC/Central Bank matters, Tax authority actions, OFAC orders, Sanctions                               |  
|                                  | Securities Law Disputes (private party claims, class actions, etc.) | Sky investor claims, Lawsuits against Sky CORE actors                                                            |  
|                                  | Intellectual Property                                | Patent Claims/Trolls, Trademark Claims/Trolls                                                                     |  
|                                  | General Litigation – Any other Party-Party Disputes  | Disputes between Sky and 3rd party suppliers, Breach of contract disputes, Employment law disputes, Disputes between Sky and Ecosystem actors |  
| Commercial Matters (non-litigious) | Commercial and Contracts                            | Commercial transactions (Audits, listing agreements, inter-Agent agreements affecting Sky Core etc.), Contracts and Procurement (includes contract management), Competition/Anti-Trust Law, Data protection and Privacy, Insurance Law |  
|                                  | Intellectual Property, Information Technology        | IT procurement, licenses, and contracts, Convergent technologies, Intellectual property rights, Data protection and privacy, Trademark/Patent applications |  
|                                  | Corporate Structuring, Entity Formation/Reporting, Corporate Financing and Tax | Entity formation assistance provided to Sky Core, Annual Reporting/Filings, Company Law, Corporate Finance, Tax Law |`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 4e5d8842-9ead-4c35-b9f5-8ee5de8f42c0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.2 - Lawyer Registry Acceptance Criteria

To be included in the Lawyer Registry, lawyers must satisfy all requirements described in the following subelements.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0730777a-e51a-4919-874d-4b395c1726c5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.2.1 - Lawyer Registry Licensing Criterion

Licensed legal professionals in their respective jurisdiction.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 42f3aa3d-ed77-44ef-8a65-f32e9a1fe1f2
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.2.2 - Lawyer Registry Technology Experience Criterion

Proven experience with Sky Ecosystem crypto, or emerging technologies.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24729585-823b-40d1-9ea0-fc1a9efb6454
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.2.3 - Lawyer Registry No Conflicts Criterion

No Conflict of Interest in the matter over which they have carriage.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1a0d5c97-9503-4587-94eb-a121a4469bcf
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.2.4 - Lawyer Registry Conflict Checks Criterion

Lawyers must have internal processes to conduct conflict checks prior to any engagement under this Section.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0536848a-6665-4613-9547-e19496cd2452
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.2.5 - Lawyer Registry Conflict Handling Criterion

Where conflicts arise within the lawyer’s firm, the firm must:  
1. In the case where a Beneficiary is handling the claim, cease all further work and immediately notify the Resilience Technical Committee in writing and cooperate with the Resilience Technical Committee to avoid a continuing conflict or, where this is not practicable, transfer the matter to another LR registered Lawyer, and  
  
2. In the case where the Guardian is handling the claim, cease all further work and immediately notify the Guardian in writing and cooperate with the Guardian to avoid a continuing conflict or, where this is not practicable, transfer the matter to another LR registered Lawyer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d10f8acc-feb9-4bbe-8e8a-090fd2f723d1
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.3 - Resilience Fund Representation Requirements

Legal counsel providing services funded through the RF must fulfill additional requirements specified in the subdocuments herein. Legal counsel qualified for RF will be listed as such in the Lawyer Registry.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 3a57039e-e705-4595-96d7-71ed11264f7a
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.3.1 - Resilience Fund Representation Experience Requirement

Lead Counsel, Lead Barristers, and Lead Trial Attorneys \(as applicable\) must each have a minimum of ten \(10\) years of relevant legal experience and demonstrable expertise in the specific areas of law, legal processes, and jurisdictions listed on the Lawyer Registry.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** b1261000-1baf-477a-9ee8-cc11dbe06a22
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.3.2 - Resilience Fund Representation Expertise Requirement

Lawyers included on the Lawyer Registry for litigious categories, must have been Lead Counsel in at least 15 cases in the relevant area of law, type of process, and jurisdiction for which they are listed. Lawyers included on the Lawyer Registry for non-litigious matters must have demonstrable experience in the relevant area of law, type of process and jurisdiction indicated in the table set out under [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Lawyer Registry Covered Areas](https://www.notion.so/4e5d88429ead4c35b9f58ee5de8f42c0).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0dd68170-9b1c-4222-92bc-ee943cef9788
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.4 - Lawyer Registry Template

Entries in the LR must follow this template:  
  
• .x: \[Advisor name and short description\]  
  
• .x.1: \[Name of Firm\]  
  
• .x.2: \[Specialization Area\]  
  
• .x.3: \[Jurisdiction\]  
  
• .x.4: \[RF qualified \(y/n\)\]

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 6d03ba8c-3832-4dbe-a40a-d6d51e786a95
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.5 - Requirements For Resilience Technical Committee

The Resilience Technical Committee will verify the eligibility criteria of new candidates in the Lawyer Registry and submit a list of approved candidates to the Support Facilitators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 28d125cb-72f6-4b9a-ba8e-1cccebad152d
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

###### A.2.10.1.1.2.6 - Lawyer Registry Update Process

The current approved lawyers in the Lawyer Registry are defined as Active Data in [A.2.11 - Lawyer Registry Current Approved Legal Counsels](https://www.notion.so/034d0ca120cb4105bffa507bfed260dc).   
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
  
• The Responsible Party is the Support Facilitators.  
  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 31a84336-5d4e-484e-9379-aceb82573aee
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-08

####### A.2.10.1.1.2.6.0.6.1 - Lawyer Registry Current Approved Legal Counsels

There are no active legal counsels in the Lawyer Registry.

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** 034d0ca1-20cb-4105-bffa-507bfed260dc
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

##### A.2.10.1.1.3 - The Guardian

The Guardian is a specialized Ecosystem Actor that will be exclusively mandated to retain and instruct counsel to assist with the legal defense of actors in the Sky Ecosystem that, due to their organizational structure or circumstances, may be unable to obtain legal representation when pursuing or defending claims against adversarial parties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 15823ae5-3ae0-4c72-85e9-e6dc5d4063bd
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.1.4 - Legal Defense Standard Operational Protocols

This document must define the Standard Operational Protocols \(SOPs\) for reacting against legal or regulatory actions against a participant of the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5649bed1-adcb-408c-b92c-08d96aa26f8d
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.2 - Legal Risk Management

This document defines the framework for managing, retaining, transferring, and structuring legal risk through instruments such as self-insurance and insurance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 545f3cf9-bf9f-4125-acdb-6e0aed805e7f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.2.1 - The Policyholder

The Policyholder is a specialized Ecosystem Actor in charge of executing agreements with external entities with the exclusive purpose of structuring and transferring risk to third parties through instruments such as insurances, reinsurances, mutuals, or other types of arrangements. These instruments will provide extended risk coverage for participants of the Sky Ecosystem and Agents.  
  
The object of the Policyholder is to  
  
• Act as a legal counterparty with insurance brokers, insurance, reinsurance, underwriters, or risk management companies.  
  
• Act as a policyholder of insurance contracts, which will have as beneficiaries participants of the Sky Ecosystem.  
  
• Hire suppliers and contractors necessary for the operation of self-insurance or insurance instruments, such as   
  
◦ The Resilience Technical Committee for claim management   
  
◦ Managers, Directors, and other executive staff of the legal vehicle. The power of directors will be limited to administrative and operative roles.  
  
Sky Governance will have all necessary control mechanisms over the Policyholder:  
  
• Sky Governance can designate and remove Directors, Supervisor, and Committees  
  
• Sky Governance can instruct the entity to act and ratify decisions  
  
• Power of directors is limited to administrative/operative roles  
  
• The Policyholder will not manage Sky's assets nor will be legally affiliated with Sky.  
  
The setup and operational budget of the legal vehicle will be sourced initially from the [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Defense Resources - Resilience Fund Current Budget](https://www.notion.so/e46f169874554c738e1c75962facfe04). This is intended to move later to a separate budget.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 7da1d47f-32e7-41b8-81c5-84bf3f3e3bbb
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.2.2 - Policyholder Management

This document governs the adding and removing of Policyholders. SKY holders must first approve the structure and associated costs of the Policyholder, based on a Governance Poll initiated by Facilitators if they deem it necessary.  
  
The list of currently approved Policyholders is maintained in [A.2.11 - Legal Resilience - Legal Risk Management - Current Active Policyholders](https://www.notion.so/466121b6bb1a4a1a9e8f38804ceeda15).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1dcac073-8399-437d-a3c2-e6431e01d1db
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.10.1.2.3 - Current Active Policyholders

There are currently no active Policyholders.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 466121b6-bb1a-4a1a-9e8f-38804ceeda15
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.3 - Privacy and Operational Security

This document must define policies pertaining to operational security, privacy, and pseudonymity for participants in the Sky Ecosystem. The general purpose of this framework is to maximize security and safety for contributors and users and minimize potential attack vectors for the Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 48299e66-0006-48af-9122-af7f0bae82a8
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.4 - Advocacy And Public Policy

This document defines the framework and processes for public policy, advocacy, and governmental relations. The general purpose of this framework is to develop innovative regulatory frameworks and standards that protect open source resources and position the Public Good Purpose of the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 954444f2-5309-4949-9360-6739e2dc61bd
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.5 - Legal And Regulatory Risk Monitoring

This document defines the general framework and standard processes for monitoring and assessing risk and implementing responses. Risk assessment will include external/jurisdictional risk monitoring and internal risk monitoring.  
  
Responses will be structured as Standard Operational Protocols \(SOPs\). The categories of Legal and Regulatory Risk Monitoring responses are:  
  
• Preventive responses that reduce the likelihood of occurrence of a risk event  
  
• Reactive responses that reduce the severity of consequences if the risk event materializes.  
  
• Emergency Responses and Contingency Plans.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1068ad63-2d09-4129-a747-9261f3c16259
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.6 - Public Procurement Framework

This document defines a Public Procurement Framework for contributors and actors involved in the Sky Ecosystem. The purpose is to develop a standard framework that governs the entire lifecycle of service providers, which includes the following processes:  
  
• Application process  
  
• Selection process \(scoring / evaluating proposals or applications\)  
  
• Hiring and payment process  
  
• Performance evaluation and reporting  
  
• Terminating involvement and resolving disputes

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 4e7b23a6-3fb7-4c2e-8def-fd7748ba48e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.7 - Audit Procedure for Atlas Amendments

This document must define the procedure for performing ex-post legal and technical audits of amended Atlas documents. The general purpose of this procedure is to ensure internal consistency and alignment of the Atlas as a whole.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** bb32958c-55de-4ddc-92a8-36e960aef035
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.8 - Technical And Legal Standards

This document defines the required technical-legal standards and tools such as, but not limited to, legal agreements \([A.2.10](https://www.notion.so/522d3ea538424434979af349d08f8009)\), templates, and legal structures required to perform specific functions or roles in the Sky Ecosystem. The general purpose of this framework is to minimize trust assumptions and dependencies on specific actors, minimize personal exposure, and increase the accountability and predictability in their behavior.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0f2b04d1-7d80-4867-9431-8c6b3967421e
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.10.1.9 - Code And Asset Licenses

Ecosystem Actors should generally release code and assets under the Apache 2.0 license, unless there are specific and clear reasons to use a different license, as determined by the Support Facilitators.  
  
The Support Facilitators must ensure that a Foundation and other appropriate legal entities exists to properly protect intellectual property and trademarks related to the Sky Ecosystem.  
  
Such Foundations or other entities must over time be set up to follow instructions from Sky Governance.  
  
All Ecosystem Actors must take steps to protect the brands and trademarks relevant to the Sky Ecosystem, and in doing so must follow guidelines and best practice defined by the Foundations related to Trademarks, Code and Asset licenses.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 22229615-b337-484e-96c8-95798cb2a151
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

## A.2.11 - Resilience Research and Preparedness

This Article defines infrastructure and processes to support resilience research and legal preparedness.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 32f31a29-1698-4f68-aa6a-f9ba2207609f
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

### A.2.11.1 - Resilience Research And Preparedness

The Support Scope is responsible for conducting ongoing resilience and preparedness research.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** be08e5cd-6928-4794-af1b-e578c45513e1
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

#### A.2.11.1.1 - Role Of Support Facilitators

The Support Facilitators must ensure that resilience research and preparedness efforts are continuously maintained to ensure the ecosystem is well-positioned to handle any legal uncertainty or risk that should arise. These projects must generally be broadly diversified across all jurisdictions where the Sky Ecosystem could be directly or indirectly exposed, but efforts and resources must be prioritized towards jurisdictions where risks are more likely to emerge.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0632e95f-19cc-46ea-ba87-75fa69c43cf1
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.11.1.1.0.3.1 - Resilience Research And Preparedness - Element Annotation

This element refers to activities and studies aimed at strengthening Sky Ecosystem’s ability to respond effectively to legal uncertainties or risks, including monitoring and analysis of legal trends and regulatory changes; scenario planning and risk modeling; and the development of strategies to mitigate potential threats.

**Document Type:** Annotation
**UUID:** f5dced5c-9ade-4f8f-875c-2f37fa83c4c6
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-01

#### A.2.11.1.2 - Budget

The Resilience Research and Preparedness budget is specified in [A.2.12 - Resilience Research And Preparedness - Budget - Current Budget](https://www.notion.so/6bf945c7fc3347c4948e2d048b6d94c7). The Support Facilitators can trigger a payout from the budget to a relevant recipient address through a Governance Poll.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f1a1da04-3e9d-49d2-bbdf-d897b3a262b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-04-21

##### A.2.11.1.2.1 - Current Budget

The Resilience Research and Preparedness budget is  
  
• Up to 2,000,000 USDS available per year.  
  
The full amount is immediately available at the start of the calendar year.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 6bf945c7-fc33-47c4-948e-2d048b6d94c7
