$$  
Rfactor = \\frac{2u\_m - 1}{2u\_m \\left\( u\_{opt}\(\\alpha + 1\) - 1 + \\frac{\\beta u\_{opt}}{slope1} \\right\)}  
$$  
  
The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8045-bb0f-d92f66bdbd02
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.1 - Maximum Profit Utilization Definition

$u_{m}$ is the maximum profit utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8069-8583-d29794368231
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.1.1 - Maximum Profit Utilization Current Value

The current value of $u_{m}$ is 70%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-801f-8a4a-c446a0874991
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.2 - Target Utilization Definition

$u_{opt}$ is the target utilization specified in [A.4.4 - Rate Setting Mechanism](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380d393a5e822bee99089).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-801c-adcc-e368d876251d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3 - Alpha Definition

$\\alpha$ is calculated using the formula:  
  
`alpha = ((SKY Borrow Maximum Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate) / Slope 1) - 1`  
  
The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8044-8271-d5a71683feba
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3.1 - SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition

`SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is the maximum value of the SKY Borrow Rate at 100% Utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-801e-851d-df8f669dec63
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3.1.1 - SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Current Value

The current value of the `SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is 30%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8044-9b14-e16c1338d149
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition

`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate specified in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d73800bb048c401105867e3).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-805b-87e3-e51cc5b802f8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.3.3 - Slope 1 Definition

`Slope 1` is the Slope 1 parameter specified in [A.4.4 - Slope 1](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f184b9fbb0a9c6fdce).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80f5-bbbb-e64d28ff75ed
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.4 - Beta Definition

$\beta$ is a tuning parameter that determines how much profit is made at maximum utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80d0-89ec-c398c0e38e56
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.4.1 - Beta Current Value

The current value of $\beta$ is 100%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8052-9722-e59aa65a5404
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.5.5 - Slope 1 Definition

$slope1$ is the Slope 1 parameter specified in [A.4.4 - Slope 1](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f184b9fbb0a9c6fdce).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8053-84c9-f780e591d3ca
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6 - Utilization Function Definition

`f(Utilization)` is calculated using the formula:  
  
`f(Utilization) = Utilization * ((SKY Borrow Maximum Rate - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Beta) * Utilization + SKY Borrow Minimum Rate - SKY Borrow Rate)`  
  
The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-805c-a553-f691094fd9d5
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6.1 - Utilization Definition

`Utilization` is the utilization as specified in [A.4.4 - Utilization Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f4abffc06c85f8b653).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80d5-8f2d-ef4afa5e0ee1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6.2 - SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition

`SKY Borrow Maximum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Maximum Rate specified in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Maximum Rate Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d73801e851ddf8f669dec63).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8056-b051-d769efa16701
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6.3 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate Definition

`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate specified in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d73800bb048c401105867e3).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-805e-899b-fff5eb340c04
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6.4 - Beta Definition

`Beta` is the beta specified in [A.4.4 - Beta Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380d089ecc398c0e38e56).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80b7-804c-e84d3d019d8e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.2.1.6.5 - SKY Borrow Rate Definition

`SKY Borrow Rate` is the SKY Borrow Rate specified in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380bbb153f232a5aecd3d).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c5-93de-f54cb83f9664
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.2.2 - Parameters Modification

The parameters specified in [A.4.4 - Parameters Definition](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380388dd5c02fec520512) that are set by governance may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80bf-89ee-c0190b8d300d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

##### A.4.4.1.2.3 - stUSDS Holders’ Risk Bearing

Holders of stUSDS accept the risk associated with providing capital for SKY-backed borrowing. In the event that the liquidation of a borrower’s staked SKY collateral does not cover the outstanding debt, stUSDS balances will be subject to a haircut proportional to the shortfall. This mechanism ensures that the Sky Protocol is isolated from losses originating from the SKY-backed borrowing facility.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8004-a906-f7dfdc6da951
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

##### A.4.4.1.2.4 - Debt Ceiling

The maximum amount of USDS that can be borrowed against staked SKY is dynamically determined by, and equal to, the total amount of USDS currently held within the stUSDS contract. This dynamic ceiling will replace the static `DC-IAM` module and parameters associated with protocol-dependent, SKY-backed borrowing.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-803f-89b8-d6049eb54922
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

##### A.4.4.1.2.5 - SKY Borrow Rate

The interest rate charged to borrowers \(SKY Borrow Rate\) is dynamic and market-driven, based on the utilization of funds within the stUSDS contract. This dynamic rate will replace the static `Stability Fee` parameter associated with protocol-dependent, SKY-backed borrowing.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80bb-b153-f232a5aecd3d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.5.1 - Rate Setting Mechanism

The SKY Borrow Rate adjusts to target a 90% utilization rate of the USDS in the stUSDS contract. When stUSDS utilization is below 90%, the rate gradually decreases; when above 90%, it gradually increases. The SKY Borrow Rate cannot fall below the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate. See [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d73800bb048c401105867e3).  
  
The specific parameters and formula governing the rate of adjustment are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80d3-93a5-e822bee99089
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1 - Rate Setting Parameters

The parameters of the rate setting mechanism are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8009-baed-cbe18a85f474
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.1 - Utilization

`Utilization` is the percentage of funds in the stUSDS contract that are used to fund borrowing against staked SKY specified in [A.4.4 - Utilization Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f4abffc06c85f8b653).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-809b-b1b9-f84ec65117f7
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.2 - Target Utilization

`Target Utilization` is the target utilization rate of USDS in the stUSDS contract specified in [A.4.4 - Rate Setting Mechanism](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380d393a5e822bee99089).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8041-8178-df16012883d9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.3 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate

`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate` is the rate that the SKY Borrow Rate cannot fall below specified in [A.4.4 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d73800bb048c401105867e3).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80bd-af91-cbd745b2612e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4 - Slope 1

`Slope 1` represents the spread of the SKY Borrow Rate over the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate when Utilization is at Target Utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80f1-84b9-fbb0a9c6fdce
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4.1 - Slope 1 Current Value

The current value of the `Slope 1` parameter is 12.575%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-806f-90b8-c3805eebf355
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.4.2 - Slope 1 Modification

The `Slope 1` parameter may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8040-b8ce-e33be6cc2d42
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5 - Slope 2

`Slope 2` represents the spread of the SKY Borrow Rate at 100% Utilization over the SKY Borrow Rate at Target Utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80fa-8f4a-c669f7b54393
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5.1 - Slope 2 Current Value

The current value of the `Slope 2` parameter is 12.575%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c8-b0cd-fdcb78ad2414
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5.2 - Slope 2 Modification

The `Slope 2` parameter may be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8003-9354-cdd1164e6ba5
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5.3 - Slope 2 Methodology

Under normal circumstances governance should set the `Slope 2` parameter based on the following formula:  
  
`Slope 2 = Slope 1 * alpha`  
  
The parameters of this formula are specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8034-a902-d7035d2e7e4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5.3.1 - Slope 1 Definition

The `Slope 1` parameter is specified in [A.4.4 - Slope 1](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f184b9fbb0a9c6fdce).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-805f-98a6-d4be1ef32447
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########## A.4.4.1.2.5.1.1.5.3.2 - Alpha Definition

The `alpha` parameter is specified in [A.4.4 - Alpha Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380448271d5a71683feba).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80ef-81a5-c9c1292541b6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.5.1.2 - Rate Setting Formula

The Sky Borrow Rate is calculated according to the following formula when Utilization is less than or equal to Target Utilization:  
  
`SKY Borrow Rate = SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Utilization / Target Utilization * Slope 1`  
  
The SKY Borrow Rate is calculated according to the following formula when Utilization is greater than Target Utilization:  
  
`SKY Borrow Rate = SKY Borrow Minimum Rate + Slope 1 + (Utilization - Target Utilization) / (1 - Target Utilization) * Slope 2`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80ae-b5fb-eb43b02e9aa6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.5.2 - SKY Borrow Minimum Rate

The SKY Borrow Minimum Rate is calculated according to the following formula:  
  
`SKY Borrow Minimum Rate = Sky Savings Rate + stUSDS Accessibility Reward`  
  
The formula ensures the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate covers the baseline cost of capital \(Sky Savings Rate\) plus the incentive cost \(stUSDS Accessibility Reward\), preventing value-draining arbitrage where users could borrow below the Sky Savings Rate.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-800b-b048-c401105867e3
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

####### A.4.4.1.2.5.2.1 - Parameters Definition

The parameters of the SKY Borrow Minimum Rate formula are further defined in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-802d-9b07-faa5492ea510
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-02

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.2.1.1 - Sky Savings Rate Definition

`Sky Savings Rate` is defined in [A.3.2 - Core Stability Parameters - Parameters - Sky Savings Rate](https://www.notion.so/73d9112621c94e8abedf8a51a432bb59).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-802e-b879-c154bab0167b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-02

######## A.4.4.1.2.5.2.1.2 - stUSDS Accessibility Reward Definition

`stUSDS Accessibility Reward` is defined in [A.4.4 - stUSDS Accessibility Reward](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380748c32fe879d75d790).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80a4-95bb-d48ce1f5bd71
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-19

##### A.4.4.1.2.6 - stUSDS Risk Parameters

The liquidation parameters for SKY-backed loans funded via stUSDS are:  
   
◦ Liquidation Ratio: 125%      
◦ `Calc`: StairstepExponentialDecrease      
◦ `Tau`: 0 days  
◦ `Tolerance`: 0.5  
◦ `Cut`: 0.99  
◦ `Step`: 60 seconds  
◦ `Buf`: 120%      
◦ `Cusp`: 40%  
◦ `Tail`: 6,000 seconds  
◦ `Chip`: 0.1%  
◦ `Stopped`: 3  
◦ `Tip`: 300 USDS      
◦ `Chop`: 13%      
◦ `Hole`: 250,000      
◦ `Dust`: 30,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80aa-a679-d848a9506be0
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.6.1 - Modification Of stUSDS Risk Parameters

Except as specified in [A.4.4 - Modification Of stUSDS Auction Parameters](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380998bd7cc95abf8d495), the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors, have the ability to modify any of the parameters defined in [A.4.4 - stUSDS Risk Parameters](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380aaa679d848a9506be0). The modification of said parameters is pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without requiring a Governance Poll.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8008-9fc4-c262ed22f791
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.6.1.1 - Modification Of stUSDS Auction Parameters

A clear justification and analysis must be provided to validate any proposed changes to the `Calc`, `Tau`, `Buf`, `Cusp`, `Tail`, `Chip`, `Tip`, `Chop`, or `Hole` parameters. Before these changes are added to an Executive Vote, the Core Executor Agents must first propose a Governance Poll. However, in an emergency, the Core Executor Agents have the authority to bypass the Governance Poll and add the proposed parameters directly to an Executive Vote. These parameters must be regularly monitored and updated if needed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8099-8bd7-cc95abf8d495
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

##### A.4.4.1.2.7 - stUSDS Accessibility Reward

The stUSDS Accessibility Reward is an incentive mechanism, similar to the USDS Accessibility Reward, designed to encourage Prime Agents and Integrators to promote stUSDS adoption. See [A.2.4 - Accessibility Reward Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d7380b4873cec532edc750c). It is calculated as a percentage of the stUSDS balance associated with a Reward Code. The reward is initially set at 0.05% for the Integrator portion and 0.05% for the Prime Agent Management Fee, totaling 0.1%.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8074-8c32-fe879d75d790
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

##### A.4.4.1.2.8 - stUSDS Bounded External Access Module

The stUSDS Bounded External Access Module \(stUSDS BEAM\) enables designated, Governance-whitelisted operators to adjust the stUSDS Rate \(`str`\), the SKY Borrow Rate \(`duty`\), the maximum amount that users can deposit into the stUSDS contract \(`cap`\), and the maximum Debt Ceiling \(`line`\). Adjustments are governed by the stUSDS BEAM smart contract logic and specific parameters set by Sky Governance. stUSDS BEAM holds four parameters that can be set for each stUSDS parameter: \(i\) `min`, \(ii\) `max`, \(iii\) `step`, and \(iv\) `tau`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8043-9ce4-ee69a1cb4078
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.8.1 - Definitions

The documents herein define the parameters of the stUSDS BEAM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80e4-92f9-d73d204e4aba
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.1 - Min Definition

The `min` parameter defines the minimum value for the `str` and `duty` parameters that can be set using the stUSDS BEAM. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `min`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80da-8abb-cf40b1266d95
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.2 - Max Definition

The `max` parameter defines the maximum value for the `str` and `duty` parameters that can be set using the stUSDS BEAM. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `max`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80cf-accf-e0c17a6caa69
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.3 - Step Definition

The `step` parameter limits how much the `str` and `duty` parameters can be increased or decreased in a single transaction, bound by the `tau` parameter. Each of the `str` and `duty` parameters has a specific `step`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8083-a22d-d62dc1524d35
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.4 - Max Cap Definition

The `maxCap` parameter defines the maximum value for the `cap` parameter that can be set by the stUSDS BEAM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-807e-9bb6-f9ce20ec8061
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.1.4.1 - Max Cap Current Value

The `maxCap` is currently set to 1,000,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c1-a9e1-df14311d7e3f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.5 - Max Line Definition

The `maxLine` parameter defines the maximum value for the `line` parameter that can be set by the stUSDS BEAM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8028-9932-fd240f4f67e2
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.1.5.1 - Max Line Current Value

The `maxLine` is currently set to 1,000,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8083-8bea-fd0c35392bed
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.1.6 - Tau Definition

The `tau` parameter defines the minimum time interval, in seconds, that must elapse between consecutive uses or operations of the stUSDS BEAM.  
  
A stUSDS BEAM operation may adjust one or more parameters. Once a stUSDS BEAM operation is executed, the `tau` duration must expire before any subsequent stUSDS BEAM operation can be performed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-807e-aec8-c82da5e8e3de
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.1.6.1 - Tau Current Value

The `tau` is currently set to 57,600 seconds \(16 hours\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c4-acb0-f7e0cceb9ff0
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.8.2 - stUSDS Parameters

The stUSDS BEAM parameters for each stUSDS parameter set by the stUSDS BEAM are defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80f5-908c-dec1616e623f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.2.1 - Str Parameters

The stUSDS BEAM parameters for the `str` stUSDS parameter are as follows:  
      
◦ `max` - 5,000 basis points,      
◦ `min` - 200 basis points,      
◦ `step` - 4,000 basis points.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80e5-972c-f8e6a8c86f05
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.2.2 - Duty Parameters

The stUSDS BEAM parameters for the `duty` stUSDS parameter are as follows:  
      
◦ `max` - 5,000 basis points,  
◦ `min` - 210 basis points,      
◦ `step` - 4,000 basis points.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8060-ba9a-cb2cc94151aa
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

###### A.4.4.1.2.8.3 - Parameter Adjustments

All stUSDS BEAM parameters can be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c4-90cc-fe6d8a46a833
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1 - Operators

The stUSDS BEAM Operators are whitelisted entities that can directly alter the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM. Changes to stUSDS parameters are limited by the `max`, `min`, `step`, and `tau` parameters. Operators can be added or removed by an Executive Vote.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-806d-9b57-c4cd79874347
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1 - Operator Multisig

The Operator Multisig is an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM and is controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8041-89bb-d834fa293027
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1.1 - Operator Multisig Address

The address of the Operator Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xBB865F94B8A92E57f79fCc89Dfd4dcf0D3fDEA16`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-807c-b78b-ebf96b4019d0
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1.2 - Operator Multisig Required Number Of Signers

The Operator Multisig currently has a 2/3 signing requirement.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8025-afb7-f086256e356f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1.3 - Operator Multisig Signers

The signers of the Operator Multisig are three \(3\) addresses controlled by Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8067-87f2-f5f410fe24bb
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1.4 - Operator Multisig Usage Standards

The signers of the Operator Multisig must use the multisig to operate the stUSDS BEAM in accordance with the instructions specified in [A.4.4 - Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380ec8eb9c62e675ec49d).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8009-8671-e9de0b2b5ee9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.1.5 - Operator Multisig Modification

Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis can change the signers of the Operator Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least three \(3\) signers and at least a majority of signers are required to execute transactions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-804e-9a91-ddb2b7a125a1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.2 - Operator Hot Wallet

The Operator Hot Wallet is a hot wallet controlled by a bot that will update stUSDS parameters on an automated basis as specified in [A.4.4 - Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380ab8b19d7585b5c5411). The wallet is controlled by Ecosystem Actor TechOps Services under the supervision of Core Executor Agent Atlas Axis in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. The addition of the Hot Wallet as an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM is authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8029-b457-c524a73fe9a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-19

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.2.1 - Operator Hot Wallet Address

The address of the Operator Hot Wallet on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xd06C14820048de2Fb7c9de611EcFdaCE18eC8896`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80b8-9a50-cabcb71bc772
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.2.2 - Update Of stUSDS Parameters For Hot Wallet

When the Operator Hot Wallet is added as an Operator of the stUSDS Rate, the stUSDS BEAM parameters must be updated as follows:  
      
◦ The `step` parameters for the `str` and `duty` parameters must be reduced to 400 basis points; and      
◦ The `tau` parameter must be reduced to 4 hours.  
  
These changes are authorized to proceed directly to an Executive Vote without a prior Governance Poll.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8097-8a33-f11f1e3805f8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.1.3 - Operator Update Process

stUSDS BEAM Operators can be modified by the Core Executor Agents, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors. This process will be conducted through the Operational Weekly Governance Cycle or, if necessary, through out-of-schedule Executive Votes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8062-996b-d6999b3ccf63
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2 - Update Process

The stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM are managed by the stUSDS BEAM Operators as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8067-b755-d263466e9ebb
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.1 - Initial Parameter Values Set In Executive Vote

The initial parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM in the Executive Vote deploying stUSDS and the stUSDS BEAM are:  
      
◦ `str` - 0 basis points      
◦ `duty` - 2,000 basis points      
◦ `cap` - 200,000,000 USDS      
◦ `line` - 200,000,000 USDS

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8033-be47-effd642b79c6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2 - Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig

Initially, the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM are managed by the Operator Multisig based on instructions provided by the Stability Scope Advisors.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80ec-8eb9-c62e675ec49d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2.1 - Request By Stability Scope Advisors

Any requests to change stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM must be posted to the Sky Forum by the Stability Scope Advisor recommending the change. Once Utilization is below 100% the Stability Scope Advisors should begin recommending changes based on the methodology specified in [A.4.4 - Update Methodology](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380c79f97d68432bd154d) but may deviate from the values resulting from this methodology if they determine that other values are advisable to support the growth of stUSDS and the interests of the Sky Protocol.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8043-8555-f0bdc56bb32e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2.2 - Operator Execution

Once a request to change stUSDS parameters has been posted to the Sky Forum by the Stability Scope Advisors, the stUSDS BEAM Operators must prepare and execute the proposed changes in a timely manner. Preparation can include the creation of transaction simulations to verify inputs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c7-b706-e3fcf8115095
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.2.3 - Public Communication

Once a change in stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM has been executed, the execution must be publicly communicated to the Sky Ecosystem. This communication may take the form of either \(1\) a Forum post or \(2\) inclusion of the execution in an informational dashboard that allows community members to see each stUSDS parameter change executed by the stUSDS BEAM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8052-90ef-ed737b2dfe0e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.2.3 - Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet

Once the Operator Hot Wallet has been added as an Operator of the stUSDS BEAM \(see [A.4.4 - Operator Hot Wallet](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d738029b457c524a73fe9a6)\), the stUSDS parameters set by the stUSDS BEAM must be set by the Operator Hot Wallet. These changes must be based on the update methodology specified in [A.4.4 - Update Methodology](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380c79f97d68432bd154d) and the results must be publicly visible on an information dashboard.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80ab-8b19-d7585b5c5411
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

####### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3 - Update Methodology

The documents herein define the methodology that should be used for determining when and how to update stUSDS parameters with the stUSDS BEAM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c7-9f97-d68432bd154d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.1 - Short Term Process

Initially when stUSDS parameters are set manually by the Operator Multisig \(see [A.4.4 - Manual Parameter Updates By Operator Multisig](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380ec8eb9c62e675ec49d)\), the Stability Scope Advisors may deviate from the long term process specified in [A.4.4 - Long Term Process](https://www.notion.so/272f2ff08d73809b8e49d23cfe279213) in their best judgment based on the guidelines specified herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80d4-a078-c4950602d153
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.1.1 - Initial Supply Rate

The initial value of the `str` parameter must be set extraordinarily high to a value of approximately 40% initially to incentivize deposits. This rate is not sustainable and must be lowered as specified in [A.4.4 - Gradual Reduction In Supply Rate](https://www.notion.so/272f2ff08d7380cea8f8fd76179aa2f4).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-802d-83b3-e18a635b5d25
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.1.2 - Gradual Reduction In Supply Rate

The `str` parameter must be gradually lowered to a more sustainable level as the market approaches the optimal target of 90% utilization.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80ce-a8f8-fd76179aa2f4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.1.3 - Growth Of Market Size

The `cap` and `line` parameters must be scaled up over time. By the time the market reaches 300,000,000 USDS the `str` parameter must be normalized to a level that is in line with the Interest Rate Model.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80c7-9cb0-e78efe7ff2b9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2 - Long Term Process

Once the process is fully automated \(see [A.4.4 - Automatic Updates By Operator Hot Wallet](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380ab8b19d7585b5c5411)\) the automated process must fully conform to the specifications herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-809b-8e49-d23cfe279213
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.1 - Conditions For Update

The stUSDS parameters should be updated if \(1\) the current Utilization \(see [A.4.4 - Utilization Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380f4abffc06c85f8b653)\) deviates from the Utilization as of the last time the stUSDS parameters were set by more than 2.4% and \(2\) the time since the last update is greater than `tau` \(see [A.4.4 - Tau Definition](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d73807eaec8c82da5e8e3de)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-8074-9711-e84dcaefe6b4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

######### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2 - Calculations For Update

The new values for each stUSDS parameter should be set to be as close as possible to the values specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80b4-8f99-e6245aa7426a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

########## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.1 - Str Calculation

The `str` must be calculated as specified in [A.4.4 - stUSDS Rate](https://www.notion.so/1e7f2ff08d7380d6975ad47540a0e064).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8040-a37a-c0dedac291ab
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

########## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.2 - Duty Calculation

The `duty` must be calculated as specified in [A.4.4 - Rate Setting Formula](https://www.notion.so/264f2ff08d7380aeb5fbeb43b02e9aa6).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-804c-b6b3-c534b53c94b3
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

########## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.3 - Cap Calculation

The Stability Scope Advisors must calculate the maximum amount that users can deposit into the stUSDS contract \(`cap`\) as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80a0-9f2e-ee8da5ecf61e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

########### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.3.1 - Short Term Calculation

In the short term while Utilization is above 100%, the `cap` must be set to 200,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8093-b46b-dac270d44e5a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.3.2 - Long Term Calculation

In the long term, the `cap` must be gradually increased when Utilization is above 85% according to the following formula:  
  
`cap = 1.2 * current SKY borrowing`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8082-b299-e9b8513e9c24
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########## A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.4 - Line Calculation

The Stability Scope Advisors must calculate the maximum amount that users can borrow against their staked SKY \(`line`\) as specified in the documents herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8056-8185-de34edafd583
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

########### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.4.1 - Short Term Calculation

In the short term while Utilization is above 100%, the `line` must be set to 200,000,000 USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-80c4-bb9a-deb0f653b48f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

########### A.4.4.1.2.8.3.3.2.2.4.2 - Long Term Calculation

In the long term, the `line` must be gradually increased when Utilization is above 85% according to the following formula:  
  
`line = 1.14 * current SKY borrowing`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 264f2ff0-8d73-8085-a110-da82dc263de1
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-04

#### A.4.4.1.3 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures

The documents herein define short-term logic for SKY staking rewards and SKY-backed borrowing pending the implementation of the Sky Treasury Management Function and the stUSDS system. Short-term logic regarding MKR staking rewards and MKR-backed borrowing is also included herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8027-9679-f0fb0913e9ab
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

##### A.4.4.1.3.1 - Short-Term SKY Staking Rewards

USDS rewards for SKY stakers will be enabled as specified in [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc). Until the Treasury Management Function \(TMF\) is live, USDS rewards for SKY stakers will be temporarily funded by the Smart Burn Engine; this interim mechanism will be discontinued once the TMF becomes operational.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-808a-9de1-eb71cbd2e550
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-12

###### A.4.4.1.3.1.1 - SKY Staking Rewards Parameters

When SKY Staking launches, the short-term Staking Rewards parameters will be specified in this document pursuant to [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Update Smart Burn Engine Parameters](https://www.notion.so/1f1f2ff08d7380a09f8dd276dc25010b).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8010-b98f-d17baade94cb
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-20

##### A.4.4.1.3.2 - Short-Term SKY-Backed Borrowing

SKY-backed borrowing will be enabled as specified in [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc). In the short term, pending the development of the stUSDS system, SKY-backed borrowing will be funded directly by Sky. This funding must be replaced by the stUSDS system as soon as the stUSDS system is developed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-8028-b2ca-de14f0326b82
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-07

###### A.4.4.1.3.2.1 - SKY-Backed Borrowing Parameters

When SKY Staking launches, the risk parameters for SKY-backed borrowing funded directly by Sky will be specified in this document pursuant to [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Launch SKY Staking](https://www.notion.so/1f1f2ff08d73800b85d2e16438f6b21d) and [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - Update Liquidation Parameters For Borrowing Against Staked SKY](https://www.notion.so/1f1f2ff08d7380cea2f6e201edad4c30).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1e7f2ff0-8d73-80a2-aca1-f1036163aab6
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-20

##### A.4.4.1.3.3 - Short-Term MKR Staking Rewards

As the legacy governance token of the Sky Protocol, MKR stakers are currently eligible for USDS rewards. This functionality will be phased out as specified in[A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc). The documents herein define the parameters for USDS rewards to MKR stakers during this interim period.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8003-95a9-ec9a730bb16d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-12

###### A.4.4.1.3.3.1 - MKR Staking Rewards Parameters

The short-term staking rewards parameters are:  
      
◦ rewardsDistribution: Set to the Splitter  
◦ rewardsDuration: 15,649 seconds  
◦ farms \(Whitelisted set of farms to choose from\): LsMkrUsdsFarm  
◦ fee \(Exit fee\): 0%

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8056-9501-c315eb79364a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-12

##### A.4.4.1.3.4 - Short-Term MKR-Backed Borrowing

Borrowing against staked MKR is currently supported and funded directly by Sky. This functionality will be phased out as specified in [A.4.1 - Core Tokens - SKY - MKR To SKY Upgrade](https://www.notion.so/d5af8504ddd6416d8429897497b072dc). The documents herein define the risk parameters for MKR-backed borrowing during this interim period.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8013-8eee-d5d5cdc81913
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-12

###### A.4.4.1.3.4.1 - MKR-Backed Borrowing Parameters

The current risk parameters for MKR-backed borrowing funded directly by Sky are:  
      
◦ Cut: 0.99     
◦ Step: 60 seconds  
◦ Buf: 1.20      
◦ Cusp: 0.40      
◦ Tail: 6,000 seconds      
◦ Chip: 0.1%      
◦ Tip: 300 DAI      
◦ Calc: StairstepExponentialDecrease      
◦ Ilk.chop: 8%      
◦ Tolerance: 0.5      
◦ Dust: 30,000 DAI      
◦ Ilk.hole: 3M DAI      
◦ Stability Fee: 20%  
◦ spotter\[ilk\].mat \(Liquidation Ratio\): 125%      
◦ DC-IAM line: 45M      
◦ DC-IAM gap: 45M      
◦ DC-IAM ttl: 30 minutes

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1f1f2ff0-8d73-8010-a684-cd88d60b8461
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-12

## A.4.5 - Distribution Of Agent Tokens

When Sky invests capital in Agents, the tokens Sky receives in exchange are distributed in accordance with the terms of the Ecosystem Accord between Sky and the respective Agent.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 9a8207f2-641f-4992-adc5-0d0cf7b45bc1
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

## A.4.6 - Protocol Mechanisms

This Article defines maintenance or housekeeping mechanisms and contracts used to administer the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-804c-968b-ed5113924371
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

### A.4.6.1 -  Token Transfers To Sky

This Section defines standard procedures for sending tokens to Sky.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80cf-b835-c107624da132
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

#### A.4.6.1.1 - Process for Returning Tokens To Sky When No Specific Process Exists

The documents herein define processes for sending tokens to Sky. These include the DssBlow2 contract for adding Dai and USDS to the Surplus Buffer, and the Pause Proxy contract for non-stablecoins.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-801b-8dc9-dee0bdc23785
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

##### A.4.6.1.1.1 - Transfer Of Stablecoins To Sky Protocol

The documents herein define the instructions for transferring stablecoins to Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80a8-9c2d-c92138b3af19
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

###### A.4.6.1.1.1.1 - Send Dai Or USDS To DssBlow2 Contract

Transfers of Dai or USDS that are not required to pay down vault debt should result in an increase to the Sky Protocol Surplus Buffer \(see [A.3.6 - A1 - Surplus Buffer And Smart Burn Engine - Surplus Buffer](https://www.notion.so/11af2ff08d73806aafb0ca38393197d3)\). This process is handled via the DssBlow2 contract, as described in the subdocuments.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-8031-8223-d2659dd80c82
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.4.6.1.1.1.1.1 - DssBlow2

Dai or USDS tokens can be transferred to the DssBlow2 contract, MCD\_BLOW2, at `0x81EFc7Dd25241acd8E5620F177E42F4857A02B79`. Calling the `blow` function on this contract will cause any Dai or USDS tokens held by it to be added to the Surplus Buffer. During this process, the ERC-20 tokens are burned and the tokens are instead reflected as an internal balance in the MCD\_VAT contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80de-a773-d3a2d519ec4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-19

######## A.4.6.1.1.1.1.1.1 - Unrecoverability Of Non-Dai Or USDS Tokens

Only Dai or USDS are supported by DssBlow2. Any other tokens sent to DssBlow2 are not recoverable by Sky. Senders bear full responsibility; such transfers are not considered a valid receipt of funds owed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-806d-a9f4-f62327e8d23f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

###### A.4.6.1.1.1.2 - Transfer Of Other Stablecoins

Stablecoins other than Dai or USDS cannot be added to the Surplus Buffer via DssBlow2. They should either \(1\) be converted to Dai or USDS and sent to DssBlow2 or \(2\) be sent to the Pause Proxy contract without conversion. Receipt of Dai or USDS is Sky’s preferred method of receiving stablecoins.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-8099-b223-ff3bc9499f06
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

##### A.4.6.1.1.2 - Transfer Of Non-Stablecoins To Sky Protocol

The documents herein define the instructions for transferring non-stablecoins to Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80d5-b205-c4a0537c2725
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

###### A.4.6.1.1.2.1 - Send Non-Stablecoins To Pause Proxy

Transfers of non-stablecoins to Sky should be sent to the Pause Proxy contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80fd-8dff-db3527b13878
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

####### A.4.6.1.1.2.1.1 - Pause Proxy

The Pause Proxy Contract, MCD\_PAUSE\_PROXY, at `0xbe8e3e3618f7474f8cb1d074a26affef007e98fb`, is under the direct control of Sky Governance. Sky Governance may take actions through Executive Votes to interact with the tokens held by the Pause Proxy, including selling these tokens.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 272f2ff0-8d73-80b6-91aa-fbbc5b2d1121
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

# A.5 - The Accessibility Scope

The Accessibility Scope governs accessibility and distribution efforts, and regulates user-facing frontends.

**Document Type:** Scope
**UUID:** 9e3f76e6-3343-4e70-af0b-c914be2e8d5a
**Last Modified:** 2024-08-12

## A.5.1 - Brand Identity

This Article governs the brand identity of Sky.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** f20f79e8-3c47-4983-891a-3897d15f16c0
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-01

### A.5.1.1 - Brand Identity

This Section defines the management and use of Sky’s brand identity.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** bbc1a91c-c693-43f0-99d0-03f646e1e403
**Last Modified:** 2024-11-03

#### A.5.1.1.1 - Website And Domain

The IP rights of the Sky website must be transferred to an entity similar to the Dai Foundation.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f297c216-f0ef-40b6-84c1-55a8f71b0b52
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

#### A.5.1.1.2 - Dai

Dai must remain as a valid token and product, with no actively maintained brand presence beyond community assets, educational material and its token name.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5e5f6259-2cc0-48e4-b33e-bf7da3d03985
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-20

## A.5.2 - Accessibility Communication Channels

This Article regulates accessibility assets, including communication channels and Sky’s communication presence on external websites.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** f3f46c23-9197-486e-b0ab-2206017541ff
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-01

### A.5.2.1 - Accessibility Communication Channels

This Section defines rules for managing accessible communication channels to enhance public access to, and interaction with, the Sky Ecosystem. These communication channels are distinct from Sky’s governance-focused communication channels.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 2839403c-d7ff-4188-9903-412d40f7d2e7
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-25

#### A.5.2.1.1 - External Platforms

Sky must support the accessibility of the Sky Ecosystem by paying Ecosystem Actors to maintain accounts and channels on external platforms, such as Twitter and Telegram. These accounts may develop and share Accessibility content that follows the brand guidelines.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** f32fca2b-8a67-447b-b28d-07d6317a4895
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-25

#### A.5.2.1.2 - Moderation

Accessibility Communication channels are subject to the moderation policies specified in [A.2.9 - Ecosystem Communication Channels - Moderation](https://www.notion.so/f8b2257042644fdaa79323a1e3661da1).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 21ef2ff0-8d73-8043-80b2-d1597da3d5e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

#### A.5.2.1.3 - Budget

The Accessibility communication channel budget is available to maintain the tasks described in [A.5.4 - A1 - Accessibility Communication Channels](https://www.notion.so/2839403cd7ff41889903412d40f7d2e7) and its subdocuments.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0f22173b-3ea4-4a40-8ece-d1ad822b68a0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-26

##### A.5.2.1.3.1 - Amount

The Accessibility communication channel budget is:  
  
• 0 USDS per month, implemented with DssVest.   
  
It is a monthly recurring budget.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 47062226-43b9-4e47-a9d4-6792bcce256b
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.2.1.3.2 - Modification

The Accessibility Facilitator can propose to modify the budget by posting a message in the Sky Core Forum specifying the proposed new value. This triggers a Governance Poll in the Weekly Governance Cycle that edits the value if it is accepted.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0fa1bd7f-155a-46fe-acf1-d6a655a1eb29
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-01

## A.5.3 - Accessibility Campaigns

This Article regulates accessibility campaigns.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 2ee10f81-20c3-4768-b899-53e6ceef540e
**Last Modified:** 2025-01-14

### A.5.3.1 - Accessibility Campaigns

This Section defines infrastructure and processes pertaining to accessibility campaigns.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 72d79ea1-3c87-4a62-8aa7-8364c24e4f56
**Last Modified:** 2024-12-02

#### A.5.3.1.1 - Accessibility Facilitators Responsibility

The Accessibility Facilitators must research and develop a framework for long-term Accessibility Campaigns.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** af665205-baa4-468b-b3d7-cb5ac38531b6
**Last Modified:** 2025-01-14

#### A.5.3.1.2 - Early Bird Reward

As a part of the brand reveal phase, the Accessibility Facilitators set up an Early Bird Reward System. The Early Bird Reward System rewarded all users signing up before the launch date of USDS and SKY with double the SKY rewards for the first month following launch.  
  
The accumulated SKY rewards were paid out as an airdrop as specified in [A.5.5 - Accessibility Campaigns - Early Bird Reward - Token Distribution](https://www.notion.so/122f2ff08d73809facffe36e38619a53).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 13cf2ff0-8d73-80c3-b6a7-f4374f59f8ca
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-14

##### A.5.3.1.2.1 - Token Distribution

To distribute the Early Bird Reward the following actions have been or will be taken:  
  
• 27,222,832.8 newly minted SKY, equal to 120% of the total estimated Early Bird Reward distributions, was transferred to the multisig wallet operated by the Accessibility Facilitators at the address `0x14D98650d46BF7679BBD05D4f615A1547C87Bf68` on the Ethereum Mainnet.  
  
• The remaining SKY in the `0x14D98650d46BF7679BBD05D4f615A1547C87Bf68` multisig that is not used to fund the MerkleDistributor will be burned by calling the `burn` function on the multisig. The estimated amount of SKY needed to fund the MerkleDistributor is 22,685,694.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 122f2ff0-8d73-809f-acff-e36e38619a53
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-14

## A.5.4 - Location Resilience

This Article defines the location-filtering rules applicable to Ecosystem Actors that operate Frontends on behalf of Agents; and Ecosystem Actors that operate Frontends and receive Accessibility Rewards.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** d788d60f-be4b-4b93-aa08-d3935081c8e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

### A.5.4.1 - Location Resilience

This Section defines requirements for Ecosystem Actors and Agents to implement limited filtering and full blocking of IPs.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** c16b1bfc-770d-4b1a-9e20-b462b0313607
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

#### A.5.4.1.1 - Reduce Exposure To Locations

Ecosystem Actors in the Sky Ecosystem must actively explore and implement reasonably available options to reduce their infrastructure's exposure to locations identified in the following subdocuments as subject to either IP filtering \("limited filtering"\) or IP blocking \("full block"\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8ada4881-662c-485a-9183-bf865d616863
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

#### A.5.4.1.2 - Consequence For Non-Compliance

All frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors on behalf of Agents are required to follow the location-filtering rules defined in [A.5.8 - A1 - Location Resilience](https://www.notion.so/c16b1bfc770d4b1a9e20b462b0313607) and its subdocuments. The failure to adhere to these rules will result in penalties.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8a08d30d-dc7d-4c8a-abd6-fa4eb09db972
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

#### A.5.4.1.3 - Limited Filtering Of Features & IPs

Limited filtering requires frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors to avoid displaying or describing features to users with IPs flagged for limited filtering.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d28a2385-10dc-4ae6-a70d-99a813c260bd
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.4.1.3.1 - Restricted Features

The features that must be restricted from being displayed or described to users flagged for Limited Filtering are:  
  
• Staking Rewards  
  
• Sky Savings Rate  
  
• Any other feature related to yield or rewards.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 5dfe650f-40de-4f4a-8ce5-a5813638cda5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-02

##### A.5.4.1.3.2 - Flagged IPs

Frontends must develop internal processes to determine their strategy for limited filtering, to ensure optimal balance of resilience and accessibility.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** d3bf5367-a03b-455a-b3de-82993d9a86b0
**Last Modified:** 2024-12-02

#### A.5.4.1.4 - Full Block

Full block requires frontends operated by Ecosystem Actors to deny service of any kind to users with IPs flagged for Full Block.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 266268d5-f9a8-4623-9026-dfe0c09d7b81
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.4.1.4.1 - Flagged IPs

User IPs flagged for Full Block are:  
  
• Cuba  
  
• Iran  
  
• Syria  
  
• North Korea  
  
• Crimea and Sevastopol  
  
• Donetsk People’s Republic  
  
• Luhansk People’s Republic of Ukraine  
  
• Kherson Oblast  
  
• Zaporizhzhia Oblast

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 0e20f7b6-a20f-48b5-896b-c17946b30e0a
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

## A.5.5 - Launch Project

The Launch Project is a temporary bootstrapping effort to support the deployment of Sky’s early launch phases. This Article defines the infrastructure and processes related to the Launch Project, including its objectives and budget.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 1f433d9d-7cdb-406f-b7e8-f9bc4855eb77
**Last Modified:** 2024-11-26

### A.5.5.1 - Launch Project

This Section defines high-level objectives and requirements for the Launch Project. The Launch Project budget and the provisions for payout are defined in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 19cee01a-4355-4500-ba94-96bf5a578e7c
**Last Modified:** 2024-12-04

#### A.5.5.1.1 - Beta Launch

The Beta Launch is a crucial milestone in the Launch Project, marking the time at which three key elements will be unveiled: the new brand, USDS and SKY.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** b08b4730-3320-40a9-8f48-13f683810284
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

#### A.5.5.1.2 - Objectives

The subdocuments herein specify the Launch Project’s objectives.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1acd3abb-84d0-4446-8b03-9c56ff456c11
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.1 - Coordinate Development

The Launch Project must coordinate the development of the new brand with website, marketing and communications strategy. The Launch Project must also effectively integrate the ecosystem’s smart contract development into the launch.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 16a6150f-43f5-43a8-870b-3d9e36700a76
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.2 - Develop Technology

The Launch Project must develop key AI and governance technology, and secure partnerships with high-value companies in relevant fields to rapidly anchor Sky’s ability to leverage AI and governance technology.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c5e98c1-81e7-439f-afd2-d33e9df69321
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.3 - Attract High-Value Actors

The Launch Project must work to attract high-value actors who are less traditionally prone to interact with the world of crypto.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** ac7db792-ac88-4333-ab4e-8070d380c739
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.4 - Regulatory Outreach

The Launch Project may also deal with various initiatives related to regulatory outreach or legal resilience efforts in the Sky Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 3e88b92c-3b0e-4971-8140-48bfd623fd16
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.5 - Fund Infrastructure

The Launch Project must fund security and other critical infrastructure needs for the Ecosystem as required during the bootstrapping phase.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** ea2009df-85d7-44f7-a4e1-e6e754185b6f
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.6 - Purpose System

The Launch Project must explore options to help bootstrap the Purpose System and may request governance votes to allocate SKY from the Pause Proxy as grants or donations to relevant causes. Such payments must be approved through a SKY vote. Any approved payments will be funded separately; that is, they will not count against the Launch Project's budget.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** a853bef2-c3ed-48d9-a718-8f8e8411d2e3
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.2.7 - Launch Season Work

The Launch Project must prepare for its dissolution and replacement, following Beta Launch. Once the new brand has been revealed, significant transparency improvements for the Launch Project must also be implemented.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 72081a46-35bb-4586-beed-2ed6e17b6d91
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

#### A.5.5.1.3 - Long Term Focus

The Launch Project's long-term focus must be on coordinating effective marketing and launch processes around the next launch phases; using communications and marketing to prepare the community for the potential of increased governance participation; and driving growth to the Ecosystem, Agents, and Sky Governance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** cca5b9d9-17e9-4640-8c9d-a941524cac23
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

#### A.5.5.1.4 - Transparency Exception

The Launch Project is a one-time exception to the requirements for maximum end-to-end transparency for all governance processes, Facilitator decisions, and budget spending defined by the Atlas. Due to the unique opportunity presented by the new brand and its well-coordinated release alongside the Beta Launch, the Launch Project must optimize for creating the best possible first impression, given its potential to have an outsized impact on the long-term success of the entire Ecosystem.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** cd3bfc90-a42a-4861-8c8a-2629bc2828be
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.4.1 - After Beta Launch

The Launch Project must follow a higher standard of transparency after the Beta Launch, when the first impression of the new brand and the overall launch process has already been set.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 31a88a75-138b-447b-964a-5bf8c9233f23
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-15

#### A.5.5.1.5 - Include Non-Crypto Key Partners

The Launch Project must engage key non-crypto partners and, during the earliest phases, shield them from the unfamiliar challenges unique to decentralized governance and politics. This approach will enable them to participate successfully in the Beta Launch and gain a clear understanding of the system without unnecessary distractions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 54dc3d88-03ae-49a9-b20a-6734bdfa8365
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

#### A.5.5.1.6 - Budget

The budget available for the Launch Project is specified in [A.5.9 - Launch Project - Budget - Amount](https://www.notion.so/8031238c017e4019a05b816db4886e2d). This is a one-time budget with no renewal. Payouts from the budget can be triggered by the Accessibility Facilitator through a message in the Sky Core Forum, specifying the amount for lump-sum payments or the DssVest parameters for DssVest payments, along with the recipient address.   
  
The payment is bundled directly into the next Executive Vote without requiring a Governance Poll. Each time a payout is made, the remaining budget in [A.5.9 - Current Remaining Amount](https://www.notion.so/e54a74d00cb14df3900cb7e2f3e4f101) must be reduced to reflect the expenditure. Spending cannot exceed the total one-time budget.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8a88d69e-33cf-459f-8e1c-de4ac965d76b
**Last Modified:** 2024-09-02

##### A.5.5.1.6.1 - Amount

Initial one-time budget for the Launch Project:  
  
• 80,000,000 USDS  
  
• 360,000,000 SKY - The SKY can at most be spent at the rate of 48,000,000 SKY per month.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 8031238c-017e-4019-a05b-816db4886e2d
**Last Modified:** 2024-12-02

#### A.5.5.1.8 - Sky On Solana Short Term Crosschain Strategy

The Accessibility Facilitators must work with Advisors and Ecosystem Actors to research and implement a decentralized crosschain solution using Wormhole that enables the bridging of USDS to Solana.  
  
Once the crosschain solution is implemented, the Accessibility Facilitators must use the USDS integration boost system to encourage integration and adoption of USDS on Solana.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 107f2ff0-8d73-80ac-a765-d155988eeaa8
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-14

#### A.5.5.1.9 - Sky Ecosystem Liquidity Bootstrapping

The Accessibility Facilitators must work with advisors and Ecosystem Actors to implement a liquidity bootstrapping program using up to 20 million USDS and 320 million SKY. These assets can be used for liquidity programs, or for liquidity infrastructure integration costs. Anything leftover from usage in liquidity programs must be returned to Sky control at the end of the bootstrapping program.  
  
The funds can be paid into multisig accounts for future use via executive votes at will by the Accessibility Facilitators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 109f2ff0-8d73-8029-baba-da835b70d03e
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-14

#### A.5.5.1.7 - Budget Remaining Amount

The remaining budget for the Launch Project is defined as Active Data  in [A.5.9 - Current Remaining Amount](https://www.notion.so/e54a74d00cb14df3900cb7e2f3e4f101).   
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
  
• The Responsible Party is the Accessibility Facilitators.  
  
• The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** b67dd000-a611-4092-9664-fd6c47bb478e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-08

##### A.5.5.1.7.0.6.1 - Current Remaining Amount

Remaining available one-time budget for the Launch Project:  
  
• 1 USDS  
  
• 0 SKY - The SKY can at most be spent at the rate of 48,000,000 SKY per month.

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** e54a74d0-0cb1-4df3-900c-b7e2f3e4f101
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

# A.6 - The Agent Scope

The Agent Scope regulates all Agents within the Sky Ecosystem and comprises all Agent Artifacts. Each Agent Artifact governs the operations of a particular Agent.

**Document Type:** Scope
**UUID:** 1b3f2ff0-8d73-80e6-86b0-c28bf9a97896
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

## A.6.1 - Agent Artifacts

This Article includes the Artifacts of each Agent.

**Document Type:** Article
**UUID:** 1b4f2ff0-8d73-805a-af66-de296b4aed33
**Last Modified:** 2025-03-13

### A.6.1.1 - List Of Prime Agent Artifacts

The documents herein each set out the unique Artifact for a particular Prime Agent. Prime Agent Artifacts contain all rules, processes, parameters, and information relevant to the Prime Agent. Prime Agent Artifacts are collections of documents that define each Agent’s strategic vision and day-to-day operational logic.

**Document Type:** Section
**UUID:** 1b4f2ff0-8d73-8082-862b-dcd586862638
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

#### A.6.1.1.1 - Spark

The documents herein specify all of the logic for Spark, including Spark’s strategy and how it uses the Sky Primitives to operationalize this strategy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8157-a4b8-f568e2f09fe3
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

##### A.6.1.1.1.1 - Introduction

Spark is an Agent focused on building on USDS in the Ethereum and adjacent DeFi ecosystem. This includes driving adoption of USDS and deploying collateral backing USDS at attractive risk-adjusted returns. Spark does this through the Spark Liquidity Layer, SparkLend, and Spark Savings.  
- The Spark Liquidity Layer directly provides USDS, sUSDS, and USDC liquidity across networks and DeFi markets.  
- SparkLend is a lending market focused on USDS borrowing, sourcing liquidity directly from Sky to provide the best borrow rates for USDS.  
- Spark Savings enables stablecoin holders to earn the best risk-adjusted rate in DeFi, at large scale, with minimal liquidity constraints.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-817a-8226-c2f02043f49a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

##### A.6.1.1.1.2 - Sky Primitives

The documents herein implement the Sky Primitives for Spark. See [A.2.4](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d738095bb8fed052141f936).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81bb-be7f-f9684974185c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.1.1.3.1.1.4.1 - Spark Foundation

**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.1.1.3.1.1.4.2 - Phoenix Labs

Phoenix Labs is a development company that provides services to the Spark Foundation. Phoenix Labs is a “Nested Contributor”, i.e., a core contributor to both Spark and Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-8080-b051-dc4338fca191
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.1.1.3.1.1.4.3 - Spark Assets Foundation

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1 - Parameters

The documents herein define the parameters of the Single Instance of the Agent Token Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8171-ac30-d38ae817f318
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.1 - Token Name

The name of Spark’s token is Spark.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8190-bfa9-d30fc10092a5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.2 - Token Symbol

The symbol of Spark’s token is SPK.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8108-9af4-efa7fdfc8870
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.3 - Genesis Supply

The Genesis Supply of SPK is 10 billion.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81d2-b5de-fe2ab653b894
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.4 - Token Address

The address of SPK on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0xc20059e0317DE91738d13af027DfC4a50781b066`. The address of SPK on Base is `0x24327d9138F9f3fc77BEcB10d9BDc2ABb324EE50`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8123-8009-ea0ec2c4e7d9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.5 - Token Admin

The token Admin is Spark’s SubProxy Account on the Ethereum Mainnet at `0x3300f198988e4C9C63F75dF86De36421f06af8c4`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81a6-b778-ecb3c7fe21b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.6 - Token Emissions

Token emissions beyond the Genesis Supply are permanently disabled; this cannot be reverted by Spark Governance. Sky Governance retains the ability to revert where Spark is in violation of Risk Capital requirements and emissions are required by the Risk Framework. See [A.3.3](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73802fade0e260f04707cc).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8136-a2c8-fdd586893833
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.7 - Custom Instance Parameters

The documents herein define the custom parameters of the Single Instance of the Agent Token Primitive, if any.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-813a-97b6-dcd1c9ae5c11
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.1.7.1 - Staking

SPK token holders can stake their tokens. The rewards for staking will be specified in a future iteration of the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-80f0-9971-ce311b14cd92
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the operational processes for minting and initial distribution of the tokens from the Genesis Supply.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-814c-8311-de7c495739f3
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-19

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2.1 - Minting Of Tokens To SPK Company Ltd

The Genesis Supply was minted to an account owned by SPK Company Ltd. The address of the account on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0x6FE588FDCC6A34207485cc6e47673F59cCEDF92B`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81f4-9896-cf2ae394f051
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2.2 - Transfer Of Tokens To Sky

SPK Company Ltd transferred 6.5 billion SPK tokens from the SPK Company Ltd account to the Sky Pause Proxy. The SPK Company Ltd account is specified in [Minting Of Tokens To SPK Company Ltd](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381f49896cf2ae394f051).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8128-94ff-e38edf299f2d
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2.3 - Transfer Of Tokens To Spark SubProxy Account

SPK Company Ltd transferred 918,760,451 SPK tokens from the SPK Company Ltd account to the Spark SubProxy Account. The SPK Company Ltd account is specified in [Minting Of Tokens To SPK Company Ltd](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381f49896cf2ae394f051).   
  
The Spark SubProxy Account is specified in [SubProxy Account](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381e2a240e4e07ab97739).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-808d-abca-d230b04f232e
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2.4 - Transfer Of Tokens For Token Launch

The SPK Company Ltd account will transfer SPK tokens in connection with the token launch. The SPK Company Ltd account is specified in [Minting Of Tokens To SPK Company Ltd](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381f49896cf2ae394f051).   
  
The amount and nature of these distributions will be specified in a future iteration of the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-805e-af8e-ee39ed489f38
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-19

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.1.4.2.1.2.5 - Transfer Of Tokens To Spark Foundation

The SPK Company Ltd account will transfer all of the SPK tokens held by it, other than the tokens specified in [Transfer Of Tokens For Token Launch](https://www.notion.so/210f2ff08d73805eaf8eee39ed489f38), to the Spark Foundation. The SPK Company Ltd account is specified in [Minting Of Tokens To SPK Company Ltd](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381f49896cf2ae394f051).   
  
The address of the Spark Foundation on the Ethereum Mainnet is `0x92e4629a4510AF5819d7D1601464C233599fF5ec`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8150-ac0d-d6d1d46e1567
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27

######### A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1 - Single Instance Configuration Document

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.1 - Parameters

The parameters of the Root Edit Primitive are fully specified by the Operational Process Definition in [Operational Process Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73815ba935e86f9d8607bd).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8108-982d-f8e8d5a3c920
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Spark Agent Artifact. Information on Spark governance that is unrelated to the use of the Root Edit Primitive is located at [Governance Information Unrelated To Root Edit Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73810c830ccd1577e044c5).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-815b-a935-e86f9d8607bd
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1 - Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Spark Agent Artifact in routine or normal conditions \(i.e., non-emergency situations\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81a9-970e-d1bd300f83f7
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1 - Root Edit Proposal Submission

The Root Edit process begins with a SPK token holder submitting a proposal through the Powerhouse system containing a draft Artifact Edit Proposal. A SPK token holder must hold at least 1% of the total token supply to submit a proposal. The proposal must also be posted on the Sky Forum under the “Spark Prime” category. The proposal must include an upfront review of opportunities and associated risks, outlining expected benefits, potential risks, and corresponding mitigation strategies for consideration by the community.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ec-937a-c3eb8477a3d2
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Root Edit Proposal Submission Requirements Exception For Nested Contributors

Nested Contributors are always authorized to submit Artifact Edit Proposals and do not have to fulfill the token-holding requirements defined in [Root Edit Proposal Submission](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381ec937ac3eb8477a3d2). However, all other procedural requirements within the Root Edit process continue to apply.  
  
To see the Agent’s Nested Contributors, see [Phoenix Labs](https://www.notion.so/210f2ff08d738080b051dc4338fca191).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ad-bc60-cc8686dbc6a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1.2 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures

Until the Powerhouse system supports submitting Artifact Edit Proposals, SPK token holders may submit Artifact Edit Proposals by posting them to the Sky Forum under the “Spark Prime” category. The title of the post must include the text “Spark Artifact Edit Proposal”. The post must include cryptographic proof that the author controls an account holding the required percentage of the total SPK token supply specified in [Root Edit Proposal Submission](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381ec937ac3eb8477a3d2).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-800f-9afe-cc9f32d18606
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.2 - Root Edit Expert Advisor Review

All Spark Root Edit proposals must be reviewed by the Spark Risk Council. The mandate, scope, and review process of the SRC are specified in [Spark Risk Council](https://www.notion.so/272f2ff08d7380ecb6f6f7a58825ac58).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8197-9dd4-ecc38e842f0e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.3 - Root Edit Proposal Review By Operational Facilitator

Within seven \(7\) days of the proposal being submitted, the Operational Facilitator must review the Root Edit Proposal for alignment. The Operational Facilitator also checks if the author of the proposal complies with the requirements in [Root Edit Proposal Submission](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381ec937ac3eb8477a3d2).  
  
If the proposal is aligned, and the author is entitled to submit it, the Operational Facilitator must respond to the Forum post to announce their finding. In their Forum post, the Operational Facilitator must also confirm that the proposal is feasible for Operational GovOps to operationalize.  
  
If the proposal is misaligned, or the author is not entitled to submit it, the Operational Facilitator must respond to the Forum post to announce their finding and provide the reasoning for it.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8183-8bca-c05833f422f8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-18

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.4 - Root Edit Token Holder Vote

Where their review of the proposal results in a finding of alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and Spark Artifact, and where the proposal passes review by the SRC \(defined as “Passed SRC Review” in [SRC Risk Review](https://www.notion.so/272f2ff08d73805daa63dd24240f57f5).\), the Operational Facilitator next triggers a Snapshot poll to allow token holders to vote on the proposal. Token holders may vote directly or through Delegates. See [Delegation Framework](https://www.notion.so/256f2ff08d7380c896ddde4297d57f13). The poll is open for three \(3\) days. A poll must have more than 50% of votes cast, excluding abstentions, in favor to be approved.  
  
Spark’s governance runs in a weekly cycle that begins every Monday. Upon receiving all approvals, the proposal is automatically included in the next cycle. The cut-off time is Friday 8:00 am UTC to ensure the Operational Facilitator has sufficient time to prepare the needed polls for the following Monday. After the cut-off time, it is at the discretion of the Operational Facilitator whether the proposal can be included in the immediate next cycle, or the following cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-811e-9885-d09218023e41
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-19

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.5 - Root Edit Artifact Update

At the conclusion of the poll, if the proposal is approved, the Operational Facilitator submits the edit to Powerhouse to formally update the Agent Artifact. Regardless of the outcome, the Operational Facilitator updates the Powerhouse System to include the result of the vote, including any pertinent documents.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ee-8632-ca3a733aea07
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.5.1 - Short Term Transitionary Measures

Until the Powerhouse system supports updating Agent Artifacts, the Operational Facilitator works with the Governance Facilitators to update the Atlas GitHub repository located at [https://github.com/sky-ecosystem/next-gen-atlas/pulls](https://github.com/sky-ecosystem/next-gen-atlas/pulls) to reflect proposals approved by Prime Governance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-80a0-a077-e924af72b6b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.6 - Artifact Edit Restrictions

The Spark Artifact cannot be edited in any way that violates the Sky Core Atlas or its specifications of the Sky Primitives, or in any way that is otherwise misaligned. The Operational Facilitator must enforce this rule through their review of Artifact Edit Proposals.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-816a-8fb2-f7089fbc00f7
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.6.1 - Time-Limited Root Edit Restrictions On Removal Of Nested Contributors

For a period of three years after June 4, 2025, any Artifact Edit that would have the effect of removing a Nested Contributor must be approved by a vote of SKY holders in addition to a vote of SPK holders to be effective.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81a0-9311-f852e6cfee31
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-12

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.2 - Non-Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Spark Agent Artifact in non-routine conditions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ab-8fb0-f54891eeb182
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.3 - Emergency Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Spark Agent Artifact in urgent or emergency situations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8169-9a75-cf1d74077eb2
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.3.1 - Root Edit Voting Process in Urgent and Emergency Situations

In an Urgent or Emergency Situation, as defined by the Sky Core Atlas in [A.1.8 - Emergency Response System - Emergency Response - Definition Of Emergency Situations](https://www.notion.so/67a49f53fb824e539875434d85a682cd), the Operational Facilitator may allow a Root Edit to occur more quickly than the timeline specified above. Where feasible, the Operational Facilitator should announce the decision to deploy the emergency Root Edit protocol and provide their reasoning via a public Sky Forum post \(under the “Spark Prime” category\), unless doing so would endanger Spark or its users.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81d7-af68-eb99efcf353b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.2.2.2.1.2.4 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures

The parameters specified in [Allocation System Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73810e88dcc9cc19a35945) and [SparkLend](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381c78698d1a2dc550a82) will be controlled by Sky Core Governance until Sky determines that the SPK token is decentralized enough to allow for meaningful governance by tokenholders. At such time, which is currently estimated for September 17, 2025, control will transition to Spark Governance. This transitionary measure ensures better decentralization of the SPK token before Spark Governance takes full control of the named parameters under the Root Edit Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 210f2ff0-8d73-80de-bfe6-cf9fcace2d15
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-27
