######### A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1 - Single Instance Configuration Document

The documents herein contain the Instance Configuration Document for the Single Upkeep Rebate Primitive Instance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8102-a3ac-fe94886604e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.1 - Parameters

Every Prime Agent is entitled to the Upkeep Rebate Primitive for tokens of other Prime Agents that they hold. Because this right automatically applies, there are no parameters.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8129-b3a9-dc4817d124dc
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of the Single Instance of the Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81b0-8cf2-ebc3ece6cd8c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2.1 - Routine Protocol

This document defines the protocol for routine ongoing management of the Single Instance of this Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8110-8397-f03edc540158
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.1 - Spark Holds Tokens Of Other Agents In Its SubProxy Account

Spark keeps all tokens of other Agents it holds in its SubProxy account.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8184-a0b8-d30ae6e580a1
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.2 - Spark Deducts Rebate From Ecosystem Upkeep Fees

When paying Ecosystem Upkeep fees, Spark deducts the rebate from the fees it pays.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81a2-9139-d30ba9363f2a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.3 - Operational GovOps Reviews Rebate

Operational GovOps reviews Spark’s calculation of the rebate before executing a return of surplus to token holders. In the event of any issues, Operational GovOps cannot execute the distribution. If Operational GovOps does not execute the distribution, Operational GovOps must post an explanation on the Sky Forum under the “Spark Prime” category and work with Spark to resolve the disagreement. If Operational GovOps and Spark cannot resolve the disagreement, it must be escalated to Core GovOps.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-810d-9a7e-d97aabfa3308
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.3.3.2.1.2.2 - Non-Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the protocol for non-routine ongoing management of the Single Instance of this Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ff-baa5-ef6db8e8c8c0
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

This Instance inherits the base class of operational logic defined in [A.2.4 - Routine Protocol](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73807089a9c21962a54912), subject to the qualifications specified in [A.2.4 - Near-Term Process](https://www.notion.so/1b3f2ff08d73802292a2ceb9e4827fb9).   
  
Modifications to the base operational logic automatically propagate to this Instance. In future iterations of the Spark Artifact, a version of the full process definition customized to Spark will be included herein.

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.5.1.2.1.2.1.1 - Agent Customizations

The Prime Agent may define instance-specific customization of the routine protocol to extend the baseline functionality defined in the Sky Core Atlas. This can include custom routines or processes layered on top of the inherited Sky Core logic. Any extensions must remain fully aligned with the requirements specified in the Sky Core Atlas. This document defines those customizations, if any.  
  
\[No customization presently.\]

**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81e7-96fe-fab832d76d89

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.5.2.2.1.1.5 - Integration Boost Data Submission Format

The Data Submission Responsible Actor calculates the net deposits based on on-chain events and makes the data available through an API endpoint located at [https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/incentivized-pools/](https://info-sky.blockanalitica.com/api/v1/incentivized-pools/).

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

The Data Submission Responsible Actor is BA Labs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-815a-8b63-cc51030e4d08
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.5.2.2.1.1.7 - Integration Boost Savings Rate Adjustment Strategy

The Integration Boost is calculated based on per block values for USDS in Aave and the Sky Savings Rate.

######### A.6.1.1.1.2.5.3.1.4 - In Progress Invocations Directory

This document contains a Directory of all prospective Instances of the Pioneer Chain Primitive whose Invocation is currently in progress. Invocations that are completed successfully are moved to [Active Instances Directory](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d738134b267f54165b6635b), whereas failed Invocations are Archived in [Hub Data Repository](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73815f888cd7c49bda8e67).

**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8100-9550-e65d6c939960

###### A.6.1.1.1.2.6 - Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives

The documents herein implement the Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives for Spark. See [A.2.4 - A9 - Supply Side Stablecoin Primitives](https://www.notion.so/1b2f2ff08d73801b8e84c86a016fa6cb).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81b8-8990-d6040ffa95c7
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1 - Allocation System Primitive

The documents herein contain all data and specifications for Spark’s Allocation System Primitive Instances. Spark implements the Allocation System Primitive using the Spark Liquidity Layer \(SLL\). Developed prior to the introduction of the Sky Primitives, the SLL serves as the prototype for all Prime Agents’ Allocation Systems.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-810e-88dc-c9cc19a35945
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.1.4 - In Progress Invocations Directory

This document contains a Directory of all prospective Instances of the Allocation System Primitive whose Invocation is currently in progress. ########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2 - Off-Chain Operational Parameters

The documents herein list the off-chain operational parameters for the SLL on each blockchain. These operational parameters are protocol settings managed outside of smart contracts \(off-chain\), used by operators and off-chain systems to guide the functioning of the Spark Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8095-883c-fc681e5ea48b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.1 - Off-chain Operational Parameters For Ethereum Mainnet

The document herein lists the current off-chain operational parameters for the Spark Liquidity Layer on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8079-b160-f9e3a0946f5c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.1.1 - Minimum Operation Size Ethereum Mainnet

The minimum transaction size for operations on Ethereum Mainnet is \(`MAINNET_MIN_OPERATION_SIZE`\):  
◦ 500,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8099-9f79-f36b63fd198f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.1.2 - Debt Ceiling Buffer Ethereum Mainnet

The buffer amount below the maximum debt ceiling is \(`DEBT_CEILING_BUFFER`\):  
◦ 10,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8052-b609-dca8ff2d4672
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.2 - Off-chain Operational Parameters For Base

The document herein lists the current off-chain operational parameters for the Spark Liquidity Layer on Base.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80a6-bbc5-c1630bc95858
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.2.1 - Minimum Operation Size Base

The minimum transaction size for operations on Base is \(`BASE_MIN_OPERATION_SIZE`\):  
◦ 100,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80c6-9123-f432ce6ffa7d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.2.2 - Minimum Maintained Balance Of USDC On Base

The minimum balance of USDC that must be maintained in a Base account within the Spark Liquidity Layer is \(`USDC_MIN_BALANCE_BASE`\):  
◦ 800,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8044-a978-dcb2e87b6cda
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.2.3 - Ideal Balance Of USDC On Base

◦ 800,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80ce-a32e-cae869af7c81
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.2.2.4 - Maximum Balance Of USDC On Base

The maximum balance of USDC that should be maintained in a Base account within the Spark Liquidity Layer is \(`USDC_MAX_BALANCE_BASE`\):  
◦ 800,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8070-af1f-cdc67e2924f5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1 - Ethereum Mainnet

The documents herein list the current `RateLimits` for the Spark Liquidity Layer on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-802b-b4c8-d4a3459bb51f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.1 - USDS Mint Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be minted within the Spark Liquidity Layer \(`LIMIT_USDS_MINT`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDS\): 500,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDS/ day\): 500,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8064-add0-f90d4eea7381
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.2 - USDS Burn Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be burned within the Spark Liquidity Layer \(`LIMIT_USDS_BURN`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDS\): 0  
• `slope` \(USDS/ day\): 0

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8067-b4cd-f7058d8c81a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.3 - USDS For USDC Swap Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be swapped for USDC by the Spark Liquidity Layer in the Mainnet PSM \(`LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): 500,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): 300,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80de-81bd-fc3ae15c64d5
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.4 - USDC Mainnet ALM Proxy Maximum

The maximum amount of USDC that can be sent to the Ethereum Mainnet ALM Proxy \(`LIMIT_USDC_TO_DOMAIN`, hashed with Ethereum domain\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): 4,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): 2,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80fc-ae74-c85e55df2a03
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.4.1 - USDC Mainnet ALM Proxy Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol Maximum

The maximum amount of USDC that can be bridged to Ethereum Mainnet ALM Proxy using the Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol \(`LIMIT_USDC_TO_CCTP_ETH`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): 200,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): 500,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80b1-b4b8-fbed6ef6b595

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2 - Governance Processes

The documents herein describe the specific governance processes for the SLL.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8012-991c-e0d3d15252ce
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-02

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.1 - Invoking New Instances

The governance process to invoke a new Instance of the Allocation System Primitive follows the Root Edit process see [Operational Process Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73815ba935e86f9d8607bd).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8028-86a8-cb6f6c15baa3
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2 - Multisigs

The documents herein define multisigs that have privileged access to manage the SLL.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-80c6-a3ef-ec504317c858
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1 - Prime Relayer Multisig

The Prime Relayer Multisig has the `RELAYER_ROLE` as defined in [Relayer Role](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d738014beb9cd6adf33f8fe) and is controlled by Phoenix Labs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80ad-8c59-d07249890a98
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.1 - Address

The address of the Prime Relayer Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet, Base, and Arbitrum is `0x8a25A24EDE9482C4Fc0738F99611BE58F1c839AB`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-800d-93aa-cfd1d618feb4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.2 - Required Number Of Signers

The Prime Relayer Multisig currently has a 1/2 signing requirement.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-80cf-9c4c-f1ad21070696
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.3 - Signers

The signers of the Prime Relayer Multisig are two \(2\) addresses controlled by Ecosystem Actor Phoenix Labs.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-80d1-8304-f5478054912c
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.4 - Usage Standards

The signers of the Prime Relayer Multisig must use the Multisig to exercise the `RELAYER_ROLE` in accordance with the instructions specified in the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8012-9d89-f9cf008cc9d4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.5 - Modification

Ecosystem Actor Phoenix Labs can change the signers of the Prime Relayer Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least two \(2\) signers and at least a majority of signers are required to execute transactions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8051-803c-d7976e94581b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2 - Core Operator Relayer Multisig

The Core Operator Relayer Multisig has the `RELAYER_ROLE` as defined in [Relayer Role](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d738014beb9cd6adf33f8fe) and is controlled by Amatsu.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-805e-8933-eb8c93eed47e
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2.1 - Address

The address of the Core Operator Relayer Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet, Base, and Arbitrum is `0x8Cc0Cb0cfB6B7e548cfd395B833c05C346534795`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-80bf-923b-d77272a51b35
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2.2 - Required Number Of Signers

The Core Operator Relayer Multisig currently has a 2/3 signing requirement.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-805e-85cf-edb4f9a9ecb2
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2.3 - Signers

The signers of the Core Operator Relayer Multisig are three \(3\) addresses controlled by Ecosystem Actor Amatsu.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8055-a7a6-cbf6b3eec5e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2.4 - Usage Standards

The signers of the Core Operator Relayer Multisig must use the multisig to exercise the `RELAYER_ROLE` in accordance with the instructions specified in the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8021-8e68-f0fde287b39f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2.5 - Modification

Ecosystem Actor Amatsu can change the signers of the Core Operator Relayer Multisig at any time, so long as there are at least three \(3\) signers and at least two thirds of signers are required to execute transactions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-801c-b1a5-c8d522eaa647
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3 - Freezer Multisig

The Freezer Multisig has the `FREEZER_ROLE` as defined in [Freezer Role](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73800dbc16f56852483ead) and is controlled by the Governance Facilitators.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80e2-ade3-e8b90e32a6e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3.1 - Address

The address of the Freezer Multisig on the Ethereum Mainnet, Base, and Arbitrum is `0x90D8c80C028B4C09C0d8dcAab9bbB057F0513431`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8090-aad1-ca339eef98e8
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3.2 - Required Number Of Signers

The Freezer Multisig currently has a 2/4 signing requirement.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-80e6-9936-c3efc85a759f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3.3 - Signers

The Freezer Multisig has the following signers:  
  
◦ VoteWizard  
◦ JanSky  
◦ LDR  
◦ CivicSage

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-800b-9737-f14413c93654
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3.4 - Usage Standards

The signers of the Freezer Multisig should exercise their authority to freeze the Spark Liquidity Layer in the event that Spark is not complying with rules regarding Risk Capital or Asset Liability Management, or in the event of another emergency. The signers should consult with Ecosystem Actor Amatsu before exercising such authority, unless such consultation would cause a delay that could result in a loss of user funds or harm to Sky or Spark. Ecosystem Actor Amatsu may also ask the signers to exercise the Freezer Multisig in an emergency. The signers will work with Amatsu and, if necessary, other Ecosystem Actors, in good faith in determining whether to exercise their authority based on such request.   
  
Each action executed by the Freezer Multisig, including any function calls and their parameters, must be reported to the Sky community within a reasonable time frame through a post on the Sky Forum.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-8037-a1a0-f20c9c4f3592
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-11

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.3.5 - Modification

Modification of the signers of the Freezer Multisig must be approved through a Governance Poll; no Executive Vote is required.  
  
The only exceptions to this are if: 1\) a signer self-reports a loss of access to their private key due to any reason; or 2\) a signer explicitly expresses their wish to be removed as a signer. In both cases, the signer is required to communicate the loss of access to their private key, or the wish to be removed as a signer, in the form of a public Sky Forum post. The specific signer should be replaced as soon as possible, which does not require a Governance Poll.  
  
Any changes to the multisig signers that do not fall within the two exceptions listed above, or that have not been ratified by Sky Governance, should be questioned immediately and treated as malicious. Where malicious activity is suspected, the Governance Facilitators must prepare an expedited Executive Vote so that Sky Governance can vote on removing external security access from the multisig.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1d0f2ff0-8d73-800f-8899-fdfa38d85c0e
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-28

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.2.3 - Token Claim Authorization

Phoenix Labs is authorized to propose the inclusion of transfers of accrued treasury and collector revenues from the Active Instances to the Spark ALM Proxy in a Spark Spell. This request must be posted to the Sky Forum under the Spark Prime category. The Operational Executor Agent must formally approve the inclusion of the transfer in a Spark Spell, with no token holder vote needed.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 280f2ff0-8d73-8040-9e11-d79eb710596b
**Last Modified:** 2025-10-06

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.3 - Total Risk Capital (TRC) Management

The documents herein** **specify requirements related to Spark’s Total Risk Capital \(TRC\) management.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-80bd-aaf6-e383f0a38b58
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.3.1 - Spark’s Operation Of Spark Liquidity Layer And Agreement Regarding Risk Tolerance Ratio

Spark will continue to operate the Spark Liquidity Layer and agrees to stay at or below a 90% Risk Tolerance Ratio. See [A.3.3 - Risk Tolerance Ratio](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73803da696ddd376cbab65).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8012-8929-c2be81a730fa
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.1.3.2 - Spark’s Total Risk Capital (TRC) Management Processes

As operators of the Spark Liquidity Layer, Spark automatically inherits, and is subject to, the base class of operational requirements related to Total Risk Capital management defined in [A.2.4 - Primes’ Total Risk Capital \(TRC\) Management](https://www.notion.so/1fff2ff08d7380feb19edf918dfb8de9). Modifications to the base operational logic automatically propagate to the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8029-922b-dc83f71ce592
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-14

######### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2 - SLL Operational Processes

The documents herein describe common operational procedures for the SLL applicable across multiple Instances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8022-b2a7-dafb54e7320b
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1 - Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the protocol for routine ongoing management of the SLL and its active Instances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d7-b8e0-ed40b6e193c0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.1 - Role Hierarchy And Permissions

The documents herein defines roles \(Admin, Relayer, Freezer\) and their responsibilities/permissions for managing the SLL.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80f7-8bb5-fc05ce8da744
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.1 - Default Admin Role

The admin role \(`DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE`\) is the role that can grant and revoke any role, including itself and all other roles defined in the contract. The admin role is also used for general admin functions in all contracts. This role is fully controlled by Sky Governance via the Spark Proxy.   
  
`constructor(address admin) {  
        _grantRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, admin);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8089-b6a9-d75fa7b32999
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.2 - Relayer Role

The `RELAYER_ROLE` is the address for the Spark Liquidity Layer ALM Planner off-chain system that calls functions on `Controller` contracts to perform actions on behalf of the `ALMProxy` contract. The Relayer Role may be granted to an address by any address holding the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE`. The Relayer Role may be removed from an address by any address holding the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` or the `FREEZER_ROLE`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8014-beb9-cd6adf33f8fe
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.3 - ALM Controller Role

The `ALM_CONTROLLER_ROLE` is the address of the role that can call the `call` functions on the `ALMProxy` contract and update `RateLimits` contract. It includes the `MainnetController` and `ForeignController` contracts. ALM Controller contracts are accessed and modified via the Relayer Role.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-807e-b0e5-fde5001b6f30
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.4 - Freezer Role

The `FREEZER_ROLE` is the address of the emergency role that can remove a compromised Relayer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800d-bc16-f56852483ead
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2 - Controller Functions

The documents herein describe the purpose and operational use of key functions within SLL `MainnetController` and `ForeignController` contracts: USDS management \(mint/burn USDS\), Asset Transfer Management \(direct transfers, protocol deposits/withdrawals\), Cross-chain Operations \(CCTP bridging\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8088-a398-f21549e19f9f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1 - Mainnet Controller Contract Functions

The documents herein define the functions controlled by the Controller contract for Spark Liquidity Layer operations on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80d1-a08e-fd02b90aee71
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1 - Admin Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` within the `MainnetController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8073-97b4-c33493003f4b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Set The Mint Recipient

The documents herein define the process to set the `mintRecipient` for a specific `destinationDomain`. This is used in cross-chain transfers to specify the address that will receive minted tokens on the target chain.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800b-8528-f2d71906a67a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.1 - Admin Role

The operator must ensure they are working as an Admin. Only the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is allowed to `setMintRecipient`.   
  
`function setMintRecipient(uint32 destinationDomain, bytes32 mintRecipient)  
        external   
        onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80cd-8aa1-cf915f997e79
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.2 - Associate Mint Recipient With Domain

The operator must associate the `mintRecipient` with the `destinationDomain` such that any tokens minted on this domain will go to this recipient.  
  
`{  
        mintRecipients[destinationDomain] = mintRecipient;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800c-8146-d6e191067b88
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.3 - Emit Event To Logs

The operator must emit the event to the blockchain logs.  
  
`        emit MintRecipientSet(destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80fa-bea2-d8060f5378c2
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2 - Relayer Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `RELAYER_ROLE` within the `MainnetController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8051-924c-d2d095ef893f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1 - Core Vault Functions

The documents herein define the operations that are performed to maintain the desired level of liquidity and debt balance of the Spark Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8039-a15d-ef365ef5f74b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1 - Mint USDS

The documents herein define the steps for an operator to `mint` USDS from the Sky Allocation Vault to the Spark ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-808c-9bb2-dea9c0b21a72
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `Relayer`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `mintUSDS`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function mintUSDS(uint256 usdsAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-804c-913e-cc0727dfbba7
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `RateLimits` allow for minting the required amount.   
  
` rateLimited(LIMIT_USDS_MINT, usdsAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80ef-a02d-efc256770ebe
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3 - Mint USDS To Buffer

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `mint` USDS into the Buffer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8001-af35-fe7a0fed490d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3.1 - Encode Mint Function Call

The operator must encode the `mint` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the address `vault` from which USDS will be `drawn`, and the `amount` of USDS to `mint`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8033-b7af-fbdfefbb664f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `draw` function of the vault contract.  
  
`    {  
        // Mint USDS into the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(vault),  
            abi.encodeCall(vault.draw, (usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8076-b7f3-ed0acf610c91
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4 - Transfer USDS To ALM Proxy

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `transfer` USDS from the Buffer to the ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80cc-9f78-f01cd3f224a6
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4.1 - Encode Transfer Function

The operator must encode the `transfer` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the `buffer` address USDS will be transferred from, the `proxy` address that will receive USDS \(i.e. ALM Proxy\), and the `amount` of USDS to `transfer`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80d2-91df-ee6dea985945
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `transferFrom` function of the USDS contract.  
  
`        // Transfer USDS from the buffer to the proxy  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.transferFrom, (buffer, address(proxy), usdsAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80a8-8899-d383fd0a5609
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2 - Burn USDS

The documents herein define the steps for an operator to return and then `burn` Spark’s USDS debt in the Sky Allocation Vault.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-809b-a3fb-c475fdef5d96
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `burnUSDS`. They must also ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function burnUSDS(uint256 usdsAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80f6-a1a9-d29c1dab0c2b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `RateLimits` allow for minting the required amount.   
  
`cancelRateLimit(LIMIT_USDS_MINT, usdsAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80ec-a1ba-e45b281d4310
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3 - Transfer USDS To Buffer

The operator must call the `MainnetController` to `transfer` USDS from the ALM Proxy to the Buffer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-803d-94d3-eb92f96efdbb
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3.1 - Encode Transfer Function Call

The operator must encode the `transfer` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the `buffer` address USDS will be transferred to, and the `amount` of USDS to `transfer`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80d7-b263-e39ee17e7fe5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `transfer` function of the USDS contract.  
  
`     {  
        // Transfer USDS from the proxy to the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.transfer, (buffer, usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8006-97be-f341223bdc22
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4 - Burn USDS

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `burn` USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8043-952c-f695537f2b3b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4.1 - Encode Wipe Function Call

The operator must encode the `wipe` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the address `vault` from which USDS will be `burned`, and the `amount` of USDS to `burn`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-807f-a209-f605c663e92e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `wipe` function of the vault contract.  
  
`// Burn USDS from the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(vault),  
            abi.encodeCall(vault.wipe, (usdsAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8069-aff7-f37c1cb244e2
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2 - ERC-4626 Functions

The documents herein define the general SLL operational procedures for interacting with ERC4626-compliant tokenized vaults. ERC4626 is a standard interface for vaults representing shares of an underlying ERC20 token. SLL can integrate with various ERC4626 vaults \(e.g., Fluid Finance\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80ba-ae9e-e7a29a5b2306
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.1 - General Deposit to ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to deposit assets from the ALM Proxy to the ERC-4626 vault to receive yield-bearing shares.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a deposit.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: SLL contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient underlying asset, and the deposit amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant Instance Configuration Document\).  
      
◦ The SLL Operator approves the target ERC4626 vault \(identified by its `token` address in the Instance Configuration Document\) to spend the underlying `asset` from the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator calls the `deposit(uint256 amount, address receiver)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the `amount` of underlying asset and the ALM `proxy` as the receiver of vault shares.  
      
◦ The number of shares received is recorded.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters \(vault address, asset address, rate limits\), and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document \(ICD\) \(e.g., [Instance-specific Operational Processes](https://www.notion.so/1fff2ff08d73802d8a91d54f502f1d01) or other relevant ERC4626 ICDs\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80c7-a892-e330d7e08fc9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.2 - General Withdraw from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to withdraw a yield-earning balance from the ERC-4626 vault to the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a withdrawal.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: SLL contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient shares of the ERC4626 vault token, and the withdrawal amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant ICD\).  
      
◦ SLL calls the `withdraw(uint256 assets, address receiver, address owner)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the `amount` of underlying assets to withdraw, with the ALM `proxy` as both `receiver` \(of assets\) and `owner` \(of shares being burned\).  
      
◦ The number of shares burned is recorded.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters, and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8056-a310-f45870089f9f
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.3 - General Redeem from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to redeem yield-bearing shares from the ERC-4626 vault, receiving the corresponding amount of underlying assets into the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a redemption of shares.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: SLL contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient shares of the ERC4626 vault token.  
      
◦ SLL calls the `redeem(uint256 shares, address receiver, address owner)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the number of `shares` to redeem, with the ALM `proxy` as both `receiver` \(of assets\) and `owner` \(of shares being redeemed\).  
      
◦ The amount of underlying assets received is recorded, and relevant `RateLimits` \(for withdrawal\) are updated.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters, and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800e-aa95-e9df78e9a6e6
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3 - Aave Functions

The documents herein describe the general SLL operational procedures for depositing to and withdrawing from Aave lending pools.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a3-97fd-d9396c741d4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1 - General Deposit to Aave ATokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to deposit to Aave lending pools.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a deposit to an Aave instance.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: SLL contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient underlying asset, and the deposit amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant Aave ICD\).  
      
◦ The SLL Operator identifies the `underlying` asset address and Aave `pool` address from the specific Aave ICD.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator approves the Aave `pool` to spend the `underlying` asset from the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator calls the `supply(address asset, uint256 amount, address onBehalfOf, uint16 referralCode)` function on the Aave `pool`, providing the `underlying` asset address, `amount`, ALM `proxy` address \(as `onBehalfOf`\), and referral code \(typically 0\).  
      
◦ The ALM Proxy receives `aTokens` representing the deposited assets.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters \(aToken address, underlying asset address, pool address, rate limits\), and operational examples, refer to the specific Aave Instance Configuration Document \(ICD\) \(e.g., [Instance-specific Operational Processes](https://www.notion.so/1fff2ff08d73807389b0d70799e0c4e7) or other relevant Aave ICDs\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80cf-947d-c5d939556141
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2 - General Withdraw from Aave ATokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to withdraw from Aave lending pools.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a withdrawal from an Aave instance.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: SLL contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient `aTokens` for the instance, and the withdrawal amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant Aave ICD\).  
      
◦ The SLL Operator identifies the `underlying` asset address and Aave `pool` address from the specific Aave ICD.  
      
◦ The SLL Operator calls the `withdraw(address asset, uint256 amount, address to)` function on the Aave `pool`, providing the `underlying` asset address, `amount` to withdraw, and the ALM `proxy` address \(as `to`\).  
      
◦ The amount of underlying assets withdrawn is recorded, and relevant `RateLimits` are updated.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters, and operational examples, refer to the specific Aave ICD.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80be-a70a-de6ac1b39d9b
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-20

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4 - PSM Functions

The documents herein define the swap operations performed by the Spark Liquidity Layer in the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8011-b099-deb1a878eef7
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1 - Swap USDS to USDC

The documents herein define a series of operations for an operator to `swap` USDS to USDC through the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8010-8d6b-cb2baf5ea208
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `swapUSDSToUSDC`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function swapUSDSToUSDC(uint256 usdcAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80b4-a2cd-d1800dfe9eb9
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure that `RateLimits` allows for swapping the required USDS amount to USDC.   
  
`rateLimited(LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC, usdcAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-809c-a3dd-d9839083f379
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.3 - Convert To 18 Token Format

The operator must convert USDC amounts to an 18 token decimal format using `psmTo18ConversionFactor`.  
  
`{  
        uint256 usdsAmount = usdcAmount * psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-808f-a8b4-f2d4a9639afa
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.4 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure that the ALM Proxy has enough USDS balance to `swap` for the required USDC amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8072-a8a9-cc314ca983d0
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.5 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the `daiUsds` contract to spend the `usdsAmount` on behalf of the `proxy`. `daiUsds` is a contract that facilitates a 1:1 swap between USDS and DAI.   
  
`proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.approve, (address(daiUsds), usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80f7-87e4-c89b36df54dd
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-06

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.6 - Swap USDS To DAI

The operator must swap USDS to DAI. USDS is swapped to DAI in a 1:1 ratio through the `daiUsds` contract and sent back to the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(daiUsds),  
    abi.encodeCall(daiUsds.usdsToDai, (address(proxy), usdsAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80e7-ab5d-dbed3616ba6e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.7 - Approve PSM Spend

The operator must approve the PSM to spend the newly acquired DAI. The approval is needed for the PSM to be able to `swap` DAI for USDC.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(dai),  
    abi.encodeCall(dai.approve, (address(psm), usdsAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800c-b47a-c261884c307b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.1.8 - Swap DAI To USDC

The operator must swap DAI to USDC. DAI is swapped to USDC in the PSM at a 1:1 ratio with no fee, using the `buyGemNoFee` function and return USDC to the `proxy`.  
  
`        proxy.doCall(  
            address(psm),  
            abi.encodeCall(psm.buyGemNoFee, (address(proxy), usdcAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-806a-b300-d1cf9e7e5581
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2 - Swap USDC to USDS

The documents herein define a series of operations for an operator to `swap` USDC to USDS through the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-809a-8275-d839cf968a8e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `swapUSDCToUSDS`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function swapUSDCToUSDS(uint256 usdcAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80a5-ab10-f401cbbe573a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure that `RateLimits` allows for swapping the required USDC amount to USDS.   
  
`cancelRateLimit(LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC, usdcAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80a0-92bf-fcb8ca6ee8b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.3 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure ALM Proxy has enough USDC balance to `swap` for the required USDS amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80be-b8ad-fead8402b920
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.4 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the PSM to spend USDC. The approval is needed for the PSM to be able to execute a `swap` of USDC.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(usdc),  
    abi.encodeCall(usdc.approve, (address(psm), usdcAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80ce-8bf8-d1c390a79a32
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.5 - Calculate Swap Limit

The operator must calculate the `swap` `limit` per transaction. The maximum amount of USDC that can be swapped to DAI in one transaction is calculated based on the DAI balance held by the PSM. `psmTo18ConversionFactor` converts DAI’s 18 token decimals to USDC’s 6 token decimals.  
  
`uint256 limit = dai.balanceOf(address(psm)) / psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8010-8594-f7cd3ce14857
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.6 - Swap USDC To DAI Directly If Possible

The operator must perform a `direct swap` feasibility check and `swap` USDC to DAI, if possible. If the `usdcAmount` is less than or equal to the `limit`, a direct swap should be performed. `_swapUSDCToDAI` is called to execute the swap from `USDC` to `DAI`.  
  
`if (usdcAmount <= limit) {  
    _swapUSDCToDAI(usdcAmount);  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80bb-801e-c3df651f8e8e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.7 - Swap USDC To DAI Iteratively If Needed

If `direct swap` is not possible, the operator must perform an `iterative swap` of USDC to DAI with DAI `refilling`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8082-9f0d-dc31c8860a5c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.8 - Split Into Multiple Swaps If Limit Exceeded

If the `usdcAmount` exceeds the limit, the operator must split the swap into multiple smaller swaps as follows.   
      
1. The operator must refill the PSM with DAI by calling `psm.fill()`.   
      
2. The operator must recalculate the limit to see how much USDC can be swapped after the refill.   
      
3. The operator must swap the maximum possible USDC amount that doesn't exceed the limit.   
      
4. The operator must update `remainingUsdcToSwap` by subtracting the amount just swapped.   
      
5. The operator must repeat the process until the full `usdcAmount` is swapped.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8098-94f9-ffe0a818586d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.9 - Split Into Multiple Swaps If Limit Exceeded

If the PSM can't be filled, the transaction reverts with `DssLitePsm/nothing-to-fill`.  
  
`else {  
    uint256 remainingUsdcToSwap = usdcAmount;  
  
    while (remainingUsdcToSwap > 0) {  
        psm.fill();  
  
        limit = dai.balanceOf(address(psm)) / psmTo18ConversionFactor;  
  
        uint256 swapAmount = remainingUsdcToSwap < limit ? remainingUsdcToSwap : limit;  
  
        _swapUSDCToDAI(swapAmount);  
  
        remainingUsdcToSwap -= swapAmount;  
    }  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-807c-98ed-f459724743cd
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.10 - Convert USDC Amount To DAI Amount

The operator must convert the USDC amount to the DAI amount, accounting for the token decimal difference.  
  
`{  
        uint256 daiAmount = usdcAmount * psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8078-aaaf-d0735476119a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.11 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the `daiUsds` contract to spend the `daiAmount` on behalf of the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(dai),  
    abi.encodeCall(dai.approve, (address(daiUsds), daiAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80ac-a10e-c9627d547eaa
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.4.2.12 - Swap DAI to USDS

The operator must swap DAI to USDS. DAI is swapped to USDS at a 1:1 ratio through the `daiUsds` contract and sent back to the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
            address(daiUsds),  
            abi.encodeCall(daiUsds.daiToUsds, (address(proxy), daiAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8006-9020-d16eec8cd20a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5 - Bridging Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by an operator to bridge liquidity between Ethereum Mainnet and the destination blockchains for the Spark Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8068-9f3c-d93dfa2d767b
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1 - Bridge USDC Using Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol

The documents herein define the process to bridge USDC using the Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-804c-9a80-c5444c3ed5b2
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `transferUSDCToCCTP`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function transferUSDCToCCTP(uint256 usdcAmount, uint32 destinationDomain)  
        external  
        onlyRole(RELAYER)  
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8042-9519-dcebf0cf6624
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the bridging transaction complies with `RateLimits`. The `LIMIT_USDC_TO_CCTP` parameter enforces a rate limit on total USDC transferred via CCTP. The `LIMIT_USDC_TO_DOMAIN` parameter enforces a rate limit on USDC transferred to a specific `destinationDomain`.  
  
`rateLimited(LIMIT_USDC_TO_CCTP, usdcAmount)  
        rateLimited(  
            RateLimitHelpers.makeDomainKey(LIMIT_USDC_TO_DOMAIN, destinationDomain),  
            usdcAmount  
        )`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80c6-868e-f169d338e623
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.3 - Verify Mint Recipient

** **The operator must verify the `mint` recipient. They must check that a mint recipient \(mapping from domain IDs to recipient addresses\) is configured for the `destinationDomain`. If no recipient is configured, the transaction will revert with an error message.  
  
`bytes32 mintRecipient = mintRecipients[destinationDomain];  
require(mintRecipient != 0, "MainnetController/domain-not-configured");`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8083-8599-c64f516d7ecf
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.4 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure the ALM Proxy has enough USDC to cover the amount instructed in the transfer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8039-80a4-cc35fe239c9d
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.5 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the CCTP to spend USDC on behalf of the `proxy`. This action is necessary for the CCTP contract to initiate the cross-chain transfer.   
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(usdc),  
    abi.encodeCall(usdc.approve, (address(cctp), usdcAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-809e-9d56-dd43a13833e6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.6 - Initiate USDC Transfer Through CCTP

The operator must initiate the USDC transfer through CCTP.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8000-8041-d1871242ed1a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.6.1 - Check CCTP Transfer Limit

The operator must check the `transfer limit`. They must retrieve the maximum amount of USDC that can be transferred in a single CCTP message. This limit is fetched from the `localMinter` contract associated with CCTP.   
  
`uint256 burnLimit = cctp.localMinter().burnLimitsPerMessage(address(usdc));`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8015-9de1-e777dec8b5c4
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.6.2 - Initiate Single Transaction If Possible

If a single transaction is possible within the per-message limit, the operator must initiate the CCTP transfer for the entire USDC amount.  
  
` {  
    _initiateCCTPTransfer(usdcAmount, destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-808f-aa6e-f4ed1d4ed927
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.6.3 - Initiate Smaller Transactions If Needed

If `usdcAmount` exceeds the per-message limit, the transfer must be split into multiple smaller batches executing the following loop until the remaining amount is less than or equal to the limit.   
      
1. The operator must transfer the maximum allowed \(`burnLimit`\) using `_initiateCCTPTransfer`.  
      
2. The operator must reduce the remaining `usdcAmount` by the `burnLimit`.  
  
`while (usdcAmount > burnLimit) {  
    _initiateCCTPTransfer(burnLimit, destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
    usdcAmount -= burnLimit;  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-805c-9720-e506b0aabc95
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

################# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.1.6.4 - Send Remaining USDC

The operator must send the remaining USDC amount \(if applicable\). If there is any `usdcAmount` left after the loop, they must send the remaining amount in a single transfer, ensuring the entire amount is transferred, even if it didn't divide evenly by the `burnLimit`.  
  
`if (usdcAmount > 0) {  
    _initiateCCTPTransfer(usdcAmount, destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80cd-a254-dc3bdb1c0fe8
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.5.2 - Bridge USDS / sUSDS Using OP Token Bridge

This document defines the process for an operator to bridge USDS or sUSDS using the OP Token Bridge. This process will be specified in a future iteration of the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-802b-b68a-cb25f7b2194e
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-07

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2 - Foreign Controller Contract Functions

The documents herein define the functions controlled by the Controller contract for Spark Liquidity Layer cross-chain operations on a destination blockchain.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8038-8735-c2f27e103198
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.1 - Admin Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` within the `ForeignController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-807f-8f21-f12d470b7330
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.1.1 - Set The Mint Recipient

The documents herein define the process for an operator to set a `mintRecipient` for a specific `destinationDomain`. This is used in cross-chain transfers to specify the address that will receive minted tokens on the target chain.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-805b-a39d-f024050c68a4
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.1.1.1 - Admin Role

The operator must ensure they are working as an Admin. Only the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is allowed to `setMintRecipient`.   
  
`function setMintRecipient(uint32 destinationDomain, bytes32 mintRecipient)  
        external   
        onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-800c-9919-e9deb2c8d39e
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.1.1.2 - Associate Mint Recipient With Domain

The operator must associate the `mintRecipient` with the `destinationDomain`, meaning that whenever tokens are minted on this domain, they will go to this recipient.  
  
`{  
        mintRecipients[destinationDomain] = mintRecipient;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8085-8de3-d4c46a303965
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.1.1.3 - Emit Event To Logs

The operator must emit the event to the blockchain logs.  
  
`        emit MintRecipientSet(destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8062-9472-fbe35c4d80e3
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2 - Relayer Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `RELAYER_ROLE` within the `ForeignController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-807e-b3bc-d4b0ad7d5bed
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2.1 - ERC-4626 Functions

The documents herein define the operations that are performed to deposit and withdraw liquidity from yield-bearing Integrator vaults.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80bf-b369-d16808f40aed
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2.1.1 - Deposit to ERC-4626 Vault

This document defines the steps to deposit assets from the ALM Proxy to the ERC-4626 vault to receive yield-bearing shares.   
  
The process for depositing to ERC-4626 Tokens on destination blockchain through the `ForeignController` contract is the same as the one for depositing to ERC-4626 Tokens on Ethereum Mainnet through the `MainnetController` contract; see [General Deposit to ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d7380c7a892e330d7e08fc9).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8016-8d69-dbfbfaf912dd
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2.1.2 - Withdraw ERC-4626 Tokens

This document defines the steps to withdraw a yield-earning balance from the ERC-4626 vault to the ALM Proxy.   
  
The process for withdrawing ERC-4626 Tokens on destination blockchain through the `ForeignController` contract is the same as the one for withdrawing ERC-4626 Tokens on Ethereum Mainnet through the `MainnetController` contract, see [General Withdraw from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73804e8f56d0acb2a45d7e).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8077-a68d-f4cf1adad2be
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2.1.3 - Withdraw ERC-4626 Tokens

This document defines the steps to withdraw a yield-earning balance from the ERC-4626 vault to the ALM Proxy.   
  
The process for withdrawing ERC-4626 Tokens on destination blockchain through the `ForeignController` contract is the same as the one for withdrawing ERC-4626 Tokens on Ethereum Mainnet through the `MainnetController` contract, see [General Withdraw from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73804e8f56d0acb2a45d7e).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8082-bab3-c4484b330022
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-30

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.2.2.1.4 - Redeem ERC-4626 Tokens

This document defines the steps for an operator to redeem yield-bearing shares from the ERC-4626 vault, receiving the corresponding amount of underlying assets into the ALM Proxy.  
  
The process for redeeming ERC-4626 Tokens on destination blockchain through the `ForeignController` contract is the same as the one for redeeming ERC-4626 Tokens on Ethereum Mainnet through the `MainnetController` contract, see [General Redeem from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73800eaa95e9df78e9a6e6).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8079-92fb-c8d8e2c20254

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3 - Rate Limit Management

The documents herein define the protocol for querying, setting, and adjusting `RateLimits` for Instances using their `RateLimitID`s. The ratelimits must be maintained in line with Spark’s strategy, market conditions, and security considerations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-804b-ac14-c83ceb3c18ce
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.1 - RateLimits Query

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to query the current `RateLimits` for a specific key:  
  
`Function getRateLimitData(bytes32 key) external override view returns (RateLimitData memory) {  
        return _data[key];  
    }  
  
    function getCurrentRateLimit(bytes32 key) public override view returns (uint256) {  
        RateLimitData memory d = _data[key];  
  
        // Unlimited rate limit case  
        if (d.maxAmount == type(uint256).max) {  
            return type(uint256).max;  
        }  
  
        return _min(  
            d.slope * (block.timestamp - d.lastUpdated) + d.lastAmount,  
            d.maxAmount  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8047-887b-e15ef9fe5377
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.2 - Set RateLimit

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to set the `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function setRateLimitData(  
        bytes32 key,  
        uint256 maxAmount,  
        uint256 slope,  
        uint256 lastAmount,  
        uint256 lastUpdated  
    )  
        public override onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)  
    {  
        require(lastAmount  <= maxAmount,       "RateLimits/invalid-lastAmount");  
        require(lastUpdated <= block.timestamp, "RateLimits/invalid-lastUpdated");  
  
        _data[key] = RateLimitData({  
            maxAmount:   maxAmount,  
            slope:       slope,  
            lastAmount:  lastAmount,  
            lastUpdated: lastUpdated  
        });  
  
        emit RateLimitDataSet(key, maxAmount, slope, lastAmount, lastUpdated);  
    }  
  
    function setRateLimitData(bytes32 key, uint256 maxAmount, uint256 slope) external override {  
        setRateLimitData(key, maxAmount, slope, maxAmount, block.timestamp);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-808c-a506-d1a7c73c2b7a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.3 - Set Unlimited RateLimit

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to set an unlimited `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function setUnlimitedRateLimitData(bytes32 key) external override {  
        setRateLimitData(key, type(uint256).max, 0, type(uint256).max, block.timestamp);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80bd-9ed8-d9f02f98adfd
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.4 - Set Trigger For RateLimit Decrease

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to trigger a decrease of a `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function triggerRateLimitDecrease(bytes32 key, uint256 amountToDecrease)  
        external override onlyRole(CONTROLLER) returns (uint256 newLimit)  
    {  
        RateLimitData storage d = _data[key];  
        uint256 maxAmount = d.maxAmount;  
  
        require(maxAmount > 0, "RateLimits/zero-maxAmount");  
        if (maxAmount == type(uint256).max) return type(uint256).max;  // Special case unlimited  
  
        uint256 currentRateLimit = getCurrentRateLimit(key);  
  
        require(amountToDecrease <= currentRateLimit, "RateLimits/rate-limit-exceeded");  
  
        d.lastAmount = newLimit = currentRateLimit - amountToDecrease;  
        d.lastUpdated = block.timestamp;  
  
        emit RateLimitDecreaseTriggered(key, amountToDecrease, currentRateLimit, newLimit);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-806c-9147-e838f2f9a2b9
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.5 - Set Trigger For RateLimit Increase

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to trigger an increase of a `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function triggerRateLimitIncrease(bytes32 key, uint256 amountToIncrease)  
        external override onlyRole(CONTROLLER) returns (uint256 newLimit)  
    {  
        RateLimitData storage d = _data[key];  
        uint256 maxAmount = d.maxAmount;  
  
        require(maxAmount > 0, "RateLimits/zero-maxAmount");  
        if (maxAmount == type(uint256).max) return type(uint256).max;  // Special case unlimited  
  
        uint256 currentRateLimit = getCurrentRateLimit(key);  
  
        d.lastAmount = newLimit = _min(currentRateLimit + amountToIncrease, maxAmount);  
        d.lastUpdated = block.timestamp;  
  
        emit RateLimitIncreaseTriggered(key, amountToIncrease, currentRateLimit, newLimit);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-80a1-a9ca-cd04b80a41af
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8033-989a-c14bbec487d1

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.3.1 - Remove Compromised Relayer As Freezer

In the event of a compromised Relayer, the `FREEZER_ROLE` can call the function to `removeRelayer` from the Controller contract. This function takes an address, and then the Freezer can remove the compromised Relayer, thereby preventing it from doing any harm to the system. The backstop relayer can then take over. This function should only be used if the keys to the relayer multisig have been leaked or compromised, and the relayer is now in the hands of an external bad actor.   
  
`mainnetController.removeRelayer(compromisedRelayer)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 201f2ff0-8d73-8016-9093-dcee9f410157
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.3.2 - Redeem All L2 Positions

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.2.2.3.2.1 - ERC-4626 Withdrawal Action

In order to withdraw all ERC-4626 balances, the operator must execute the following action:  
  
`foreignController.redeemERC4626(address(token), token.balanceOf(address(proxy)))  
`  
For more detailed instructions on the code to execute this, see [Withdraw ERC-4626 Tokens](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d738077a68df4cf1adad2be) and [Redeem ERC-4626 Tokens](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73807992fbc8d8e2c20254).

`

######## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3 - Active Instances

The Instances of the Spark Liquidity Layer with `Active` Status are stored herein. The `RRC Framework Full Implementation` status defines whether the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a fully implemented risk model \(see [A.3.3 - Fully Implemented Risk Models](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73808baa55c9b4ad359976)\) or a pending risk model \(see [A.3.3 - Pending Risk Models](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d738061a5c3fe76dfd65be3)\). If the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a fully implemented risk model the status is `Covered`. If the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a pending risk model the status is `Pending`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8139-9996-c339030ad494
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3 - Instance-specific Operational Processes

The documents herein contain operational procedures or monitoring requirements unique to this Instance that deviate from or otherwise supplement the general SLL processes. For the general operational procedures applicable to all Aave-type instances. See [Aave Functions](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73802fa111e426fc9b17c8) and [Aave AToken Withdrawal Action](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d738094bb95ca9d373e29c2).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1fff2ff0-8d73-8073-89b0-d70799e0c4e7
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1 - Process Definition For Depositing

The documents herein define the steps to deposit assets from the ALM Proxy to the Aave pool to receive yield-bearing ATokens.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8037-9b1c-cdf93e11ecb9
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `depositAave`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive`  i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function depositAave(address aToken, uint256 amount)  
external  
onlyRole(RELAYER)  
isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80e1-906c-cd4054dda9b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.2 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure ALM Proxy holds enough of the underlying asset to cover the instructed `deposit` amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80ab-8bd6-ddc25c75ba01
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.3 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `deposit` amount is allowed within the `RateLimits`.  
  
        `rateLimited(  
RateLimitHelpers.makeAssetKey(LIMIT_AAVE_DEPOSIT, aToken),  
amount  
)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8064-afbe-d3a28960ea05
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.4 - Initialize ERC-20 Token Interface

The operator must initialize the `underlying` variable as an ERC-20 token interface.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80b9-9142-fc2e3dc45035
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.4.1 - Initialize Interface For Address

The operator must initialize the interface for the `address of the underlying asset` retrieved from the `aToken` contract \(the contract that represents the deposited assets in Aave\). The `IERC20` interface allows interaction with ERC-20 tokens, including performing actions like transferring, approving, and checking balances.  
  
` {  
IERC20    underlying = IERC20(IATokenWithPool(aToken).UNDERLYING_ASSET_ADDRESS());`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80a2-91eb-ed927e8280b0
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.5 - Initialize Pool Variable

The operator must initialize the `pool` variable as an interface for the Aave pool.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8065-a973-c593b2a6fb72
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.5.1 - Retrieve Aave Address

The operator must retrieve the Aave pool contract address associated with the given `aToken`. This address represents the Aave lending pool where the assets are deposited. `IAavePool` interface allows interaction with the Aave pool's functions \(like `supply`\).  
  
  
  
`    IAavePool pool       = IAavePool(IATokenWithPool(aToken).POOL());`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8008-b188-efdebd6bde46
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.6 - Call Approve Function

The operator must call the `approve` function to update the allowance of the `underlying` asset contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8081-9651-c9720b150610
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.6.1 - Encode Function Call

The operator must encode the `approve` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` allowing the Aave `pool` address to spend up to `amount` of the `underlying` token from the ALM Proxy’s balance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8010-89d9-cf69f11559d3
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.6.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` specifying the `address` of the `asset` contract they want to deposit into.  
  
       `// Approve underlying to Aave pool from the proxy (assumes the proxy has enough underlying).  
proxy.doCall(  
address(underlying),  
abi.encodeCall(underlying.approve, (address(pool), amount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80ca-964d-fbcf72d6b735
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.7 - Call Deposit Function

The operator must call the `deposit` function to transfer the `underlying` asset to the Aave lending pool and receive the `aTokens`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8022-a50f-c0a4cc87e72d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.7.1 - Encode Function Call

The operator must encode the `deposit` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the address of the `underlying` token, the `amount` of the underlying asset to `deposit` and the `address(proxy)` that will receive the resulting `aTokens` \(i.e. ALM Proxy\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-803d-b43c-ea22bedd1f36
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.1.7.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `supply` function on Aave \(`pool`\).  
  
        `// Deposit underlying into Aave pool, proxy receives aTokens  
proxy.doCall(  
address(pool),  
abi.encodeCall(pool.supply, (address(underlying), amount, address(proxy), 0))  
);  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8076-9138-ead5c2bcbe56
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############# A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2 - Process Definition For Withdrawing

The documents herein define the steps for an operator to withdraw a yield-earning balance from the Aave AToken vaults to the ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80a1-96c0-e6b0ea8dffc7
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `withdrawAave` tokens. Also, they ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function withdrawAave(address aToken, uint256 amount)  
external  
onlyRole(RELAYER)  
isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80e2-8494-dae8a0dcaa7c
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.2 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure the ALM Proxy holds sufficient `aTokens` to cover the instructed `withdraw` amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-805f-ac8b-f7bff23b63f8
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.3 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `withdraw` amount is allowed within the `RateLimits`.  
  
`// Check withdrawal limits.  
rateLimited(  
RateLimitHelpers.makeAssetKey(LIMIT_AAVE_WITHDRAW, aToken),  
amount  
)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8042-b84f-d864b6eae56b
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.4 - Initialize Pool Variable

The operator must initialize the `pool` variable as an interface for the Aave pool.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80e0-8b26-e0555b17fa55
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.5 - Retrieve Aave Address

The operator must retrieve the Aave pool contract address associated with the given `aToken`. This address represents the Aave lending pool from which the assets are withdrawn.  
  
    `IAavePool pool       = IAavePool(IATokenWithPool(aToken).POOL());`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8012-ba14-d30413394870
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.6 - Call Withdraw Function

The operator must call the `withdraw` function to withdraw a required amount of `underlying` asset from Aave `pool` address and receive the corresponding `aTokens`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80ca-bc1c-f18ef3491383
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.6.1 - Encode Function Call

The operator must encode the `withdraw` function using `abi.encodeCall` with the `underlying asset address` from the `aToken` contract, specifying which token is being withdrawn, the `amount` of the underlying asset to `withdraw`, and the `address(proxy)` of the recipient of the withdrawn assets \(i.e. ALM Proxy\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8095-9ae2-cbbaf3a4a2db
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.6.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `withdraw` function of the Aave `pool` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-80d0-88d8-e2fd9f654b38
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.6.3 - Decode For Underlying Assets

The operator must decode the raw bytes data returned from the `doCall()` function into `uint256` value, representing the amount of underlying assets that were successfully withdrawn from the Aave pool \(`amountWithdrawn`\).  
  
       ` // Withdraw underlying from Aave pool, decode resulting amount withdrawn.  
// Assumes proxy has adequate aTokens.  
amountWithdrawn = abi.decode(  
proxy.doCall(  
address(pool),  
abi.encodeCall(  
pool.withdraw,  
(IATokenWithPool(aToken).UNDERLYING_ASSET_ADDRESS(), amount, address(proxy))  
)  
),  
(uint256)  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-805a-adf7-db323a65a14d
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

############## A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.1.3.2.7 - Decrease RateLimit

The operator must decrease the `RateLimit` based on the assets redeemed.  
  
`rateLimits.triggerRateLimitDecrease(  
            RateLimitHelpers.makeAssetKey(LIMIT_AAVE_WITHDRAW, aToken),  
            amountWithdrawn  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 200f2ff0-8d73-8056-9c09-d905f384b794
**Last Modified:** 2025-09-15

########### A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.2.3 - Ethereum Mainnet - Aave Core USDS Instance Configuration Document

############ A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3 - Instance-specific Operational Processes

The documents herein defines the operations performed to manage the Ethena Instance, including rate limiting, role-based access control, and cooldown functionality.
