######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.1.4 - SparkLend Risk Parameters Kill Switch

The kill switch disables all borrowing across SparkLend markets in the event of a depeg on key collateral assets.   
  
The kill switch is defined in terms of a threshold for specified pegged assets. If the ratio of the price of a specified asset to its peg is equal to or less than the threshold, then any user can trigger the kill switch to disable borrowing across all SparkLend markets.  
  
After the kill switch is triggered, markets can be reactivated by Sky Governance after resetting the kill switch. Resetting the kill switch is subject to the Governance Security Delay specified in [A.1.9 - A2 - Sky Core Governance Security - Governance Security Delay Requirements](https://www.notion.so/9349994e9918494c9e9ae993082ae055).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ad-9988-c4cf1131b7c5
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.1.4.1 - SparkLend Risk Parameters Kill Switch Current Configuration

The kill switch currently covers the following assets with the specified thresholds:  
  
• STETH/ETH - 0.95

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81e0-aa7c-eef111b3366f
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of SparkLend. Future iterations of the Artifact will specify operational processes owned by Spark.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8111-9529-f59698bf2213
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2.1 - SparkLend Risk Parameters Modification

The modification of SparkLend parameters is temporarily controlled by Sky Core, but will be transitioned to Spark in the future. Currently, the Stability Facilitators, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors and Phoenix Labs, may recommend changes to any of the parameters specified in the subdocuments of [SparkLend Risk Parameters Definitions](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d738131a82bc5110c35fd49) or [SparkLend Risk Parameters Cap Automator Parameter Definitions](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381fdbfe2e56d7cf11fb5).  
  
As a general rule, the modification of said parameters is pursuant to the Operational Weekly Cycle and can be effected directly via an Executive Vote, without requiring a Governance Poll.   
  
Changes to the parameters defined in the following documents are exceptions to the general rule and require a Governance Poll followed by an Executive Vote:   
  
• [Liquidation Threshold Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381eabd7efeb73a44923a)   
• [Liquidation Bonus Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381cca9e4d29ba266e7b0)

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-819c-9641-d87ad5b7058b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2.2 - Collateral Onboarding/Offboarding

The onboarding/offboarding of SparkLend collateral is temporarily controlled by Sky Core, but will be transitioned to Spark in the future. Currently, it is implemented by the Stability Facilitators, in consultation with the Stability Scope Advisors and Phoenix Labs, through the Operational Weekly Cycle.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-819a-a0df-db47678d4724
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2.3 - Spark Protocol-Aave Revenue Share

Spark Protocol must pay out 10% of the income it generates from operating the borrowing and lending functionality of the protocol that is based on the Aave codebase. The documents herein define the Spark Protocol-Aave Revenue Share and its associated operational processes.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-812f-a902-cd5083cf60c2
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2.3.1 - Sky Core Governance Responsibility For Virtual Revenue Share Prior to Launch of SPK

Before the launch of Agent tokens, Sky Governance is temporarily responsible for paying out a “virtual revenue share” on behalf of Spark Protocol. It is calculated by taking the total amount of Dai borrowed from Spark Protocol, and then assuming a “virtual income” equivalent to 1% of this supply, and calculating a revenue share of 10% on that basis. The calculations and payments must be done manually by the Support Facilitators at the end of each quarter.   
  
As an example: if, before the launch of Agent tokens, 200 million Dai is borrowed on Spark Protocol, then the virtual income is 1% of 200 million Dai, which gives 2 million Dai; and of that 2 million Dai the virtual revenue share is 200,000 Dai.   
  
This 200,000 Dai must be paid out in incremental payments each quarter directly by Sky Governance from the Sky Surplus Buffer to a smart contract under the control of Aave Governance. If, before the launch of Agent tokens, less than 100 million Dai is borrowed from Spark Protocol by the Sky Protocol, accrual towards the virtual revenue share payments are paused \(unpaid virtual revenue share that already accrued is still paid out at the end of the quarter\), and the counting down of the revenue share duration is paused. The virtual revenue share payments and the counting down of the remaining revenue share duration is resumed when at least 100 million Dai is again borrowed from Spark Protocol by the Sky Protocol.   
  
Once SPK tokens launch, the virtual revenue share system will be discontinued, and the standard rules of the Spark Protocol Aave Revenue Share Ecosystem Agreement shall take effect. See [Standard Agreement Post SPK Launch](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381f68b1ecb3bac92d9b3).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81b4-9f06-d4dfdbfed064
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.1.2.3.2 - Standard Agreement Post SPK Launch

Post SPK launch, the following revenue-share operational process takes effect. The revenue share payment must be calculated manually at the end of each quarter by the Spark and manually paid as Dai to a smart contract under the control of Aave Governance from Spark. The payments must occur for the revenue share duration of two \(2\) years, starting from September 25th, 2023.   
  
If at any point in time after the launch of Agent tokens, Spark Protocol is generating less than 1 million Dai per year in income for Spark Agent, accrual towards the revenue share payments are paused \(unpaid revenue share that already accrued is still paid out at the end of the quarter\), and the counting down of the revenue share duration is paused. The revenue share payments and the counting down of the remaining revenue share duration is resumed when Spark Protocol is generating more than 1 million Dai per year in income again.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81f6-8b1e-cb3bac92d9b3
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

####### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2 - Spark Pre-launch Token Rewards

The subdocuments herein define the parameters and operational processes related to Spark’s pre-launch token rewards program. These rewards will be paid exclusively out of the SPK tokens held by the Spark Foundation.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81f6-ad71-f8427ec91982
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.1 - Parameters

The parameters of the Spark Pre-launch Token Rewards are specified in the subdocuments herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-811c-8cc9-e4449fb34843
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.1.1 - Conditions For The Pre-launch Token Rewards

Spark has a pre-launch token rewards program based on the usage of its lending platform. Users of the platform will receive an airdrop of SPK tokens, depending on how much and how long they have used the platform during the pre-launch token reward period. These rewards are only for users on Ethereum Mainnet.   
  
There are two seasons of the Spark pre-launch token rewards: Season 1 and Season 2.   
  
Season 1 of pre-launch token rewards was active from August 20 2023 and lasted for nine months, ending on May 20 2024. 130,434,783 SPK tokens were allocated in this period.   
  
In Season 2 14,478,261 SPK will be rewarded per month to SparkLend users who qualify for the airdrop.   
  
Season 2 is an additional pre-farming period, which runs until the Spark Agent launches as part of Sky Endgame launch season.   
  
The monthly SPK rewards are allocated as follows:  
◦ 80 % is allocated to users borrowing DAI and/or USDS  
◦ 20 % is allocated to users supplying ETH  
  
The proposed full anti-cheat SPK Airdrop for SparkLend is calculated using the following formula:  
  
`Airdrop = 80% * (DAI Borrows + USDS Borrows - sDAI Supplies * sDAI Liquidation Threshold - sUSDS Supplies * sUSDS Liquidation Threshold) + 20% * (ETH Supplies - ETH Borrows / ETH Liquidation Threshold)`  
  
All supplies and borrows are denominated in USD based on the on-chain oracle price at that block to determine the conversion.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8168-9e38-f3301474557b
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of the Spark Pre-launch Token Rewards.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-810f-a8f3-fc99fb41145a
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.2.1 - Special Pre-launch Token Reward Programs

Spark can activate a new SPK token pre-launch token reward airdrop program to capture other growth opportunities.   
  
The program can last until the moment SPK launches, or a shorter duration. When activated, the exact details of the special pre-launch token reward airdrop program must be specified in [Special Pre-launch Token Reward Program Details](https://www.notion.so/1c3f2ff08d7380a59f29cc06b7c9b3a3).   
  
The SPK tokens for the future Spark Airdrop are allocated between all borrowers based on a formula announced by Spark and specified in the above cited document. The rate of SPK tokens being earned is 7,239,130 SPK per month, distributed on a per block basis proportional to the formula specified in the above cited document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-812d-9052-eaf9fb0e97b7
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.2.1.1 - Special Pre-launch Token Reward Program Details

The special pre-launch token reward airdrop program is defined as Active Data in [Special Pre-launch Token Reward Program Details](https://www.notion.so/1c3f2ff08d7380a59f29cc06b7c9b3a3).   
  
The Active Data is updated as follows:  
- The Responsible Party is Operational GovOps.  
- The Update Process must follow the protocol for ‘Direct Edit’.

**Document Type:** Active Data Controller
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81dd-a308-c3f1e0717e9c
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.1.3.2.2.2.1.1.0.6.1 - Special Pre-launch Token Reward Program Details

Active pre-launch token reward programs established by [Special Pre-launch Token Reward Programs](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73812d9052eaf9fb0e97b7) are:  
  
Aave V3 Main Market sUSDS \(7,239,130 SPK per month, ending with SPK token launch\):  
  
sUSDS supplies on Aave V3 Main Market are eligible to earn 7,239,130 SPK tokens per month. The SPK rewards program will start on the block during which the sUSDS market is onboarded onto the Aave V3 Main Market and lasts until the issuance of the SPK token, based on the following formula:   
  
`sUSDS Supplies - Sum_i(Stablecoin_i Borrow Amount (in USD) / Stablecoin_i Liquidation Threshold)`

**Document Type:** Active Data
**UUID:** 1c3f2ff0-8d73-80a5-9f29-cc06b7c9b3a3
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

#### A.6.1.1.2 - Grove

The documents herein specify all of the logic for Grove, including Grove’s strategy and how it uses the Sky Primitives to operationalize this strategy.

##### A.6.1.1.2.1 - Introduction

Grove is an Agent focused on unlocking the full potential of USDS through higher savings rates and new products and opportunities. Its main focus will be building an institutional-grade credit platform designed to facilitate credit creation and seamlessly move yield in and out of the onchain economy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81ce-8f79-d0b9ae8ec6c4
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.1.1.3.1.1.4.1 - Grove Foundation

The Grove Foundation is the Prime Foundation associated with Grove. Its mandate is to support the development, growth, and adoption of Grove.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8080-82f9-e63ef79abad3
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.1.1.3.1.1.4.2 - Grove Development Company

Grove Development Company is a development company that provides services to the Grove Foundation. Grove Development Company is a “Nested Contributor”, i.e., a core contributor to both Grove and Sky.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8006-9c37-ced995e74d73
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.1.4.2.1.1.2 - Token Symbol

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8108-bebe-f050bd019356
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.1.4.2.1.1.3 - Genesis Supply

The Genesis Supply of GROVE is 10 billion.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8114-b5c8-c226c6d78815
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.1.4.2.1.1.4 - Token Address

**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.1.4.2.1.1.5 - Token Admin

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81f9-a5af-fe8c4c8a9927
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.1.4.2.1.1.6 - Token Emissions

Token emissions beyond the Genesis Supply are permanently disabled; this cannot be reverted by Grove Governance. Sky Governance retains the ability to revert where Grove is in violation of Risk Capital requirements and emissions are required by the Risk Framework. See [A.3.3](https://www.notion.so/201f2ff08d73802fade0e260f04707cc).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81b8-b4ad-e5acccd66352
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######### A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1 - Single Instance Configuration Document

The documents herein contain the Instance Configuration Document for the Single Root Edit Primitive Instance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8172-bc89-cf321c2ca5ba
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.1 - Parameters

The parameters of the Root Edit Primitive are fully specified by the Operational Process Definition in [Operational Process Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73818491afef934a4e5479).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-812f-8c17-ebc5d60d55ef
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Grove Agent Artifact. Information on Grove governance that is unrelated to the use of the Root Edit Primitive is located at [Governance Information Unrelated To Root Edit Primitive](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381c580f9cd17bf728baa).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8184-91af-ef934a4e5479
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1 - Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Grove Agent Artifact in routine or normal conditions \(i.e., non-emergency situations\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-813d-9982-c1ea5386203d
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1 - Root Edit Proposal Submission

The Root Edit process begins with a GROVE token holder submitting a proposal through the Powerhouse system containing a draft Artifact Edit Proposal. A GROVE token holder must hold at least 1% of the total token supply to submit a proposal. The proposal must also be posted on the Sky Forum under the “Grove Prime” category.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8138-a8a4-c7701d6c4aad
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Root Edit Proposal Submission Requirements Exception For Nested Contributors

Nested Contributors are always authorized to submit Artifact Edit Proposals and do not have to fulfill the token-holding requirements defined in [Root Edit Proposal Submission](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d738138a8a4c7701d6c4aad). However, all other procedural requirements within the Root Edit process continue to apply.  
  
To see the Agent’s Nested Contributors, see [Grove Development Company](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d7380069c37ced995e74d73).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81cc-ac6c-efce681866d8
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.1.2 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures

Until the Powerhouse system supports submitting Artifact Edit Proposals, GROVE token holders may submit Artifact Edit Proposals by posting them to the Sky Forum under the “Grove Prime” category. The title of the post must include the text “Grove Artifact Edit Proposal”. The post must include cryptographic proof that the author controls an account holding the required percentage of the total GROVE token supply specified in [Root Edit Proposal Submission](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d738138a8a4c7701d6c4aad).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80b1-b979-f730af4922e9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.2 - Root Edit Expert Advisor Review

A future iteration of the Grove Artifact will specify guidelines for obtaining specialized review of proposals requiring advanced technical or financial analysis.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-816d-b95d-f81242d2c91f
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.3 - Root Edit Proposal Review By Operational Facilitator

Within seven \(7\) days of the proposal being submitted, the Operational Facilitator must review the Root Edit Proposal for alignment.  
  
If the proposal is aligned, the Operational Facilitator must respond to the Forum post to announce their finding. In this Forum post, the Operational Facilitator must also confirm that the proposal is feasible for Operational GovOps to operationalize.  
  
If the proposal is misaligned, the Operational Facilitator must respond to the Forum post to announce their finding and provide the reasoning for it.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-815c-b4a8-d77c79a39bd3
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.4 - Root Edit Token Holder Vote

Where their review of the proposal results in a finding of alignment with the Sky Core Atlas and Grove Artifact, the Operational Facilitator next triggers a Snapshot poll to allow token holders to vote on the proposal. The poll is open for three \(3\) days. A poll must have at least 10% of the circulating token supply participating and must have more than 50% of votes cast, excluding abstentions, in favor to be approved.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81d1-a906-fe3a246ee6db
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-28

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.4.1 - Circulating Supply Definition

For purposes of [Root Edit Token Holder Vote](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381d1a906fe3a246ee6db), the circulating supply of GROVE tokens is equal to the total supply of GROVE tokens minus the sum of:  
      
1. Tokens retained by Sky and not yet distributed as token rewards \(see [A.2.10 - Sky Retained Tokens And Reward Pools](https://www.notion.so/1fef2ff08d738034a229ead169c098bb)\); and  
      
2. Tokens retained in vesting agreements for Grove contributors and not yet distributed; and  
      
3. Tokens in the Grove SubProxy Account \(see [SubProxy Account](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d7381418366c0e84e810f08)\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8071-974d-e16fa3ebe852
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.5 - Root Edit Artifact Update

At the conclusion of the poll, if the proposal is approved, the Operational Facilitator submits the edit to Powerhouse to formally update the Agent Artifact. Regardless of the outcome, the Operational Facilitator updates the Powerhouse System to include the result of the vote, including any pertinent documents.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8147-9227-ca91e3c01734
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.5.1 - Short-Term Transitionary Measures

Until the Powerhouse system supports updating Agent Artifacts, the Operational Facilitator works with the Governance Facilitators to update the Atlas GitHub repository located at [https://github.com/sky-ecosystem/next-gen-atlas/pulls](https://github.com/sky-ecosystem/next-gen-atlas/pulls) to reflect proposals approved by Prime Governance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-805b-84de-f5c081f35808
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.6 - Artifact Edit Restrictions

The Grove Artifact cannot be edited in any way that violates the Sky Core Atlas or its specifications of the Sky Primitives, or in any way that is otherwise misaligned. The Operational Facilitator must enforce this rule through their review of Artifact Edit Proposals.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81fe-8666-eb3e630f74fb
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1.6.1 - Time-Limited Root Edit Restrictions On Removal Of Nested Contributors

For a period of three years after the Genesis Supply emissions of GROVE tokens take place, any Artifact Edit that would have the effect of removing a Nested Contributor must be approved by a vote of SKY holders in addition to a vote of GROVE holders to be effective.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80e6-aaba-df5a3637fbda
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.2 - Non-Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Grove Agent Artifact in non-routine conditions.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81d7-aef9-c5d1627f21f1
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.3 - Emergency Protocol

The documents herein define the process for using the Root Edit Primitive to update the Grove Agent Artifact in urgent or emergency situations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-818a-9503-e2b9bccfd7f6
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.3.1 - Root Edit Voting Process in Urgent and Emergency Situations

In an Urgent or Emergency Situation, as defined by the Sky Core Atlas in [A.1.8 - Emergency Response System - Emergency Response - Definition Of Emergency Situations](https://www.notion.so/67a49f53fb824e539875434d85a682cd), the Operational Facilitator may allow a Root Edit to occur more quickly than the timeline specified above. Where feasible, the Operational Facilitator should announce the decision to deploy the emergency Root Edit protocol and provide their reasoning via a public Sky Forum post \(under the “Grove Prime” category\), unless doing so would endanger Grove or its users.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8115-9ae4-c3518b7c22d0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

**Document Type:** Core

**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

######### A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1 - Single Instance Configuration Document

The documents herein contain the Instance Configuration Document for the Single Upkeep Rebate Primitive Instance.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8167-a2c0-dc8252a76b68
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.1 - Parameters

Every Prime Agent is entitled to the Upkeep Rebate Primitive for tokens of other Prime Agents that they hold. Because this right automatically applies, there are no parameters.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8107-b1cc-ce81dedeaeb3
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.2 - Operational Process Definition

The documents herein define the process for the ongoing management of the Single Instance of the Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81c0-b909-f8b18eec4135
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.2.1 - Routine Protocol

This document defines the protocol for routine ongoing management of the Single Instance of this Upkeep Rebate Primitive.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81b3-968b-e22bae2ab47d
**Last Modified:** 2025-05-29

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.1 - Grove Holds Tokens Of Other Agents In Its SubProxy Account

Grove keeps all tokens of other Agents it holds in its SubProxy account.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8150-b288-cc902c446079
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.2 - Grove Deducts Rebate From Ecosystem Upkeep Fees

When paying Ecosystem Upkeep fees, Grove deducts the rebate from the fees it pays.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81fb-b376-d9007274c4ad
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.3.3.2.1.2.1.3 - Operational GovOps Reviews Rebate

Operational GovOps reviews Grove’s calculation of the rebate before executing a return of surplus to token holders. In the event of any issues, Operational GovOps cannot execute the distribution. If Operational GovOps does not execute the distribution, Operational GovOps must post an explanation on the Sky Forum under the “Grove Prime” category and work with Grove to resolve the disagreement. If Operational GovOps and Grove cannot resolve the disagreement, it must be escalated to Core GovOps.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-81da-9f17-d5da655e2303
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-15

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a1-aac7-fcae7d1cd05b
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80ec-a62c-e7502dacb69d

**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80f3-9993-cd537fa035e5

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3 - RateLimits

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80ec-88a2-f78255a609b9
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1 - Ethereum Mainnet

The documents herein list the current `RateLimits` for the Grove Liquidity Layer on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80db-b8c8-f6e9ab0a19c8
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.1 - USDS Mint Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be minted within the Grove Liquidity Layer \(`LIMIT_USDS_MINT`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDS\): 100,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDS/ day\): 50,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8051-935e-f632da5d6c4d
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.2 - USDS Burn Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be burned within the Grove Liquidity Layer \(`LIMIT_USDS_BURN`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDS\): This parameter will be specified in a future iteration of the Grove Artifact.  
• `slope` \(USDS/ day\): This parameter will be specified in a future iteration of the Grove Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8054-a8f2-c03083750a02
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.3 - USDS For USDC Swap Maximum

The maximum amount of USDS that can be swapped for USDC by the Grove Liquidity Layer in the Mainnet PSM \(`LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): 100,000,000  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): 50,000,000

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-809f-b6ea-f767b2d196a2
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.4 - USDC Mainnet ALM Proxy Maximum

The maximum amount of USDC that can be sent to the Ethereum Mainnet ALM Proxy \(`LIMIT_USDC_TO_DOMAIN`, hashed with Ethereum domain\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): This parameter will be specified in a future iteration of the Grove Artifact.  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): This parameter will be specified in a future iteration of the Grove Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-802e-8155-feb4433b5f0a
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.1.3.1.5 - USDC Mainnet ALM Proxy Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol Maximum

The maximum amount of USDC that can be bridged to Ethereum Mainnet ALM Proxy using the Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol \(`LIMIT_USDC_TO_CCTP_ETH`\) is specified in the document herein.  
• `maxAmount` \(USDC\): max  
• `slope` \(USDC/ day\): 0

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-807f-a9d1-d1806c5bc319
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

The maximum amount of USDC that can be sent to the Avalanche ALM Proxy \(`LIMIT_USDC_TO_DOMAIN`, hashed with Avalanche domain\) is specified in the document herein.

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.2 - Governance Processes

The documents herein describe the specific governance processes for the Grove Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8025-9d6a-fc5bb55c6504
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.2.1 - Invoking New Instances

The governance process to invoke a new Instance of the Allocation System Primitive follows the Root Edit process see [Operational Process Definition](https://www.notion.so/1c1f2ff08d73818491afef934a4e5479).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8020-88c6-d5c719a7b4d7
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.2.2 - Multisigs

The documents herein define multisigs that have privileged access to manage the Grove Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a2-b732-f0ab193d7bb6
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.1 - Relayer Multisig

The Core Operator Relayer Multisig has the `RELAYER_ROLE` as defined in [Relayer Role](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d7380e4b620d436bafd12d7). In a future iteration of the Grove Artifact, additional logic will be added herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8022-887e-e04ebb0a5fb5
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.2.2.2 - Freezer Multisig

The Freezer Multisig has the `FREEZER_ROLE` as defined in [Freezer Role](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d7380bd88fbeb9c954d9adb). In a future iteration of the Grove Artifact, additional logic will be added herein.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-804c-9855-d9beb1d6b6c8
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.3 - Total Risk Capital (TRC) Management

The documents herein specify requirements related to Grove’s Total Risk Capital \(TRC\) management.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-806a-bbce-e0b4cf771495
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.3.1 - Grove Development Company’s Operation Of Grove Liquidity Layer And Agreement Regarding Risk Tolerance Ratio

Grove Development Company will operate the Grove Liquidity Layer and agrees to stay at or below a 90% Risk Tolerance Ratio. See [A.3.3 - Risk Tolerance Ratio](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73803da696ddd376cbab65).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8006-bd51-c59d07acaabc
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.1.3.2 - Grove Development Company’s Total Risk Capital (TRC) Management Processes

As operators of the Grove Liquidity Layer, Grove Development Company automatically inherits, and is subject to, the base class of operational requirements related to Total Risk Capital management defined in [A.2.4 - Primes’ Total Risk Capital \(TRC\) Management](https://www.notion.so/1fff2ff08d7380feb19edf918dfb8de9). Modifications to the base operational logic automatically propagate to the Grove Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d5-9037-c2b4dc87dae6
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2 - Grove Liquidity Layer Operational Processes

The documents herein describe common operational procedures for the Grove Liquidity Layer applicable across multiple Instances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-804a-81e6-ea91b15b0cb3
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1 - Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the protocol for routine ongoing management of the Grove Liquidity Layer and its active Instances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80c4-92cd-e1ca8c7cd0e4
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.1 - Role Hierarchy And Permissions

The documents herein defines roles \(Admin, Relayer, Freezer\) and their responsibilities/permissions for managing the Grove Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8082-8051-e8da19f7fd46
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.1 - Default Admin Role

The admin role \(`DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE`\) is the role that can grant and revoke any role, including itself and all other roles defined in the contract. The admin role is also used for general admin functions in all contracts. This role is fully controlled by Sky Governance via the Grove Proxy.   
  
`constructor(address admin) {  
        _grantRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, admin);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8028-bc83-ea8cea8a571e
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.2 - Relayer Role

The `RELAYER_ROLE` is the address for the Grove Liquidity Layer ALM Planner off-chain system that calls functions on `Controller` contracts to perform actions on behalf of the `ALMProxy` contract. The Relayer Role may be granted to an address by any address holding the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE`. The Relayer Role may be removed from an address by any address holding the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` or the `FREEZER_ROLE`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80e4-b620-d436bafd12d7
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.3 - ALM Controller Role

The `ALM_CONTROLLER_ROLE` is the address of the role that can call the `call` functions on the `ALMProxy` contract and update `RateLimits` contract. It includes the `MainnetController` and `ForeignController` contracts. ALM Controller contracts are accessed and modified via the Relayer Role.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8098-8bc4-f02871bff276
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.1.4 - Freezer Role

The `FREEZER_ROLE` is the address of the emergency role that can remove a compromised Relayer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80bd-88fb-eb9c954d9adb
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2 - Controller Functions

The documents herein describe the purpose and operational use of key functions within the Grove Liquidity Layer `MainnetController` contracts: USDS management \(mint/burn USDS\), Asset Transfer Management \(direct transfers, protocol deposits/withdrawals\), Cross-chain Operations \(CCTP bridging\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8091-9589-e3dab72d5801
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1 - Mainnet Controller Contract Functions

The documents herein define the functions controlled by the Controller contract for Grove Liquidity Layer operations on Ethereum Mainnet.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8078-9258-ebbebd518ddd
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1 - Admin Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` within the `MainnetController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8030-8049-dd583094e9c8
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Set The Mint Recipient

The documents herein define the process to set the `mintRecipient` for a specific `destinationDomain`. This is used in cross-chain transfers to specify the address that will receive minted tokens on the target chain.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80c2-b5fd-f5b9d38932d6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.1 - Admin Role

The operator must ensure they are working as an Admin. Only the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is allowed to `setMintRecipient`.   
  
`function setMintRecipient(uint32 destinationDomain, bytes32 mintRecipient)  
        external   
        onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-801a-9f5f-d7ffb3b7f4fa
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.2 - Associate Mint Recipient With Domain

The operator must associate the `mintRecipient` with the `destinationDomain` such that any tokens minted on this domain will go to this recipient.  
  
  
`{  
        mintRecipients[destinationDomain] = mintRecipient;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80b3-a185-c2ab7bb89e0e
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.1.1.3 - Emit Event To Logs

The operator must emit the event to the blockchain logs.  
  
  
`        emit MintRecipientSet(destinationDomain, mintRecipient);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8008-9fe7-ded234a4c492
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2 - Relayer Functions

The documents herein define the operations performed by the `RELAYER_ROLE` within the `MainnetController` contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8065-835e-ed777baacd68
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1 - Core Vault Functions

The documents herein define the operations that are performed to maintain the desired level of liquidity and debt balance of the Grove Liquidity Layer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8003-bd6b-ee6db5df7c13
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1 - Mint USDS

The documents herein define the steps for an operator to `mint` USDS from the Sky Allocation Vault to the Grove ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8095-98c6-df40b57970aa
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `Relayer`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `mintUSDS`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function mintUSDS(uint256 usdsAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a6-ba05-f9b67b3918a0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `RateLimits` allow for minting the required amount.  
  
` rateLimited(LIMIT_USDS_MINT, usdsAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a5-8cca-dac2fb5c32f5
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3 - Mint USDS To Buffer

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `mint` USDS into the Buffer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80f3-b44b-cab23c041a9e
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3.1 - Encode Mint Function Call

The operator must encode the `mint` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the address `vault` from which USDS will be `drawn`, and the `amount` of USDS to `mint`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8025-b6f9-fe33e8bb88ef
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.3.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `draw` function of the vault contract.  
  
`    {  
        // Mint USDS into the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(vault),  
            abi.encodeCall(vault.draw, (usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8003-833e-d14d6b42fd39
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4 - Transfer USDS To ALM Proxy

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `transfer` USDS from the Buffer to the ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d7-92c6-ffd43dedb2ae
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4.1 - Encode Transfer Function

The operator must encode the `transfer` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the `buffer` address USDS will be transferred from, the `proxy` address that will receive USDS \(i.e. ALM Proxy\), and the `amount` of USDS to `transfer`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8025-b8da-f1bf35c5ea09
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.1.4.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `transferFrom` function of the USDS contract.  
  
`        // Transfer USDS from the buffer to the proxy  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.transferFrom, (buffer, address(proxy), usdsAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8027-bfef-c0532c6e0484
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2 - Burn USDS

The documents herein define the steps for an operator to return and then `burn` Grove’s USDS debt in the Sky Allocation Vault.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d1-814a-e4d4a8456844
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `burnUSDS`. They must also ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function burnUSDS(uint256 usdsAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8035-90fc-e3185047b2c4
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure the `RateLimits` allow for minting the required amount.  
  
`cancelRateLimit(LIMIT_USDS_MINT, usdsAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8006-9e4b-ee9ef5c7a4f3
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3 - Transfer USDS To Buffer

The operator must call the `MainnetController` to `transfer` USDS from the ALM Proxy to the Buffer.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-809b-bbb7-d3d579f9d0f3
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3.1 - Encode Transfer Function Call

The operator must encode the `transfer` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the `buffer` address USDS will be transferred to, and the `amount` of USDS to `transfer`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d3-ba91-da89c1684cd1
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.3.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `transfer` function of the USDS contract.  
  
`     {  
        // Transfer USDS from the proxy to the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.transfer, (buffer, usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-807a-81f4-e51a1af090fe
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4 - Burn USDS

The operator must call the `MainnetController` contract to `burn` USDS.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8084-b02c-cd69fc4aff2f
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4.1 - Encode Wipe Function Call

The operator must encode the `wipe` function call, using `abi.encodeCall` with the address `vault` from which USDS will be `burned`, and the `amount` of USDS to `burn`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8071-bf14-fbe95b50a406
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.1.2.4.2 - Send Encoded Call

The operator must send the encoded call using `proxy.doCall()` to the `wipe` function of the vault contract.  
  
`// Burn USDS from the buffer  
        proxy.doCall(  
            address(vault),  
            abi.encodeCall(vault.wipe, (usdsAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8028-8efc-ead72afab47c
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2 - ERC-4626 Functions

The documents herein define the general Grove Liquidity Layer operational procedures for interacting with ERC4626-compliant tokenized vaults. ERC4626 is a standard interface for vaults representing shares of an underlying ERC20 token. The Grove Liquidity Layer can integrate with various ERC4626 vaults.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8049-ac4b-db44e10d2ead
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.1 - General Deposit to ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to deposit assets from the ALM Proxy to the ERC-4626 vault to receive yield-bearing shares.  
      
◦ The Grove Liquidity Layer Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a deposit.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: Grove Liquidity Layer contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient underlying asset, and the deposit amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant Instance Configuration Document\).  
      
◦ The Grove Liquidity Layer Operator approves the target ERC4626 vault \(identified by its `token` address in the Instance Configuration Document\) to spend the underlying `asset` from the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The Grove Liquidity Layer Operator calls the `deposit(uint256 amount, address receiver)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the `amount` of underlying asset and the ALM `proxy` as the receiver of vault shares.  
      
◦ The number of shares received is recorded.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters \(vault address, asset address, rate limits\), and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document \(ICD\).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8021-82fb-d53025328f5a
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.2 - General Withdraw from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to withdraw a yield-earning balance from the ERC-4626 vault to the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The Grove Liquidity Layer Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a withdrawal.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: Grove Liquidity Layer contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient shares of the ERC4626 vault token, and the withdrawal amount is within instance-specific `RateLimits` \(defined in the relevant ICD\).  
      
◦ Grove Liquidity Layer calls the `withdraw(uint256 assets, address receiver, address owner)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the `amount` of underlying assets to withdraw, with the ALM `proxy` as both `receiver` \(of assets\) and `owner` \(of shares being burned\).  
      
◦ The number of shares burned is recorded.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters, and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-803a-afc1-d2d9b07e1bf5
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.2.3 - General Redeem from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure

This document defines the steps for an operator to redeem yield-bearing shares from the ERC-4626 vault, receiving the corresponding amount of underlying assets into the ALM Proxy.  
      
◦ The Grove Liquidity Layer Operator, acting as `RELAYER`, initiates a redemption of shares.  
      
◦ Pre-conditions are checked: Grove Liquidity Layer contract `isActive`, ALM Proxy has sufficient shares of the ERC4626 vault token.  
      
◦ Grove Liquidity Layer calls the `redeem(uint256 shares, address receiver, address owner)` function on the target ERC4626 vault, specifying the number of `shares` to redeem, with the ALM `proxy` as both `receiver` \(of assets\) and `owner` \(of shares being redeemed\).  
      
◦ The amount of underlying assets received is recorded, and relevant `RateLimits` \(for withdrawal\) are updated.  
      
◦ For detailed call structures, instance-specific parameters, and operational examples, refer to the specific ERC4626 Instance Configuration Document.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80e1-bc8f-f5ab82807db8
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3 - PSM Functions

The documents herein define the swap operations performed by the Grove Liquidity Layer in the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-807d-ba26-d805de3a39f6
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1 - Swap USDS to USDC

The documents herein define a series of operations for an operator to `swap` USDS to USDC through the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8012-83a0-d2727f4b503c
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `swapUSDSToUSDC`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function swapUSDSToUSDC(uint256 usdcAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80ee-9471-e21762f36650
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure that `RateLimits` allows for swapping the required USDS amount to USDC.   
  
`rateLimited(LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC, usdcAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-800b-8b81-cc2c5dddf08b
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.3 - Convert To 18 Token Format

The operator must convert USDC amounts to an 18 token decimal format using `psmTo18ConversionFactor`.  
  
`{  
        uint256 usdsAmount = usdcAmount * psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-807c-bdcb-f5c2440ca075
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.4 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure that the ALM Proxy has enough USDS balance to `swap` for the required USDC amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80e9-8b29-f4c8915ff436
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.5 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the `daiUsds` contract to spend the `usdsAmount` on behalf of the `proxy`. `daiUsds` is a contract that facilitates a 1:1 swap between USDS and DAI.   
  
`proxy.doCall(  
            address(usds),  
            abi.encodeCall(usds.approve, (address(daiUsds), usdsAmount))  
        );`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80a5-b827-ed34d83b4ee6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.6 - Swap USDS To DAI

The operator must swap USDS to DAI. USDS is swapped to DAI in a 1:1 ratio through the `daiUsds` contract and sent back to the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(daiUsds),  
    abi.encodeCall(daiUsds.usdsToDai, (address(proxy), usdsAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-805f-af68-fc8baf9b895d
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.7 - Approve PSM Spend

The operator must approve the PSM to spend the newly acquired DAI. The approval is needed for the PSM to be able to `swap` DAI for USDC.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(dai),  
    abi.encodeCall(dai.approve, (address(psm), usdsAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8040-8a9f-d96012630377
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.1.8 - Swap DAI To USDC

The operator must swap DAI to USDC. DAI is swapped to USDC in the PSM at a 1:1 ratio with no fee, using the `buyGemNoFee` function and return USDC to the `proxy`.  
  
`        proxy.doCall(  
            address(psm),  
            abi.encodeCall(psm.buyGemNoFee, (address(proxy), usdcAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-804f-a807-e6bf96986624
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2 - Swap USDC to USDS

The documents herein define a series of operations for an operator to `swap` USDC to USDS through the PSM.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8050-bc6e-d6d38c30b957
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `swapUSDCToUSDS`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function swapUSDCToUSDS(uint256 usdcAmount)  
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8041-b325-f146f63ffaf9
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.2 - Check RateLimits

The operator must ensure that `RateLimits` allows for swapping the required USDC amount to USDS.  
  
`cancelRateLimit(LIMIT_USDS_TO_USDC, usdcAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8077-8d7c-cda22b3745dc
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.3 - Check ALM Proxy

The operator must ensure ALM Proxy has enough USDC balance to `swap` for the required USDS amount.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80d7-afc3-e5e353429a00
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.4 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the PSM to spend USDC. The approval is needed for the PSM to be able to execute a `swap` of USDC.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(usdc),  
    abi.encodeCall(usdc.approve, (address(psm), usdcAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80c0-8b58-ccb37bb6a75f
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.5 - Calculate Swap Limit

The operator must calculate the `swap` `limit` per transaction. The maximum amount of USDC that can be swapped to DAI in one transaction is calculated based on the DAI balance held by the PSM. `psmTo18ConversionFactor` converts DAI’s 18 token decimals to USDC’s 6 token decimals.  
  
`uint256 limit = dai.balanceOf(address(psm)) / psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8003-9094-f5464990efc6
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.6 - Swap USDC To DAI Directly If Possible

The operator must perform a `direct swap` feasibility check and `swap` USDC to DAI, if possible. If the `usdcAmount` is less than or equal to the `limit`, a direct swap should be performed. `_swapUSDCToDAI` is called to execute the swap from `USDC` to `DAI`.  
  
`if (usdcAmount <= limit) {  
    _swapUSDCToDAI(usdcAmount);  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80f4-b19e-d654d1c36de5
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.7 - Swap USDC To DAI Iteratively If Needed

If `direct swap` is not possible, the operator must perform an `iterative swap` of USDC to DAI with DAI `refilling`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80fd-9a2f-dd7a393a054c
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.8 - Split Into Multiple Swaps If Limit Exceeded

If the `usdcAmount` exceeds the limit, the operator must split the swap into multiple smaller swaps as follows.  
      
1. The operator must refill the PSM with DAI by calling `psm.fill()`.  
      
2. The operator must recalculate the limit to see how much USDC can be swapped after the refill.  
      
3. The operator must swap the maximum possible USDC amount that doesn't exceed the limit.  
      
4. The operator must update `remainingUsdcToSwap` by subtracting the amount just swapped.  
      
5. The operator must repeat the process until the full `usdcAmount` is swapped.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80c5-9661-e24d56581f05
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.9 - Split Into Multiple Swaps If Limit Exceeded

If the PSM can't be filled, the transaction reverts with `DssLitePsm/nothing-to-fill`.  
  
`else {  
    uint256 remainingUsdcToSwap = usdcAmount;  
  
    while (remainingUsdcToSwap > 0) {  
        psm.fill();  
  
        limit = dai.balanceOf(address(psm)) / psmTo18ConversionFactor;  
  
        uint256 swapAmount = remainingUsdcToSwap < limit ? remainingUsdcToSwap : limit;  
  
        _swapUSDCToDAI(swapAmount);  
  
        remainingUsdcToSwap -= swapAmount;  
    }  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80e6-8ba6-fdc0fbb096a0
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.10 - Convert USDC Amount To DAI Amount

The operator must convert the USDC amount to the DAI amount, accounting for the token decimal difference.  
  
`{  
        uint256 daiAmount = usdcAmount * psmTo18ConversionFactor;`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8073-80a4-f7a1af22ae6a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.11 - Approve Contract Spend

The operator must approve the `daiUsds` contract to spend the `daiAmount` on behalf of the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
    address(dai),  
    abi.encodeCall(dai.approve, (address(daiUsds), daiAmount))  
);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-800c-af9a-d593f3b49dca
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

################ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.2.1.2.3.2.12 - Swap DAI to USDS

The operator must swap DAI to USDS. DAI is swapped to USDS at a 1:1 ratio through the `daiUsds` contract and sent back to the `proxy`.  
  
`proxy.doCall(  
            address(daiUsds),  
            abi.encodeCall(daiUsds.daiToUsds, (address(proxy), daiAmount))  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-807d-8e59-d92e00a2ee4a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3 - Rate Limit Management

The documents herein define the protocol for querying, setting, and adjusting `RateLimits` for Instances using their `RateLimitID`s. The ratelimits must be maintained in line with Spark’s strategy, market conditions, and security considerations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8047-bbd2-cc827593a312
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.1 - RateLimits Query

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to query the current `RateLimits` for a specific key:  
  
`Function getRateLimitData(bytes32 key) external override view returns (RateLimitData memory) {  
        return _data[key];  
    }  
  
    function getCurrentRateLimit(bytes32 key) public override view returns (uint256) {  
        RateLimitData memory d = _data[key];  
  
        // Unlimited rate limit case  
        if (d.maxAmount == type(uint256).max) {  
            return type(uint256).max;  
        }  
  
        return _min(  
            d.slope * (block.timestamp - d.lastUpdated) + d.lastAmount,  
            d.maxAmount  
        );  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8040-a031-f64b63e8c942
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.2 - Set RateLimit

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to set the `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function setRateLimitData(  
        bytes32 key,  
        uint256 maxAmount,  
        uint256 slope,  
        uint256 lastAmount,  
        uint256 lastUpdated  
    )  
        public override onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)  
    {  
        require(lastAmount  <= maxAmount,       "RateLimits/invalid-lastAmount");  
        require(lastUpdated <= block.timestamp, "RateLimits/invalid-lastUpdated");  
  
        _data[key] = RateLimitData({  
            maxAmount:   maxAmount,  
            slope:       slope,  
            lastAmount:  lastAmount,  
            lastUpdated: lastUpdated  
        });  
  
        emit RateLimitDataSet(key, maxAmount, slope, lastAmount, lastUpdated);  
    }  
  
    function setRateLimitData(bytes32 key, uint256 maxAmount, uint256 slope) external override {  
        setRateLimitData(key, maxAmount, slope, maxAmount, block.timestamp);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8021-8dda-e82c006d544f
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.3 - Set Unlimited RateLimit

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to set an unlimited `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function setUnlimitedRateLimitData(bytes32 key) external override {  
        setRateLimitData(key, type(uint256).max, 0, type(uint256).max, block.timestamp);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8054-99d0-c2fee6a6f66e
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.4 - Set Trigger For RateLimit Decrease

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to trigger a decrease of a `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function triggerRateLimitDecrease(bytes32 key, uint256 amountToDecrease)  
        external override onlyRole(CONTROLLER) returns (uint256 newLimit)  
    {  
        RateLimitData storage d = _data[key];  
        uint256 maxAmount = d.maxAmount;  
  
        require(maxAmount > 0, "RateLimits/zero-maxAmount");  
        if (maxAmount == type(uint256).max) return type(uint256).max;  // Special case unlimited  
  
        uint256 currentRateLimit = getCurrentRateLimit(key);  
  
        require(amountToDecrease <= currentRateLimit, "RateLimits/rate-limit-exceeded");  
  
        d.lastAmount = newLimit = currentRateLimit - amountToDecrease;  
        d.lastUpdated = block.timestamp;  
  
        emit RateLimitDecreaseTriggered(key, amountToDecrease, currentRateLimit, newLimit);  
    }`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-801d-8ad1-dfc9e4e62c1f
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.1.3.5 - Set Trigger For RateLimit Increase

The following code sets out instructions for the operator to trigger an increase of a `RateLimit` for a specific key:  
  
`function triggerRateLimitIncrease(bytes32 key, uint256 amountToIncrease)  
        external override onlyRole(CONTROLLER) returns (uint256 newLimit)  
    {  
        RateLimitData storage d = _data[key];  
        uint256 maxAmount = d.maxAmount;  
  
        require(maxAmount > 0, "RateLimits/zero-maxAmount");  
        if (maxAmount == type(uint256).max) return type(uint256).max;  // Special case unlimited  
  
        uint256 currentRateLimit = getCurrentRateLimit(key);  
  
        d.lastAmount = newLimit = _min(currentRateLimit + amountToIncrease, maxAmount);  
        d.lastUpdated = block.timestamp;  
  
        emit RateLimitIncreaseTriggered(key, amountToIncrease, currentRateLimit, newLimit);`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-802a-8278-f79b966a9a05
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.2 - Non-Routine Protocol

The documents herein define the process for non-routine ongoing management of the Grove Liquidity Layer and its active Instances.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8078-8a79-dd6ad94e7af7
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3 - Emergency Protocol

The documents herein define all the possible actions that can be taken in case of an emergency within Grove Liquidity Layer operations.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8060-b680-fc64ab555deb
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3.1 - Remove Compromised Relayer As Freezer

In the event of a compromised Relayer, the `FREEZER_ROLE` can call the function to `removeRelayer` from the Controller contract. This function takes an address, and then the Freezer can remove the compromised Relayer, thereby preventing it from doing any harm to the system. The backstop relayer can then take over. This function should only be used if the keys to the relayer multisig have been leaked or compromised, and the relayer is now in the hands of an external bad actor.   
  
`mainnetController.removeRelayer(compromisedRelayer)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-808b-8d15-e827398fde1e
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3.2 - Redeem All Mainnet Positions

The documents herein define the actions that should be performed by an operator if there is a need to recover the liquidity from Mainnet Protocols and centralize it in the Mainnet Grove ALM Proxy.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8091-8786-f894aba7a952
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3.2.1 - ERC-4626 Withdrawal Action

In order to withdraw all ERC-4626 balances, the operator must execute the following action:  
  
`mainnetController.redeemERC4626(address(token), token.balanceOf(address(proxy)))  
`  
For more detailed instructions on the code to execute this, see [General Withdraw from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d73803aafc1d2d9b07e1bf5) and [General Redeem from ERC-4626 Tokens Procedure](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d7380e1bc8ff5ab82807db8).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-805a-b5e2-ca53eab6510a
**Last Modified:** 2025-06-20

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3.3 - USDC to USDS Swap Action

This document defines the action that should be performed by an operator if there is a need to centralize all recovered liquidity in USDS.  
  
`mainnetController.swapUSDCToUSDS(usdc.balanceOf(address(proxy))  
`  
For more detailed instructions on the code to execute this, see [Swap USDC to USDS](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d738050bc6ed6d38c30b957).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-8077-a074-d13121920851
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

########### A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.2.2.3.4 - USDS Burn Action

This document defines the action that should be performed if there is a need to repay and then burn Grove’s USDS debt.  
  
`mainnetController.burnUSDS(usds.balanceOf(address(proxy))  
`  
More detailed instructions on the code to execute this, see [Burn USDS](https://www.notion.so/218f2ff08d7380d1814ae4d4a8456844).

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 218f2ff0-8d73-80ef-a14d-e6c982148ca3
**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

**Last Modified:** 2025-07-04

######## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3 - Active Instances

The Instances of the Grove Liquidity Layer with `Active` Status are stored herein. The `RRC Framework Full Implementation` status defines whether the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a fully implemented risk model \(see [A.3.3 - Fully Implemented Risk Models](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d73808baa55c9b4ad359976)\) or a pending risk model \(see [A.3.3 - Pending Risk Models](https://www.notion.so/200f2ff08d738061a5c3fe76dfd65be3)\). If the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a fully implemented risk model the status is `Covered`. If the Instance Financial RRC is calculated based on a pending risk model the status is `Pending`.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 1c1f2ff0-8d73-8174-92ff-fc421b5718a1
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-27

############ A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3 - Instance-specific Operational Processes

The documents herein defines the operations performed to manage the Ethena Instance, including rate limiting, role-based access control, and cooldown functionality.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80b5-b452-f67ff2c0b3ee
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.1 - Delegated Signers

The documents herein contain the addresses authorized as `delegatedSigners` in the `ethenaMinter` contract. `delegatedSigners` are set up and removed in the `MainnetController` contract by the `Relayer` role.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80ab-be3f-e59bc88cc9b5
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.1.1 - Addresses Of Delegated Signers

`delegatedSigner` addresses  
      
◦ These addesses will be specified in a future iteration of the Spark Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-807b-9f9f-c2cbc9717830
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-13

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.2 - Set A Delegated Signer In The EthenaMinter Contract

The documents herein define the process for an operator to set a delegated signer to the EthenaMinter contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-803c-91f7-c999cb4d2a06
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.2.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `setDelegatedSigner`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
  
  
`function setDelegatedSigner(address delegatedSigner)   
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80cb-b89f-fb9d2b59d9f9
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.2.2 - Encode Function

The operator must use `proxy.doCall()` to forward the call to the `ethenaMinter` contract and call `setDelegatedSigner` function to set the address that will be authorized as a `delegatedSigner`. To call on `ethenaMinter` contract, the function must be encoded using `abi.encodeCall`.  
  
  
  
`{  
    proxy.doCall(  
        address(ethenaMinter),  
        abi.encodeCall(ethenaMinter.setDelegatedSigner, (address(delegatedSigner)))  
    );  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8095-8e9a-e3cbceefa8f5
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.3 - Remove A Delegated Signer In The Ethena Minter Contract

The documents herein define the process for an operator to remove a delegated signer from the Ethena Minter contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-807b-a483-cca62a41f7e0
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.3.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `removeDelegatedSigner`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function removeDelegatedSigner(address delegatedSigner)   
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8044-91f1-ee5a09ba3022
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-13

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.3.2 - Encode Function

The operator must use `proxy.doCall()` to forward the call to the `ethenaMinter` contract and call `removeDelegatedSigner` function to remove the authorization for the `address` to act as a `delegatedSigner`. To call on `ethenaMinter` contract, the function must be encoded using `abi.encodeCall`.  
  
`{  
    proxy.doCall(  
        address(ethenaMinter),  
        abi.encodeCall(ethenaMinter.removeDelegatedSigner, (address(delegatedSigner)))  
    );  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80ea-86ac-e70d97727bc3
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-13

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.4 - Approve Minting of USDe By Ethena Minter Contract

The documents herein define the process for an operator to approve the minting of USDe by the EthenaMinter contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8085-b7d8-e9afdab244ea
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.4.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `prepareUSDeMint`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function prepareUSDeMint(uint256 usdcAmount)   
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8029-94d3-cbb6af101287
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.4.2 - Enforce Rate Limit

The operator must enforce a rate limit on how much USDC can be approved for minting USDe.  
  
`rateLimited(LIMIT_USDE_MINT, usdcAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8087-9725-d44992009d3b
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.4.3 - Encode Function

The operator must use `proxy.doCall()` to send an approval call to the `usdc` contract, allowing the `ethenaMinter` contract to spend up to the specified `amount` of USDC. They must encode the function using `abi.encodeCall`.  
  
` {  
    proxy.doCall(  
        address(usdc),  
        abi.encodeCall(usdc.approve, (address(ethenaMinter), usdcAmount))  
    );  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80dd-8b15-ead3ffb01219
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.5 - Approve Burning of USDe By EthenaMinter Contract

The documents herein define the process for an operator to approve the burning of USDe by the EthenaMinter contract.

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80dc-8337-db32bdd82c51
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.5.1 - Relayer Role

The operator must ensure they are working as a `RELAYER`. Only the `RELAYER` role is allowed to `prepareUSDeBurn`. Also, they must ensure the contract `isActive` i.e. can process the request.  
  
`function prepareUSDeBurn(uint256 usdeAmount)   
        external   
        onlyRole(RELAYER)   
        isActive`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-809f-8704-e288b73f51b4
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.5.2 - Enforce Rate Limit

The operator must enforce a rate limit on how much USDe can be approved for burning.  
  
`rateLimited(LIMIT_USDE_BURN, usdeAmount)`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80a8-9eab-ef43057ebfeb
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.1.3.5.3 - Encode Function

The operator must use `proxy.doCall()` to send an approval call to the `usde` contract, allowing the `ethenaMinter` contract to spend up to the specified `amount` of USDe. They must encode the function using `abi.encodeCall`.  
  
`{  
    proxy.doCall(  
        address(usde),  
        abi.encodeCall(usde.approve, (address(ethenaMinter), usdeAmount))  
    );  
}`

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-8042-85c3-e17681ca21aa
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############# A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.2.2.3 - Rate Limit IDs

The specific `RateLimitID`\(s\) for this conduit’s inflow and outflow will be specified in a future iteration of the Grove Artifact.

**Document Type:** Core

**Document Type:** Core

**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.2.2.4.1 - Inflow Rate Limits

The inflow rate limits are:  
• `maxAmount`: 250,000,000  
• `slope`: 100,000,000 per day

**Document Type:** Core
**UUID:** 24df2ff0-8d73-80c3-9c91-e955723a4b8f
**Last Modified:** 2025-08-12

############## A.6.1.1.2.2.6.1.3.1.4.2.2.4.2 - Outflow Rate Limits
